collection-of-beauty-cristo-en-casa-de-m: A bodegón that folds a domestic kitchen scene around a small framed vignette of Christ admonishing Martha. The young Velázquez sets garlic, eggs and a glistening fish in tight tenebrist focus, signalling the Sevillian taste for moral allegory dressed as still life.
collection-of-beauty-crossing-the-river-: Charon ferries a soul across the underworld river, with paradise and a fiery hell flanking the panel in classic Netherlandish duality. Patinir, credited as the first dedicated landscape painter, expands the mythological subject into a panoramic geography of cliffs, water and atmospheric distance.
collection-of-beauty-crow-and-blossom-by: A solitary crow perches on a flowering branch, the bird's blue-black plumage set against pale petals. Koson's shin-hanga woodblock print refines Edo-period kachō-e bird-and-flower conventions for early-twentieth-century export collectors, prizing economical line and atmospheric ground.
collection-of-beauty-crucifixion-andrea-: The central panel of Mantegna's San Zeno altarpiece predella, showing Christ crucified between the thieves on a rocky knoll. Mantegna deploys archaeological architectural detail and sharp foreshortening, treating the figures with the chiselled severity of Roman relief sculpture.
collection-of-beauty-cult-of-beauty-001: Whistler's full-length portrait of his model and lover Joanna Hiffernan in a white muslin dress, holding a lily. Conceived as a study in tonal harmony rather than narrative, the picture became a touchstone for the Aesthetic movement's pursuit of art for art's sake.
collection-of-beauty-dance-at-bougival: A young couple turns on the floor of a riverside dance hall outside Paris, the woman's pink bonnet glowing against the dappled crowd. Renoir worked from sittings with Suzanne Valadon and Paul Lhote to fix Impressionist plein-air light into a tightly composed figural scene.
collection-of-beauty-danceringreen: A ballet dancer in a green tutu adjusts her costume in the wings, captured from Degas' habitual cropped, off-axis vantage. Pastel hatching layers powdery greens and pinks, a chromatic shorthand the artist refined throughout his Opéra de Paris series.
collection-of-beauty-daniel-in-the-lion-: Daniel kneels at dawn among nine life-sized lions, hands clasped upward as the beasts prowl, yawn and stare. Rubens drew the lions from observation in the menagerie of Archduke Albert and worked them into a Counter-Reformation meditation on faith preserved.
collection-of-beauty-dans-la-prairie-by-: Camille Monet reads in a meadow strewn with poppies and daisies, her parasol propped beside her. Monet's broken brushwork dissolves figure and field into a single screen of high-keyed colour, a Giverny-era exercise in the Impressionist treatment of figures outdoors.
collection-of-beauty-dans-la-foret-de-fo: Figures rest in the dappled clearing of the Forest of Fontainebleau, the wooded preserve south of Paris that drew the Barbizon and early Impressionist painters. Renoir's youthful palette is still rooted in earthy Courbet-like tonality before his shift to brighter spectral colour.
collection-of-beauty-david-brutus: The Roman consul Brutus sits in shadow as lictors return the bodies of his sons, executed for treason on his order. David exhibited the picture at the Salon weeks after the storming of the Bastille, and Revolutionary audiences read its civic stoicism as immediate political instruction.
collection-of-beauty-david-wilkie-chelse: Veterans cluster outside a Chelsea pub as a despatch announcing the victory at Waterloo is read aloud. Commissioned by the Duke of Wellington, Wilkie filled the genre tableau with regimental specificity, prompting crowd-control rails when it was shown at the Royal Academy in 1822.
collection-of-beauty-david-with-the-head: The young David holds out the severed head of Goliath, in which Caravaggio is widely thought to have painted his own features. Made in his fugitive late period and probably sent to Cardinal Scipione Borghese as a plea for pardon, the canvas distils tenebrist drama into a single beam of light.
collection-of-beauty-de-gion-brug-te-hon: The arched Gion bridge at Hondo on Amakusa, a woman in kimono pausing on the span. Hasui's plate from the Selection of Views of Japan series is a shin-hanga woodblock that fuses ukiyo-e composition with Western atmospheric perspective and softly graded blue skies.
collection-of-beauty-de-humani-corporis-: A muscleman écorché stands in a Paduan landscape, layers of musculature peeled back to reveal underlying anatomy. The plate, cut for Vesalius' 1543 treatise, was carved in the Titian workshop circle and revolutionised medical illustration by binding observation to a humanist visual language.

Another of the Fabrica's flayed figures, posed against a continuous Veneto skyline that links the seven plates into a single panorama when arranged in sequence. Vesalius insisted on direct dissection, and the woodcut renders the layered musculature with unprecedented anatomical accuracy.

A deeper écorché stage from the Fabrica, with skin and superficial muscle stripped away to expose the trunk and limbs. Printed in Basel by Oporinus, the plates set a new standard for the integration of artistic skill and dissection-based medical knowledge.

A skeletal study from Vesalius' Fabrica, the bones articulated in a contemplative pose against an architectural ruin. The volume's woodcuts carried Renaissance memento-mori conventions into the empirical project of anatomy.
collection-of-beauty-de-pruimenboomgaard: A celebrated plum tree in the Kameido garden east of Edo, its blossoming branch thrust across the foreground while visitors stroll behind in a red-suffused dusk. Hiroshige's print from One Hundred Famous Views of Edo was later copied in oil by Van Gogh.
collection-of-beauty-degas-a-woman-seate: Madame Valpinçon glances aside while a riotous bouquet dominates the canvas, the figure pushed to the edge in a deliberate inversion of conventional portrait hierarchy. Degas treats the flowers with thick impasto, the sitter with restrained drawing-led brushwork.
collection-of-beauty-delacroix-marc-aure: The dying emperor Marcus Aurelius commends his unworthy son Commodus to a grave circle of Stoic counsellors. Delacroix exhibited the picture at the 1845 Salon, where Baudelaire singled out its sombre violet and ochre palette as the chromatic distillation of Romantic history painting.
collection-of-beauty-delaunay-champdemar: The Eiffel Tower rises in fractured red and ochre planes above the Champ de Mars. Delaunay's Orphic Cubism dismantles the engineered iron icon into prismatic facets, part of his sustained series treating the tower as a modern motif of Parisian light and movement.
collection-of-beauty-delftsedonderslag: A topographical record of the gunpowder explosion that levelled a quarter of Delft on 12 October 1654, killing van der Poel's own daughter. The artist made the catastrophe his recurring subject, painting smouldering rubble, broken roofs and bewildered survivors with documentary specificity.
collection-of-beauty-descente-de-croix-r: A workshop variant of Rubens' Antwerp Cathedral altarpiece, with the pale body of Christ lowered diagonally onto a white shroud held by mourners. The composition's coiling diagonals and contrasted flesh tones became a Counter-Reformation template repeated across Catholic Europe.
collection-of-beauty-destruction-of-levi: God smites the sea-monster Leviathan with a sword, the beast writhing in the deep amid lightning-lit clouds. Doré's wood-engraved illustration appeared in his folio English Bible, blending Romantic sublimity with the dense cross-hatching that defined his commercial print style.
collection-of-beauty-die-kreuzabnahme-ru: A Descent from the Cross after Rubens, transposing the master's Antwerp altarpiece composition into reproductive form. The diagonal sweep of Christ's pallid body lowered onto a white winding-sheet preserves the Baroque dramaturgy of the original.
collection-of-beauty-die-landschaft-mit-: Three trees crown a low rise above a flat Dutch polder, with a thunderstorm rolling in from the left and tiny figures fishing and sketching at the margins. Rembrandt's largest landscape etching combines drypoint and engraving to model an unusually dramatic sky.
collection-of-beauty-don-juan-by-eugene-: Survivors of a foundered ship draw lots for cannibalism in an open boat, an episode adapted from Byron's poem. Delacroix turns the literary Romantic subject into a study of taut, sea-tossed bodies and overcast Channel light in the wake of Géricault's Raft of the Medusa.
collection-of-beauty-don-quijote-illustr: Don Quixote rides out across the Spanish meseta with Sancho Panza behind, an illustration from Doré's monumental 1863 edition of Cervantes. The wood-engraved plates, cut by a battery of Parisian engravers, fixed the public image of the knight-errant for the next century.

A wood-engraved plate from Doré's celebrated illustrated edition of Cervantes' novel, in which the gaunt knight and his squire move through a finely hatched landscape. The attribution to Dürer is a cataloguing slip; the image belongs to Doré's nineteenth-century print cycle.
collection-of-beauty-dong-yuan-rivers-de: A detail from a long handscroll associated with the Southern Tang master, depicting the misted reaches of the Xiao and Xiang rivers in Hunan. Dong Yuan's soft hemp-fibre brushstrokes and graded ink washes established the canonical southern landscape mode followed for centuries.
collection-of-beauty-dore-gustave-la-sai: A Counter-Reformation altar composition showing the Trinity enthroned amid swirling clouds and angels. Rubens deploys his characteristic ruddy flesh tones, golden draperies and dynamic Baroque diagonals to lift the eye toward the heavenly group.
collection-of-beauty-dore-i-had-done-a-h: A wood-engraving from Doré's late illustrated edition of Coleridge's Rime of the Ancient Mariner, showing the sailor clinging to the rigging after slaying the albatross. Dense parallel hatching builds the storm-charged sea and the cramped vertigo of the crow's nest.
collection-of-beauty-dortordordrecht: A luminous view of the Dutch port of Dordrecht with the packet-boat becalmed at sunrise, openly indebted to Cuyp. Turner dissolves shipping, water and sky into a single golden haze that anticipates his later atmospheric experiments.
collection-of-beauty-dragomirov-by-repin: A bust-length state portrait of the Russian general and military theorist, hero of the crossing of the Danube in 1877. Repin works in his vigorous realist manner, building the weathered face and braided uniform from loaded, broken strokes.
collection-of-beauty-duchess-of-polignac: A portrait of Marie Antoinette's intimate favourite, shown in a soft straw hat and informal muslin dress holding a sheet of music. Vigée Le Brun's pearly flesh tones and feathery brushwork epitomise the late ancien régime ideal of natural elegance.
collection-of-beauty-durer-ecureuil: A watercolour and gouache study of two red squirrels rendered with near-zoological precision, each tuft of fur picked out in fine brush. The sheet belongs to Dürer's lifelong investigation of nature, alongside his hare and great piece of turf.
collection-of-beauty-duse-by-repin: A charcoal portrait of the Italian tragedienne Eleonora Duse, capturing the famously expressive actress in a moment of inward concentration. Repin exploits the chalky drag of charcoal on canvas to model her face while leaving the gown in summary, atmospheric strokes.
collection-of-beauty-dziewczyna-w-ramie-: A young woman leans through a painted frame as if stepping out of the picture into the viewer's room, an illusionistic conceit Rembrandt favoured in the 1640s. Warm Dutch chiaroscuro and a few impasto highlights on the hands and lace collar carry the trompe-l'œil.
collection-of-beauty-durer-academie-de-f: A pen study of a standing female nude seen from behind, holding a staff from which a veil falls. Made during Dürer's first Italian journey, the sheet shows him grappling with classical proportion and the contrapposto idealised by his Venetian contemporaries.
collection-of-beauty-durer-agnes-durer-m: An intimate pen drawing inscribed "mein Agnes," showing the artist's wife in domestic dress with downcast eyes. The sheet, preserved at the Albertina in Vienna, is among the earliest surviving spousal portraits in Northern art.
collection-of-beauty-durer-hieronymus-ho: A late portrait of the Nuremberg patrician and councilman, rendered with almost forensic attention to white curling hair and weathered skin. Painted the year before Dürer's Four Apostles, it distils a lifetime of observing individual character.
collection-of-beauty-e-v-pavlov-by-repin: A documentary group composition showing the surgeon Evgeny Pavlov operating in a St Petersburg theatre, witnessed by gowned colleagues. Repin treats the modern clinic as a secular history piece, marshalling sharp tonal contrasts and a tightly bracketed perspective around the patient.
collection-of-beauty-e1924-3-16-pma2018: A meander of the Epte near Giverny, the small river that fed Monet's water-garden. The canvas is built from short, broken touches that knit foliage, bank and reflection into a single shimmering surface, characteristic of his late 1880s plein-air practice.
collection-of-beauty-el-jaleo-singer: A near life-size scene of a flamenco dancer mid-stride before a row of seated musicians, lit by raking footlights that cast huge shadows on the back wall. Sargent worked the picture up from sketches made in Spain, marrying Velázquez's tonal economy to a thoroughly modern sense of theatrical staging.
collection-of-beauty-eakins-thomas-wrest: Two locked grapplers studied with the anatomical exactness Eakins demanded of his Philadelphia students. The earthy palette, low viewpoint and frieze-like composition reflect his admiration for ancient athletic sculpture filtered through photographic study of the male body.
collection-of-beauty-edgar-degas-at-the-: A landau parked at a country racecourse, the Valpinçon family attending to their newborn son under the shade of a parasol. Degas crops the carriage and horses with photographic abruptness, turning a polite outing into a study of asymmetric modern composition.
collection-of-beauty-edgar-degas-the-orc: A view of the Paris Opera pit dominated by the bassoonist Désiré Dihau, with the legs and tutus of dancers cropped above the proscenium. Degas pioneers the abrupt foreground/background split and the off-axis viewpoint that would shape his ballet pictures for the next two decades.
collection-of-beauty-edgar-germain-hilai: Dancers warming up in the rehearsal room, observed from a raked corner viewpoint that pushes the orchestra rail into the foreground. Degas favoured these unguarded moments of preparation over performance, where artificial light, pastel-keyed tones and oblique geometry could be studied at leisure.
collection-of-beauty-edinburgh-ngs-singe: Gertrude, Lady Agnew of Lochnaw lounges in a bergère, her white satin gown and lilac sash dashed in with bravura wet-into-wet brushwork. The portrait's frank gaze and easy elegance made Sargent's reputation when shown at the Royal Academy in 1893.
collection-of-beauty-edmund-blair-leight: The medieval philosopher tutoring his young pupil in a richly furnished Parisian interior, the doomed pair already absorbed in one another. Blair Leighton — not the Frederic Leighton credited here — favoured this kind of fastidiously costumed romantic-historical anecdote.

A family taking refuge in a fortified chamber as conflict rages outside, painted in Blair Leighton's polished late-Victorian historical idiom. Stained-glass light, careful armour detail and a contained narrative tension are the artist's stock in trade.

A young woman leaning over a garden wall to chat with the unseen occupant beyond, a typical genre-romance vignette of late-Victorian England. Blair Leighton sets up the gentle social comedy with crisp drawing and a sunlit, almost photographic clarity of detail.

A pretty girl shopping for trimmings at an Old-English haberdasher's stall, the title quoting a popular street-cry. Blair Leighton's love of period costume drama and finely modelled drapery gives the trivial subject the weight of a small historical tableau.

A medieval lady offering or receiving a token of devotion in a candlelit chapel-like interior. Long known only through a 1908 photogravure, the picture is a classic exercise in Blair Leighton's chivalric romanticism, with meticulously rendered velvets and enamels.

A pair of lovers stealing away by torchlight through a Tudor courtyard, the woman lifting her brocade skirts as her companion checks the way ahead. Blair Leighton wraps the runaway plot in crisp costume detail and stage-managed late-Victorian sentiment.

A lutenist seated at a courtly lady's feet as the last chord fades, an invented scene that nods to Tristan and Iseult and Paolo and Francesca without illustrating either. Blair Leighton uses the lull after music to suggest dangerous feeling held in check.

A noblewoman delivered up as surety in a medieval baron's hall, her isolation underscored by the encircling knights and heavy stonework. The picture is one of Blair Leighton's late chivalric tableaux, painted with the costume-drama precision that kept his Royal Academy audience loyal.
collection-of-beauty-edouard-manet-at-th: Patrons crowded along the marble counter of a Parisian brasserie, observed with the cool detachment Manet brought to modern social life. Sketchy contours, a high horizon and the cropped figures at the edges signal his late, increasingly Impressionist-inflected handling.
collection-of-beauty-edouard-manet-olymp: A reclining nude courtesan meets the viewer's gaze with cool defiance, attended by a Black maid bearing flowers from a client. Manet flattens form with stark lighting and sharp tonal contrasts, scandalising the 1865 Salon by transposing Titian's Venus into contemporary Parisian demi-monde.
collection-of-beauty-edouard-manet-the-p: A young woman sits alone at a café table, an unlit cigarette between her fingers and a brandy-soaked plum before her. Manet captures the suspended boredom of modern Parisian leisure with loose brushwork and a muted palette of pinks, creams and pale lilacs.
collection-of-beauty-edouard-manet-031: On the terrace of a fashionable suburban restaurant, the proprietor's son leans intimately toward a lady lunching alone. Painted en plein air during Manet's late embrace of Impressionist technique, the canvas glows with dappled sunlight and feathery, summery greens.
collection-of-beauty-edouard-manet-boati: A boater in straw hat and crisp whites holds the tiller while his companion lounges in the prow against a wall of saturated blue water. The radically cropped composition and high horizon reveal Manet's debt to Japanese prints and his summer at Argenteuil with Monet.
collection-of-beauty-edvard-munch-1893-t: A skeletal figure clutches its head beneath a blood-red sky on a fjord-side bridge, mouth agape in soundless anguish. Munch combined oil, tempera and pastel on cardboard to externalise an attack of existential dread, anchoring the visual vocabulary of European Expressionism.
collection-of-beauty-edvard-munch-1922-s: Painted from the terrace of Munch's Ekely estate outside Oslo, this nocturne reduces landscape to broad zones of indigo, snow-white and shadow. A revisitation of his 1893 motif, it trades earlier symbolism for a quieter, late-career meditation on solitude under the winter sky.
collection-of-beauty-edvard-munch-bathin: Three nude men stride from the surf in a frieze-like composition, painted on the German island of Warnemünde. Munch celebrated sun, sea and male vigour with vehement verticals of brushwork, part of a triptych on the ages of man now in Helsinki's Ateneum.
collection-of-beauty-edvard-munch-jealou: A bearded man stares from the foreground in green-tinged pallor while behind him a woman in red plucks fruit beside another suitor. Munch returned to the jealousy motif repeatedly, treating it as a Garden-of-Eden parable charged with autobiographical anxiety.
collection-of-beauty-edvard-munch-the-br: The English violinist Eva Mudocci, with whom Munch had a brief affair, gazes from a cloud of dark hair in this lithograph. Drawn directly onto stone in Berlin and printed in soft black, it is among Munch's most tender portraits, sometimes called his Mona Lisa.
collection-of-beauty-edvard-munch-train-: A coastal landscape pierced by the white plume of a passing locomotive, its trail snaking across a russet shoreline. Munch cropped the scene around the rhythm of the smoke itself, turning industrial intrusion into a curling, almost serpentine compositional device.
collection-of-beauty-edvard-munch-two-hu: A man and woman stand side by side on a stony shore, facing the sea but never one another. Munch returned to this image of unbridgeable distance for decades in paintings and woodcuts, here rendered in blunt blocks of beach grey, surf white and brooding water.
collection-of-beauty-edvard-munch-vampir: A red-haired woman bends over a man, her lips at his neck while her hair cascades like flowing blood. Originally titled Love and Pain, the picture was rechristened by Munch's Symbolist circle and became a key motif of his Frieze of Life.
collection-of-beauty-edvard-munch-weepin: A naked woman sits on the edge of a bed, head bowed into her hands, the room dissolving in nervous strokes around her. Munch left the figure raw and unidealised, channelling psychological collapse through agitated brushwork rather than narrative detail.
collection-of-beauty-edward-hopper-night: An anonymous pedestrian crosses an empty street under a sharply raking street lamp, watched from a high, vertiginous viewpoint. The etching, published in Shadowland magazine, sets out the lonely urban geometry Hopper would later expand into his great oil paintings.
collection-of-beauty-el-beso-pinacoteca-: A young couple embrace on a stone staircase, the woman's blue gown and the man's red tights forming the colours of the French and Italian flags. Hayez's most popular canvas became a coded emblem of the Risorgimento and the alliance behind Italian unification.
collection-of-beauty-el-greco-christ-as-: Christ raises his hand in benediction above a crystalline globe, his pale features elongated against a stormy sky. The panel belongs to El Greco's Apostolado series in Toledo, where the saviour heads a cycle of the twelve apostles in the artist's signature steel-grey palette.
collection-of-beauty-el-greco-view-of-to: A spectral storm rolls over the Spanish hill city, with the Alcázar and cathedral picked out against acid-green hills and ink-black clouds. One of the earliest pure landscapes in Western art, El Greco rearranged Toledo's topography for symbolic, almost visionary effect.
collection-of-beauty-el-jardin-del-amor-: Elegant couples flirt and recline in a balustraded garden while putti scatter roses overhead. Painted soon after Rubens's marriage to Helena Fourment, the scene celebrates conjugal pleasure in his late, pearly Flemish manner and now hangs in the Prado.
collection-of-beauty-el-tres-de-mayo-by-: A Spanish patriot in a luminous white shirt flings out his arms before the firing squad of Napoleonic troops on Príncipe Pío hill. Commissioned after the French withdrawal, Goya treats martyrdom with the brutal lighting and crude paint of modern history painting.
collection-of-beauty-el-coloso: A naked giant rises behind a mountain ridge as a panicked crowd and herds flee across the plain below. Long attributed to Goya and tied to the Peninsular War, the picture stages collective terror with the bituminous darkness of his late, troubled imagination.
collection-of-beauty-el-dos-de-mayo-de-1: Madrileños with knives drag Mamluk cavalrymen of the imperial guard from their rearing horses in the Puerta del Sol. Companion to The Third of May, Goya's painting reconstructs the chaos of the popular uprising against Napoleon's occupation in tumbling diagonals.
collection-of-beauty-el-jardin-de-las-de: Bosch's triptych unfolds from the Garden of Eden through a teeming central panel of nude pleasure-seekers to a hellscape of musical instruments turned instruments of torture. Painted for a Habsburg patron, its inventory of fantastical hybrids has resisted definitive iconographic decoding for five centuries.
collection-of-beauty-elisabeth-louise-vi: The young duchesse sits in a relaxed pose, an open book in her lap and her powdered hair tumbling in fashionable disarray. Vigée Le Brun, official portraitist to Marie-Antoinette, favoured this informality of dress as a calculated marker of natural sensibility.

Painted during Vigée Le Brun's émigré years in Saint Petersburg, the Russian countess wears a soft white gown and a velvet wrap. The artist's Russian sitters helped sustain her career through the Revolution and account for many of her finest late portraits.
collection-of-beauty-elisabeth-vigee-leb: Emma Hamilton turns in the studio with a turbaned head and parted lips, posed as the prophetic Sibyl of Cumae. Vigée Le Brun carried the resulting composition through her exile from Naples to Vienna and Russia as a calling card of her Italian period.

The young Irish actress and future Marchioness Wellesley sits with a drift of sash and ribboned hair against a wooded ground. Vigée Le Brun's London exile period produced a string of such portraits in her airy, neoclassical mode of softened drapery and glowing skin.

Vigée Le Brun cradles her daughter Julie in a tender embrace, both turning their bare-armed warmth to the viewer. Exhibited at the 1787 Salon, the canvas adapted antique Madonna types to revolutionary tastes for sentiment and natural maternal feeling.
collection-of-beauty-elizabeth-vigee-leb: Tipu Sultan's ambassador to the court of Louis XVI stands full-length in robes of patterned silk, hand resting on a sabre. Painted in Paris in 1788 on the eve of revolution, the work remained in the artist's possession for fifty years as a record of her cosmopolitan clientele.
collection-of-beauty-enbu-by-hayami-gyos: Black-winged moths spiral upward through tongues of flame, set against a ground of deep ink. Hayami fused traditional nihonga pigments with the decorative geometry of Rinpa, producing an emblem of Taishō-era modernism now held in the Yamatane Museum.
collection-of-beauty-enoshima-in-the-sag: The tidal causeway to the sacred island of Enoshima opens before pilgrims as Mount Fuji rises in the distance. From Hokusai's Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji, the print exploits imported Prussian blue and the bold diagonal of the sandbar to organise its broad sea view.
collection-of-beauty-entree-de-cimetiere: A wrought-iron gate opens onto a hushed graveyard, its crosses and weathered stones receding into mist. Friedrich treats the cemetery as a threshold between visible world and the beyond, his characteristic Romantic meditation on mortality rendered in muted earth tones and silvery atmosphere.
collection-of-beauty-erasmus-quellinus-i: Cupid lies sprawled in slumber, bow and quiver set aside, his pearly flesh modelled in the warm glazes Rubens favoured for putti and infants. The motif of disarmed love invites the viewer to contemplate desire's vulnerability when stilled.

Quellinus, a close collaborator of Rubens in Antwerp, retells Ovid's myth of Jupiter abducting the Phoenician princess in the guise of a white bull. The composition pairs Flemish Baroque drama with the polished classicism Quellinus absorbed during his Roman training.
collection-of-beauty-escola-de-atenas-va: Painted for Pope Julius II's private library in the Stanza della Segnatura, this fresco assembles the philosophers of antiquity beneath a vast coffered vault, with Plato and Aristotle striding through the central arch. Raphael's lucid one-point perspective and idealised figures became a touchstone of High Renaissance harmony.
collection-of-beauty-eugen-de-blaas-the-: Two young Venetians exchange glances across a sunlit doorway, the woman's costume crisp with embroidered linen and a flowered shawl. De Blaas built his career on such genre scenes of working Venice, prized in Edwardian salons for their sparkling finish and gentle theatrics.
collection-of-beauty-eugen-von-blaas-pla: Two women lean together over a parapet in animated chatter, the title's German for 'small talk' framing the scene as anecdote rather than narrative. De Blaas relishes the textile detail, from the patterned shawl to the starched apron, in his typical lustrous, salon-ready finish.
collection-of-beauty-eugen-von-blaas-sha: A cluster of Venetian women pause from their chores to read aloud from a letter, faces tilted in shared attention. De Blaas mines the city's working-class courtyards for these intimate everyday vignettes, polished with the slick academic technique that earned him Belle Epoque popularity.
collection-of-beauty-eugen-von-blaas-the: A young woman spins thread while a smiling suitor leans in, the title borrowed from Mary Howitt's cautionary verse turning the courtship scene into sly allegory. De Blaas modelled such tableaux on Venetian working girls he knew from his studio quarter near the Zattere.
collection-of-beauty-eugen-von-blaas-two: Two figures stand framed by a worn brick wall, one balancing a copper water vessel, the other looking out beneath a lace-edged kerchief. The painting belongs to de Blaas's prolific Venetian street type, where ethnographic observation meets the polish of academic Salon painting.
collection-of-beauty-eugen-von-blaas-bil: A formal portrait of Arthur Graf Berchtold, a Hungarian magnate and member of the Imperial Diet, posed with the upright bearing of a statesman. De Blaas, better known for genre scenes, here demonstrates his society-portrait register with crisp tailoring and a sober palette.
collection-of-beauty-eugen-von-blaas-der: A young woman lifts a vivid red fan to half-shield her smile, the single warm accent ignites the otherwise restrained tonal harmony. De Blaas exploits such props to focus attention and showcase his command of silk, lace, and the answering blush of skin.
collection-of-beauty-eugene-de-blaas-cur: Two young women lean in conspiratorially, one whispering while the other listens with an expression caught between amusement and disbelief. The intimate framing and high finish are typical of de Blaas's Venetian genre painting, in which gossip itself becomes the subject.
collection-of-beauty-eugene-de-blaas-day: A young Venetian girl rests on the lagoon's edge, her gaze drifting beyond the picture plane while sunlight catches her loose hair and starched blouse. De Blaas pairs his trademark textile precision with a softer, more reflective mood than his lively flirtation scenes.
collection-of-beauty-eugene-de-blaas-a-p: A solitary young woman pauses at a stone window, chin propped in hand, eyes turned inward. De Blaas reduces his usual courtyard chatter to a single note of quiet introspection, the polished academic finish lending the everyday subject a hint of portrait gravity.
collection-of-beauty-eugene-delacroix: A self-portrait from the artist's late thirties, the painter facing the viewer with steady, somewhat melancholy eyes and a black coat that dissolves into shadow. Delacroix here adopts the sober, unidealised register of seventeenth-century Dutch self-portraiture rather than Romantic flourish.
collection-of-beauty-eugene-delacroix-a-: A bandit, slumped in a rocky landscape, drinks from a stream as his life ebbs away, the body twisted in a pose that recalls Delacroix's study of Géricault. The picture distils the young Romantic's taste for outsiders, suffering, and Byronic violence into a single charged moment.
collection-of-beauty-eugene-delacroix-co: Delacroix drew on his 1832 North African journey with Count de Mornay's diplomatic mission to evoke the violent equestrian games of the Moroccan court. The clashing horses and flying robes display the saturated, almost vibrating palette he developed after seeing Maghreb light at first hand.
collection-of-beauty-eugene-delacroix-le: A Natchez couple cradles their newborn on the banks of the Mississippi, fugitives from a colonial massacre, after Chateaubriand's 1801 novel 'Atala'. Delacroix turns the literary source into a sombre meditation on indigenous extinction, exhibited at the Salon of 1835 and now in the Metropolitan Museum.
collection-of-beauty-eugene-delacroix-sh: A small craft is overwhelmed by churning surf beneath a leaden sky, figures barely visible amid the spray. The late Delacroix returns again to the shipwreck motif as an emblem of human helplessness before nature, treated with a freer, almost atmospheric brush.
collection-of-beauty-eugene-delacroix-th: Delacroix records the ecstatic Aissaoua brotherhood spilling through the streets of Tangier, a procession he had witnessed during his 1832 Moroccan trip. The swirling crowd and sun-bleached architecture exemplify his lifelong fascination with North African religious spectacle.
collection-of-beauty-evening-snow-at-ter: A lone figure trudges past snow-laden trees and a thatched cottage as dusk settles over the rural outskirts of Tokyo. Hasui's shin-hanga woodblock combines Western perspective and atmosphere with the layered ink and pigment registration of the traditional Japanese print.
collection-of-beauty-faded-laurels-by-ed: An elderly soldier sits among trophies of past campaigns, his laurels withered and his armour relegated to memento. Edmund Blair Leighton, working in late-Victorian academic style, treats the chivalric subject with antiquarian precision and the warm anecdotal sentiment of the high Salon.
collection-of-beauty-famille-de-paysans-: A peasant family pauses around a sparse table in a dim cottage interior, each face turned outwards with quiet directness. The Le Nain brothers' rare empathetic treatment of rural poverty, painted in a sober earthen palette, anticipates the realism of Courbet two centuries later.
collection-of-beauty-femenine-wave: A graceful, curling wave breaks in undulating tendrils against a void, conceived as the gentler counterpart to Hokusai's better-known 'Masculine Wave'. The print belongs to the artist's late mature exploration of water as pure pattern, where ukiyo-e line approaches abstraction.
collection-of-beauty-femmes-d-alger-dans: Three women lounge with a servant in a tiled Algerian harem chamber, surrounded by hookah, slippers and richly patterned textiles. Painted after Delacroix gained rare access to a domestic interior in 1832, the Louvre canvas became a foundational source of the nineteenth-century Orientalist imagination.
collection-of-beauty-fernand-khnopff013: Khnopff portrays his sister Marguerite, a recurring presence in his work, with the cool, hieratic detachment that defines his Symbolist portraiture. The pale palette and flattened space distil her into an emblem of withdrawn, idealised femininity rather than likeness alone.
collection-of-beauty-fernand-le-quesne-l: Two nudes recline together in a calm interior, the title's 'two pearls' likening their luminous flesh to gems. Le Quesne, a Parisian Salon academician, polishes the bodies to porcelain smoothness in keeping with the late nineteenth-century French taste for refined erotic allegory.

Le Quesne pairs two nude figures in an intimate, lamp-lit setting, the title casting their pale skin as twin pearls. Exhibited within the orbit of the 1889 Paris Salon, the picture exemplifies the polished sensuality favoured by mainstream French academic painting at the century's close.
collection-of-beauty-felicien-rops-porno: A blindfolded woman in stockings is led by a pig over a frieze of allegorical figures, while putti tumble overhead. Rops's notorious Symbolist etching, executed on coloured ground, indicts bourgeois hypocrisy by personifying carnal power as a queen guided by base instinct.
collection-of-beauty-five-egrets-descend: Five egrets glide through falling snow toward a quiet shoreline, their white forms dissolving against the pale ground. Koson, the leading kachoga (bird-and-flower) printmaker of the shin-hanga era, captures the species' winter behaviour with delicately graded woodblock impressions.
collection-of-beauty-flowers-in-a-rococo: An exuberant bouquet spills from an ornate Rococo vase, painted with the broken touches and shifting planes that mark Cézanne's early move beyond Impressionism. The composition tilts the vessel forward, pressing pigment-laden blossoms against a darkened ground in dense, sculptural facets.
collection-of-beauty-foggy-morning-at-po: Mist swallows the Norman cliffs above Pourville, where Monet spent the summer of 1882 pursuing the dissolving effects of marine atmosphere. Pale lavenders and silvered greens are knit together in short, parallel strokes, leaving the horizon as little more than a suggestion.
collection-of-beauty-follower-of-rogier-: A close adaptation of Rogier van der Weyden's celebrated Descent from the Cross, with the limp body of Christ lowered amid a tightly grouped frieze of mourners. The sharp Netherlandish drawing, jewel-toned drapery, and shallow gilded space follow the prototype's compressed, sculptural staging.
collection-of-beauty-fox-in-the-snow-cou: A red fox crouches over its prey in a frozen field, painted with the palette knife's broad, material slabs that Courbet championed in his Realist landscapes. The unsentimental subject and the granular, scraped whites of the snow strip the hunt scene of Romantic glamour.
collection-of-beauty-francesco-hayez-oda: A reclining harem figure pauses over a book, her bared shoulders and embroidered cushions rendered with the polished surfaces and erotic restraint Hayez brought to his Orientalist subjects. The smooth modelling and warm half-light reflect his long career as Milan's leading Romantic portraitist.
collection-of-beauty-francesco-hayez-rit: The novelist Alessandro Manzoni, author of I Promessi Sposi, sits in sober dark dress against a neutral ground, fixing the viewer with a measured gaze. Hayez treats the literary patriot of the Risorgimento with the restrained likeness and crisp drawing of the Italian Romantic portrait tradition.
collection-of-beauty-francesco-hayez-sel: Hayez places himself among the painters Giuseppe Molteni and Giovanni Migliara, the sculptor Pelagio Pelagi, and the writer Tommaso Grossi, an intimate Milanese circle gathered around a table. Now in the Museo Poldi Pezzoli, the conversation piece records the cultural milieu that shaped Lombard Romanticism.
collection-of-beauty-francesco-hayez-sus: The biblical Susanna twists modestly at her bath, surprised by the elders whose presence is implied beyond the frame. Hayez handles the polished nude in the National Gallery, London, with a sculptural finish indebted to Canova and the warm tonalities of his Venetian training.
collection-of-beauty-francesco-hayez-the: Inhabitants of the Greek town of Parga gather their dead and their belongings before fleeing the 1819 Ottoman occupation, a contemporary tragedy embraced by Italian liberals. Hayez orchestrates the crowded shoreline as a Romantic tableau of exile, in line with his patriotic history paintings.
collection-of-beauty-francesco-hayez-056: Samson straddles the lion and pries open its jaws, his musculature lit with the academic clarity Hayez absorbed from study after the antique. The Old Testament hero is staged as a single isolated figure, drawing on the sculptural Romanticism that linked the artist to Canova's circle.
collection-of-beauty-francisco-de-goya-s: From the Black Paintings Goya applied directly to the walls of his Quinta del Sordo, the titan Cronus tears into one of his children, eyes blazing in a void. The savage handling, sooty palette, and private setting belong to the bleak, late vocabulary now reassembled in the Prado.
collection-of-beauty-francisco-de-zurbar: A life-size crucified Christ emerges from impenetrable darkness, the body modelled with sculptural tenebrism that fooled at least one early viewer into thinking the work a polychrome carving. Painted for Seville's Dominican monastery of San Pablo, it announced Zurbarán's austere devotional manner.

A bound lamb lies on a grey ledge against a dark ground, an Agnus Dei meditation on Christ's sacrifice rendered with extraordinary fleecy detail. The picture, in the Prado, distils Zurbarán's ascetic Sevillian piety into one of the most economical still lifes of the Spanish Baroque.

Saint Luke, traditionally credited with painting the Virgin's likeness, leans on a palette and gazes up at the crucified Christ, conflating the evangelist with the artist's own self-portrait. The sober brown ground and clipped lighting are characteristic of Zurbarán's monastic Sevillian commissions.
collection-of-beauty-frans-snijders-keuk: A loaded kitchen table heaves with game, fruit and copper vessels in the lavish Antwerp manner that Snyders developed alongside Rubens. Strong diagonals and life-size scale push the still life toward decorative grandeur, intended for the dining halls of patrician collectors.
collection-of-beauty-frans-snyders-a-gre: A lean greyhound seizes a piglet boar in mid-flight across a wooded clearing, captured with the anatomical precision Snyders brought to Rubens's hunt commissions. Slashing brushwork in the foliage and a smoky sky heighten the violent Baroque drama.
collection-of-beauty-frans-snyders-conce: An owl serves as conductor while a riot of songbirds reads from a sheet of music, a witty allegorical conceit Snyders treated repeatedly. The tightly observed plumage of finches, parrots and birds of prey reflects his standing as Antwerp's premier animal specialist.
collection-of-beauty-frans-snyders-cook-: A market cook arranges deer, swans and waterfowl across a generous table while a cat and dog skirmish below, staging abundance as theatre. The display piece, scaled for a Flemish patrician dining room, is typical of Snyders's collaboration with Rubens's circle.
collection-of-beauty-frans-snyders-lying: A lioness rests in profile against a darkened landscape, her tawny coat brushed with the loose, confident strokes Snyders reserved for living animals. The study likely served as a model for the great Antwerp hunt pieces produced with Rubens's workshop.
collection-of-beauty-frans-snyders-orphe: Orpheus charms a menagerie of beasts and birds with his lyre, the Ovidian subject becoming a pretext for Snyders's encyclopaedic display of fur, feather and scale. The composition crowds exotic species into a forested clearing in the Flemish Baroque manner.
collection-of-beauty-frans-snyders-and-w: Hounds tear at a cornered boar in a churning Baroque mêlée, the kind of large hunting piece Snyders supplied with workshop assistance for aristocratic galleries. Sweeping diagonals, splayed limbs and a bruised sky echo the dynamic compositions he had honed beside Rubens.
collection-of-beauty-franz-von-stuck-the: Two nude warriors lock in combat over a sprawling female nude, a Symbolist allegory of erotic rivalry typical of von Stuck's Munich Secession output. Hard contour, bronzed flesh and a smouldering ground push the mythic theme toward late nineteenth-century decadence.
collection-of-beauty-franz-von-stuck-by-: A late self-portrait on board, signed and dated 1923, presents the Munich Secession founder in dark dress against a neutral ground. The clipped scale and sober finish reflect von Stuck's habit of treating his own image as part of his public persona as artist-prince.
collection-of-beauty-franz-von-stuck-wou: A bare-breasted Amazon staggers from the field clutching her side, her body silhouetted against a stormy ground in von Stuck's customary bituminous palette. The classical subject is treated with the muscular Symbolist eroticism he made the signature of the Munich Secession.
collection-of-beauty-franz-von-stuck-004: A nude woman emerges from shadow with an enormous serpent coiled across her shoulders, its head reared at her ear. The picture, replicated in several versions, distils von Stuck's Symbolist creed: a frontal nude, deep gilded frame, and femme-fatale eroticism rendered in glazed, almost lacquered pigment.
collection-of-beauty-franz-von-stuck-sal: Salome dances before a moonlit sky, the platter bearing the head of the Baptist held up by a grinning attendant. Von Stuck stages the subject with bare midriff, jewelled adornments and the brooding theatricality favoured by fin-de-siècle Munich Symbolism.
collection-of-beauty-frederick-law-olmst: Sargent painted the landscape architect of Central Park during work on the Biltmore Estate grounds, posing him among the rhododendrons he had laid out. Loose, plein-air handling of the foliage gives way to a tighter likeness of the sitter, knitting Realist portraiture to Impressionist surface.
collection-of-beauty-fujiwara-no-yasumas: A late triptych by Yoshitoshi shows the Heian courtier Fujiwara no Yasumasa playing his flute on a moonlit moor while the bandit Hakamadare, disarmed by the music, looks on. Long stretches of muted ground and the cool autumnal palette demonstrate Yoshitoshi's reformed late style for the Meiji woodblock market.
collection-of-beauty-fuseliartistmovedto: Fuseli's draughtsman slumps against a colossal antique foot and hand, overwhelmed by the ruins of classical achievement. Drawn in red chalk and sepia wash during his Roman years, the sheet captures the Sublime sense of inadequacy before antiquity that shaped Anglo-Continental Romanticism.
collection-of-beauty-futsuka-zuki-by-kaw: A slender new moon hangs above a quiet hillside in sumi ink and faint colour on silk, mounted as a hanging scroll. Kawai Gyokudō, a leading figure of the modern Nihonga movement, won first prize at the 1907 Tokyo industry exhibition with this lyrical evocation of the second-night sky.
collection-of-beauty-g-caillebotte-le-ja: A snow-blanketed view of the painter's own riverside garden across the Seine from Argenteuil, where Caillebotte spent his final years tending plants and painting boats. The muted whites and bare branches show the late, looser brushwork he developed after retreating from Paris's Impressionist circle.
collection-of-beauty-g-caillebotte-nastu: A close, plunging view of nasturtium leaves and blossoms painted in Caillebotte's Petit Gennevilliers garden. The unusual cropping and high vantage echo the steep perspectives he favoured in his urban scenes, applied here to a horticultural subject he cultivated himself.
collection-of-beauty-g-caillebotte-un-ba: Two top-hatted men gaze down from the wrought-iron balcony of the artist's apartment at 31 Boulevard Haussmann, the same Parisian vantage point Caillebotte used in several canvases of the period. The plunging perspective and modern bourgeois subject typify his engagement with Haussmann's reshaped city.
collection-of-beauty-g-caillebotte-villa: Pastel seaside villas line the Normandy coast at Trouville, a fashionable resort favoured by Parisians on holiday. Caillebotte's looser handling and brighter palette here reflect his shift from urban scenes toward the open-air subjects of his later career.
collection-of-beauty-gauguin-bretonne: A young woman in the white coif and dark dress of Pont-Aven posed during Gauguin's first Brittany sojourn. The simplified silhouette and reserved palette anticipate the cloisonnist flattening he would push further in following summers among the Breton peasantry.
collection-of-beauty-gauguin-huttes-sous: Thatched cottages glimpsed through tree trunks, painted during Gauguin's stay in Martinique in 1887. The tropical light and broad colour areas mark his decisive turn away from Impressionist division of tone toward the synthetist style of Pont-Aven.
collection-of-beauty-general-sir-david-b: A vast late history painting commemorating the British general's discovery of Tipu Sultan's body after the storming of Seringapatam in 1799. Wilkie staged the torch-lit scene with theatrical chiaroscuro, working in the grand manner he adopted following his travels in Spain.
collection-of-beauty-gentileschi-artemis: The biblical queen swoons before the Persian king as she pleads for her people, a subject Gentileschi treats with operatic gesture and sumptuous fabrics. Painted during her Neapolitan years, it shows her assimilation of Caravaggesque drama into a more courtly, decorative key.
collection-of-beauty-gentileschi-lucrezi: The Roman matron Lucretia turns the dagger on herself rather than live with dishonour, a subject Gentileschi returned to repeatedly. The compressed half-length composition and raking light typify her late Naples manner, where female protagonists confront violence with stoic resolve.
collection-of-beauty-gentileschi-allegor: A self-portrait in the guise of Pittura, the female personification of Painting, holding brush and palette in mid-stroke. Gentileschi exploited her gender to claim an allegorical role her male peers could not assume, presenting herself as the embodiment of the art itself.
collection-of-beauty-georges-seurat-seat: A preparatory oil study for one of the bathers in Une Baignade, Asnieres, the large canvas that announced Seurat's ambitions in 1884. Built from broad criss-crossed strokes in the conté-drawing manner he called balayé, it precedes his fully developed pointillist technique.
collection-of-beauty-georges-seurat-c-18: An early still life painted while Seurat was still absorbing Barbizon and Impressionist precedents. The dense impasto and earthy palette show him before the systematic divisionism of dots that would define his mature work later in the decade.
collection-of-beauty-georges-seurat-land: An early landscape from the industrial outskirts north of Paris, painted before Seurat formulated his pointillist method. Loose, almost Corot-like brushwork registers river, sky and bank in muted tones, a quiet rehearsal for the rigorous technique to come.
collection-of-beauty-georges-seurat-les-: Three views of the same studio model — facing, in profile and from behind — set against the painter's own Grande Jatte. Seurat used the picture as a programmatic demonstration of pointillism applied to the academic motif of the nude.
collection-of-beauty-georges-seurat-view: A coastal view from Grandcamp on the Normandy shore, part of the seaside campaigns Seurat undertook each summer to test his divisionist method against changing light. Tiny calibrated dots dissolve fortification, sea and sky into a luminous, near-abstract grid.
collection-of-beauty-georges-seurat-019: Seurat's last, unfinished canvas captures a circus performance with a bareback rider, ringmaster and tiered audience. The arabesque rhythms and yellow-blue contrasts apply Charles Henry's theories of expressive line and colour, pushing pointillism toward decorative stylisation.
collection-of-beauty-georges-seurat-026: A quiet riverbank study made while Seurat was finishing his Grande Jatte campaigns. The Seine flows past the island in patiently calibrated dots of complementary colour, a small-format demonstration of the optical mixing his divisionist method depended on.
collection-of-beauty-georges-seurat-048: Labourers at work levelling earth, a subject from the industrial fringes of Paris that Seurat tackled in his early career. The broad balayé brushwork and sober palette precede the ordered dots of his pointillist maturity.
collection-of-beauty-georges-de-la-tour-: A naive young man has his palm read by an old gypsy while her accomplices pick his purse and cut his medal chain. La Tour stages the swindle in cool daylight rather than candlelight, exploiting fixed sidelong glances to spell out the deception.

A tightly cropped nocturne in which a woman shields a candle as another presents a swaddled child, often read as a Nativity in contemporary dress. The smooth, sculptural surfaces and concealed flame are hallmarks of La Tour's late Lorraine candlelit manner.

Itinerant street musicians come to blows over a hurdy-gurdy, one wielding a knife as a companion squeezes a lemon to feign a wound. La Tour stages the brawl in cool daylight, his earlier Caravaggesque manner before he fully turned to candlelit nocturnes.

A pipe-smoker leans into his glowing taper, the small flame illuminating cheek, hand and clay bowl against deep shadow. La Tour reduces the genre subject to a meditative still life in light, the kind of intimate nocturne that defines his late Lunéville workshop.

The penitent saint sits in shadow with hand on skull, contemplating an oil lamp whose smoking wick stands for the brevity of life. One of several Magdalen meditations La Tour painted, it reduces the iconography to flame, flesh and the geometry of darkness.

The Magdalen sits before a mirror that reflects only a candle and skull, her jewels discarded at her feet. La Tour pares the vanitas iconography to its essentials and lets the single flame model her face and hands with mathematical calm.

Another of La Tour's penitent Magdalens, the saint absorbed in candlelit thought before a skull and crucifix. The repeated subject across his oeuvre allowed him to refine the pictorial logic of a single flame describing flesh, fabric and bone.

An angel appears to the sleeping Joseph in a candlelit interior, the standard Lorraine nocturne formula put to a religious end. The shielded flame, smooth modelling and quiet psychology are characteristic of La Tour's mature devotional pictures.

Job sits naked on his dunghill while his wife berates him by the light of a single candle. La Tour treats the Old Testament confrontation as an intimate two-figure nocturne, the flame between them mapping every fold of skin and cloth.
collection-of-beauty-gerechtigkeit-1537: Justice personified as a nude woman holding sword and scales, a humanist subject Cranach's Wittenberg workshop produced in many versions for Saxon patrons. The sinuous body type and dark ground are signatures of his late mythological and allegorical nudes.
collection-of-beauty-gerhard-von-kugelge: A bust-length likeness of Caspar David Friedrich painted by his close friend in Dresden. Kügelgen catches the Romantic landscapist with sharp side-lit features and inward gaze, fixing the public image of the painter at the height of his early career.
collection-of-beauty-gericaulthorseman: A mounted officer of Napoleon's Imperial Guard wheels his rearing chestnut horse against a smoke-streaked sky. Géricault's Salon debut at twenty-one announced a new Romantic energy in French painting, with Rubensian colour and a kinetic diagonal that broke decisively from Davidian decorum.
collection-of-beauty-gerome-haggin-horse: A bustling North African market scene rendered with Gérôme's characteristic ethnographic precision: tethered Arabian horses, robed traders, and sun-bleached architecture under a hot, even light. The picture belongs to the Haggin Museum's deep Gérôme holding, assembled by California financier James Ben Ali Haggin.
collection-of-beauty-gerome-haggin-saddl: A crowded Cairo saddler's stall hung with embroidered tack, leather cushions and ornate stirrups. Gérôme treats the merchandise as still life within a genre scene, balancing meticulous draftsmanship of leatherwork with the sun-flooded stone arcades he studied on repeated trips to Egypt.
collection-of-beauty-gerome-haggin-the-a: A late self-reflexive subject: Gérôme depicts himself in his studio carving a tinted marble figure, addressing his lifelong campaign for polychrome sculpture. Smooth academic finish gives way to studio clutter of mallets, plaster casts and pigment pots, declaring painting and sculpture as kindred crafts.
collection-of-beauty-gerome-haggin-unfol: A standard-bearer in Cairo unfurls the green banner of the Prophet before a mosque facade, surrounded by attentive figures. Gérôme stages the ceremony like a frieze, using crisp profile poses and tile patterns rendered with near-photographic care.
collection-of-beauty-gerome-an-idyll-185: An early pastoral by Gérôme in the vein of his Néo-Grec circle, reaching back to Anacreontic and Pompeian models. Slender nudes, shallow stage-like space and porcelain-cool color place the picture closer to his Greek Interior than to his later Orientalist scenes.
collection-of-beauty-gerome-venus: Venus emerges from the sea trailing the morning star, her body lit with the pale, enamel finish Gérôme cultivated late in life. The composition revisits the Anadyomene tradition while flirting with the polychrome sculpture project that preoccupied his final decades.
collection-of-beauty-gerrit-van-honthors: A candle-lit brothel scene in which an old procuress brokers a young woman to a leering client. Honthorst, nicknamed Gherardo delle Notti in Rome, transplanted Caravaggio's tenebrism north; here a hidden flame carves the faces from the dark in glowing reds and ochres.
collection-of-beauty-gerome-the-two-maje: A male lion crouches on a desert outcrop facing the setting sun, the two 'majesties' of the title. Gérôme strips the picture to silhouette and burning sky, pursuing in paint the same monumental animal studies that fed his bronze sculpture practice.
collection-of-beauty-gerome-execution-of: A cold December morning at the Luxembourg gardens: Napoleon's marshal lies face down in the mud as the firing squad turns away, posters of his condemnation peeling on the wall behind. Gérôme uses an austere, almost reportorial composition to deliver a quiet indictment of Bourbon vengeance.
collection-of-beauty-gerome-femme-circas: A bust-length study of a young Circassian woman whose features are nearly hidden by a fine white veil, her dark eyes the only direct address to the viewer. Gérôme exploits the contrast between hyper-fine gauze and the heavy embroidered cap to flaunt his control of texture.
collection-of-beauty-gerome-cave-canem: A reclining nude rests in a Pompeian-style interior beside a watchful dog, the title quoting the famous mosaic threshold inscription 'beware of the dog'. Gérôme blends archaeological staging with cabinet-picture eroticism in a tightly polished surface.
collection-of-beauty-gerome-l-entree-du-: Christ on the donkey descends a road white with Judean dust, the procession trailing behind him under a sky bleached by glare. Gérôme treats the Gospel scene as travel reportage from his Holy Land journeys, scattering the crowd into a long, frieze-like recession.
collection-of-beauty-gerome-painting-bre: An ancient Greek artisan paints a terracotta Tanagra figurine, the Latin title declaring that 'painting breathes life into sculpture'. The picture is Gérôme's manifesto for polychromy, arguing that the white marbles museums revered were originally as colored as this little figure.
collection-of-beauty-gerome-summer-after: A rare unpopulated landscape from Gérôme: still water, a wooded shore and a single skiff under hazy summer light. Stripped of his usual ethnographic figures, the picture shows the careful tonal control underlying his more theatrical work.
collection-of-beauty-gerome-eminence-gri: Capuchin friar François Leclerc du Tremblay descends a staircase at the Palais-Cardinal, courtiers bowing to the grey habit while ignoring the man inside it. Gérôme stages the scene as silent political theatre, contrasting the friar's coarse wool against polished marble and gilded livery.
collection-of-beauty-giaele-e-sisara-ca-: Jael drives a tent peg through the temple of the sleeping Canaanite general Sisera, a biblical assassination Artemisia returned to repeatedly. Caravaggesque chiaroscuro isolates the act against deep shadow, with the heroine's calm, deliberate grip typical of Gentileschi's strong-willed women.
collection-of-beauty-giorgione-pala-di-c: A sacra conversazione altarpiece commissioned for the chapel of Tuzio Costanzo in Castelfranco Veneto, with the enthroned Virgin lifted high above SS. Francis and Nicasius. Giorgione's softly fused light and twilight landscape behind the parapet anticipate the lyrical mood of Venetian painting to come.
collection-of-beauty-giorgione-sleeping-: A nude Venus reclines along a hillside, eyes closed, her body echoing the gentle curves of the landscape. Begun by Giorgione and finished after his death by Titian, who is thought to have added the drapery and setting, the picture launched the long line of reclining Venuses culminating in the Urbino Venus.
collection-of-beauty-girl-carrying-a-bas: A young woman trudges along a windswept English coast with her basket, painted during Homer's stay at the fishing village of Cullercoats. The Northumbrian sojourn shifted his palette toward greyed blues and ochres and gave his figures the weathered seriousness that marks his late marines.
collection-of-beauty-giuseppe-arcimboldo: Part of the Four Elements cycle painted for Maximilian II in Vienna, Fire builds a head from candles, oil lamps, flintlocks and burning kindling. Arcimboldo's witty composite portraits flatter the Habsburgs as masters of the elements while showcasing his Mannerist appetite for paradox.
collection-of-beauty-giuseppe-bartolomeo: The chaste Susanna twists away from two leering elders who corner her at her bath, the apocryphal subject a favorite vehicle for the female nude. Chiari, a pupil of Carlo Maratta, paints in a late-Roman Baroque idiom of cool flesh tones and theatrical gesture.
collection-of-beauty-glazunov-by-repin: A bust-length portrait of the young composer Alexander Glazunov caught mid-thought, his gaze sliding away from the viewer. Repin works with the loose, painterly attack of his portrait practice in the late 1880s, when he documented much of the Russian musical and literary intelligentsia.
collection-of-beauty-goethe-kugelgen: A sober three-quarter portrait of Goethe at fifty-nine, painted in Weimar by the Dresden academician Gerhard von Kügelgen. The poet wears civil dress against a plain dark ground, the smooth Neoclassical handling presenting him as statesman of letters rather than Romantic genius.
collection-of-beauty-goltzius-s-right-ha: A virtuoso pen-and-ink study of the artist's own drawing hand, deformed since childhood by a burn. Goltzius turns the disability into a manifesto of skill, building the form from his signature swelling-and-tapering parallel hatchings copied across Europe.
collection-of-beauty-gowy-atalanta-prado: Hippomenes sprints past Atalanta as she stoops for the golden apple of Aphrodite, the wager that costs the huntress her freedom. The Prado canvas is a workshop version after Rubens's design, retaining the diagonal sweep of bodies and the fluttering drapery typical of his Ovidian commissions for Philip IV.
collection-of-beauty-goya-the-garroted-m: An etching of a condemned prisoner slumped in the iron collar of the garrote, crucifix in hand. Goya pares the scene to a single lit body against deep aquatint shadow, prefiguring the political grimness of the Disasters of War.
collection-of-beauty-goya-maja-naga2: A reclining nude meets the viewer's gaze without mythological alibi, the first life-size unidealized female nude in Spanish painting. Commissioned by prime minister Manuel Godoy for a private cabinet, the picture later drew the Inquisition's attention to Goya himself.
collection-of-beauty-goyo-hashiguchi-191: A young woman freshly emerged from the bath gathers her loosened hair, skin warm against the cool indigo of her robe. Hashiguchi, a leading shin-hanga designer, revived the bijin-ga tradition of Utamaro with modern psychological intimacy and richly burnished woodblock printing.
collection-of-beauty-grant-wood-american: A stern Iowa farmer and his daughter stand before a white frame house with a Carpenter Gothic window, the pitchfork echoing the seams of his overalls. Wood used his sister Nan and his dentist as models, fashioning a dry, hard-edged image that became shorthand for rural America.
collection-of-beauty-grecian-interior-le: A scene in the women's quarters of a Greek house, with nudes lounging amid braziers, drapery and patterned tile. The picture is one of Gérôme's Néo-Grec confections, dressing up an academic studio nude in archaeological props derived from Pompeian wall painting.
collection-of-beauty-guercino-martirio-d: A Bolognese Baroque altarpiece depicting the martyrdom of the Roman brothers John and Paul, beheaded under Julian the Apostate. Guercino orchestrates the figures along a steep diagonal, his characteristically warm tenebrism modelling flesh against deep shadow with the loose, painterly touch of his mature Roman period.
collection-of-beauty-guo-zhongshu-travel: Cargo ships with stern-mounted rudders glide through a snow-stilled river, accompanied by a smaller craft. Painted in fine ink on silk, the work exemplifies early Song dynasty jiehua, the precise ruled-line technique used to render boats and architecture with engineering accuracy.
collection-of-beauty-gustav-bauernfeind-: A bustling Damascus thoroughfare rendered with the German Orientalist's exacting eye for tilework, lattice and crowd. Bauernfeind, trained as an architect, treated the city's facades almost archaeologically, balancing photographic detail with the strong overhead light of the Levant.

Pilgrims and worshippers gather between the Mamluk-era Sabil of Qaytbay and the gleaming Dome of the Rock on the Haram al-Sharif. Bauernfeind, who eventually settled in Jerusalem, painted the site repeatedly, prizing the geometry of its mosaic and stone over picturesque incident.

The walled Old City catches first light from the Mount of Olives, the Dome of the Rock luminous above the Kidron Valley. One of Bauernfeind's last major canvases, painted after he made Jerusalem his home, it trades street bustle for an almost devotional topographic stillness.

Figures cluster at the threshold of one of Islam's oldest monuments, the painter dwelling on the carved stone, polychrome marble inlay and worn thresholds of the Bab al-Barid. The composition is typical of Bauernfeind's Damascene pictures, framing dense human activity within rigorously drawn architecture.

An elderly warden stands sentinel in the courtyard of the Umayyad Mosque, his white robe set against patterned stone. Bauernfeind treats the single figure with quiet dignity, the surrounding architecture rendered with the meticulous draftsmanship of his architectural training.
collection-of-beauty-gustave-caillebotte: A wet-cobbled Place de Dublin in Haussmann's rebuilt Paris, viewed from Rue de Moscou. Caillebotte, the Impressionists' patron and engineer, here departs from their feathery touch for a cool, photographic precision, deploying steep perspective and a near-life-size foreground couple.

A solitary oarsman pulls down the Yerres river near the artist's family estate, the viewpoint set startlingly low and close to the rower's straining body. Caillebotte's boating scenes treat leisure with the engineer's eye for mechanism, anticipating the cropped framing of modern photography.
collection-of-beauty-gustave-courbet-jo-: A portrait of Joanna Hiffernan, model and lover of Whistler, gazing into a hand mirror as she draws her copper-red hair through her fingers. Courbet painted at least four versions, the dense impasto and earth-toned palette characteristic of his Realist studio practice.
collection-of-beauty-gustave-courbet-la-: A breaking wave rears in heavy impasto under a brooding Channel sky, the horizon pushed high and the surf laid on with palette knife. Courbet painted dozens of these studies on the Normandy coast at Étretat in 1869, treating seascape as a study of matter rather than picturesque incident.
collection-of-beauty-gustave-courbet-le-: A wide-eyed self-portrait, the young Courbet clutching his hair and staring directly out at the viewer. Painted in his early twenties, it announces the confrontational self-fashioning that would define his career and the Realist project.
collection-of-beauty-gustave-courbet-029: A nude study in Courbet's signature register, modelled with the dense, kneaded paint and warm flesh tones of his mid-1860s work. The treatment privileges the tactile reality of the body over idealised form, characteristic of the Realist's frank handling of the figure.
collection-of-beauty-gustave-courbet-038: A young spinner has fallen asleep at her wheel, distaff drooping in her lap. Courbet treats the rural genre subject with monumental gravity, applying paint thickly with knife and brush so that the homely interior carries the weight of a Salon picture.
collection-of-beauty-gustave-dore-la-sie: A recollection of Doré's Spanish travels, figures resting under midday heat in a sun-bleached landscape. Better known as a wood-engraver, Doré painted ambitious oils late in his career, here applying the dramatic chiaroscuro of his book illustrations to a Romantic genre subject.
collection-of-beauty-gustave-dore-la-val: A column of suffering humanity processes through a darkened valley toward the figure of Christ bearing the cross. Painted in Doré's final years and exhibited posthumously, the canvas extends his Bible illustrations into vast, sermon-like oil, with the steep tonal contrasts of his engravings.
collection-of-beauty-gustave-dore-landsc: A Highland glen under turbulent skies, painted after Doré's salmon-fishing expeditions to Scotland in the 1870s. As an Alsatian outside the Parisian academy, he turned to landscape oils late, importing the grand vistas and atmospheric drama of an earlier Romantic generation.
collection-of-beauty-gustave-dore-roland: A wood engraving from Quatrelles' Légende de Croquemitaine, depicting the Carolingian hero Roland in full armour. Doré's chivalric illustration trades on the same dense crosshatching and theatrical lighting that made his Don Quixote and Bible plates ubiquitous in nineteenth-century print culture.
collection-of-beauty-gustave-dore-the-ho: The frontispiece to Doré's 1866 illustrated Bible: humans and a tigress alike struggle to save their young as the floodwaters rise. Engraved in dense black-and-white from Doré's design, the plate established the visual template for the Deluge in popular imagination for generations.
collection-of-beauty-henri-rousseau-la-e: A dark silhouetted figure pipes serpents from a moonlit jungle on a riverbank, commissioned by the mother of Robert Delaunay. Rousseau, a self-taught customs officer, never left France; his jungles were composed from the Jardin des Plantes, picture books and dream, layered in his deliberate, flattened naïve manner.
collection-of-beauty-harunobu-2-pers: A pair of figures in the slender, doll-like proportions Harunobu favoured, set within a softly muted interior. He was the pioneer of full-colour nishiki-e printing in 1760s Edo, shifting ukiyo-e away from hand-tinted limited palettes toward layered woodblock impressions.
collection-of-beauty-harunobu-bijin-sur-: An oversized nishiki-e showing a woman on a veranda outside a chamber where a party is underway, parodying the Muken-no-Kane scene from the play Hiragana Seisuiki. Harunobu's mitate updates classical drama into the language of the Edo pleasure quarters, with his characteristically delicate, almost weightless figures.
collection-of-beauty-harunobu-jouvenceau: An unsigned chuban egoyomi, or pictorial calendar print, in which the days of the long and short months are concealed within the design. Harunobu produced these privately commissioned prints for poetry circles in the mid-1760s, and their experiments drove the development of full-colour brocade printing.
collection-of-beauty-harunobu-kanzan-et-: A mitate of the Chinese Chan eccentrics Hanshan and Shide, Jittoku identifiable by his ever-present broom. Harunobu reimagines the Buddhist pair in the contemporary dress of Edo youths, the kind of layered classical-vernacular play that defined his refined nishiki-e output.
collection-of-beauty-hasegawa-tohaku-pin: The right wing of the celebrated Pine Trees screens, designated a National Treasure of Japan. Tōhaku, founder of the Hasegawa school, paints the pines with rapid sumi ink in pale wash and dry brush, leaving vast areas of unpainted paper to suggest swirling mist — a high point of Momoyama monochrome ink.
collection-of-beauty-hashiguchi-goyo-wom: A woman in a sheer blue summer kimono draws a comb through her loosened hair, her concentration absolute. Hashiguchi was a leading shin-hanga designer who oversaw every stage of his prints; the work uses mica grounds and subtle keyblock embossing to revive the bijin tradition for the Taishō era.
collection-of-beauty-hatsuhana-doing-pen: From the series Lives of Wise Women and Virtuous Wives, Hatsuhana of the joruri play Hakone Reigen prays under the icy Tonosawa falls to obtain her crippled husband's revenge. Kuniyoshi treats the ascetic subject with characteristic dynamism, the cascading water rendered in steep blue parallel lines.
collection-of-beauty-havremonet: A canvas of Le Havre's commercial basin, the harbour from which Monet had launched Impression, Sunrise two years earlier. Painted with rapid broken touches, the work continues his investigation of Channel-port atmospheres, where masts, smoke and reflected light become almost interchangeable.
collection-of-beauty-hayez-francesco-acc: A Venetian noblewoman pauses by the bocca di leone, the lion's-mouth letterbox into which secret denunciations were dropped under the Republic. Hayez, leader of Italian Romantic painting, often coded contemporary Risorgimento politics into such historical scenes, here rendered with his porcelain-smooth flesh and rich brocades.
collection-of-beauty-hayez-lot-con-le-fi: Lot's daughters ply their father with wine in the cave above the ruined Sodom, in Hayez's reading of the Genesis episode. The Milanese Romantic stages the scene like an opera, posing the figures within a sharply lit grotto and modelling flesh with the Neoclassical polish inherited from his teacher Canova.
collection-of-beauty-hayez-tamar-di-giud: The biblical Tamar, daughter-in-law of Judah, is rendered as a brooding half-length figure veiled in shadowed reds. Hayez, the central painter of Italian Romanticism, treats Old Testament heroines with the same charged sensuality and theatrical chiaroscuro he brought to his patriotic allegories.
collection-of-beauty-hayez-selbstportrat: A late self-portrait at age seventy-eight, the Milanese master appraising himself with calm, unflattering directness. The restrained palette and tightly painted head reflect the academic rigour Hayez upheld at the Brera even as Romanticism gave way to verismo.
collection-of-beauty-head-of-cyrus-broug: Herodotus' Massagetae queen receives the severed head of the Persian king Cyrus, whom she will plunge into a vessel of blood. Rubens stages the Antwerp workshop's signature pile-up of muscular soldiery and silken courtiers, the carmine drapery and pearled flesh tones unmistakably his.
collection-of-beauty-hendrick-goltzius-d: The Terentian motto — without Ceres and Bacchus, Venus grows cold — is set as a chiaroscuro nocturne, the goddess illuminated by a torch held by a satyr. Goltzius, the virtuoso Haarlem engraver, here works with brush on prepared ground in his late painterly mode.
collection-of-beauty-hendrick-goltzius-f: The colossal Farnese Hercules, leaning on his club with the apples of the Hesperides behind his back, observed from below by two awed connoisseurs. Goltzius' celebrated engraving exaggerates the antique's swelling musculature with his trademark swelling-and-tapering burin lines.
collection-of-beauty-hendrick-goltzius-p: The Haarlem brewer and shell connoisseur is shown surrounded by exotic conches and a nautilus, emblems of the Wunderkammer culture flourishing in the early Dutch Republic. Goltzius records both the sitter's penetrating gaze and the iridescence of his collection with equal precision.
collection-of-beauty-hendrick-martensz-s: A young man tunes his lute in a quiet Rotterdam interior, a music sheet open before him. Sorgh, a pupil of Teniers and Buytewech, specialised in such genteel domestic genre scenes, building tone with a warm brown ground beneath crisp local colour.
collection-of-beauty-hendrik-avercamp-wi: Skaters, kolf players and bundled townsfolk fan out across a frozen canal under a low pearly sky. The deaf-mute Avercamp made the panoramic winter landscape his exclusive subject, populating each ice with hundreds of carefully observed figures in a high horizon line inherited from Bruegel.
collection-of-beauty-henri-julien-felix-: Two costumed lovers stand at the edge of a leafless winter wood beneath a cold full moon, a tiny lit lantern hovering at the treeline. One of Rousseau's earliest important canvases, it already shows the naive precision of silhouette and dreamlike stillness that would define the Douanier's vision.
collection-of-beauty-henri-fantin-latour: A group portrait of the avant-garde gathered around Manet at his easel, with Renoir, Bazille, Monet and Zola among the standing figures. Fantin-Latour records the Batignolles circle as a sober Salon homage, painted in the muted blacks and greys he preferred to his friends' brighter palette.

Roses, peaches and grapes are arrayed on a plain tabletop against a neutral ground. Fantin-Latour's still lifes, prized in England via his dealer Edwin Edwards, distil the Dutch tradition into quiet, almost photographic studies of bloom and bloom-bearing skin.

The English etcher and printer Edwin Edwards sits at his desk while his wife Ruth stands behind him, both rendered in Fantin's habitual sober blacks. The Edwardses were the artist's closest patrons, channelling his still lifes into the lucrative London market.

A glass carafe, scattered fruit and a small bouquet are composed on a dark cloth in disciplined Salon order. Among Fantin-Latour's earliest mature still lifes, it shows the precise tonal modelling that drew Whistler's circle to his work.
collection-of-beauty-henri-matisse-1902-: The cathedral seen from Matisse's quai Saint-Michel apartment late on a winter afternoon, its towers reduced to slate-blue silhouettes over the Seine. Painted on paper during a precarious year before Fauvism, the picture already pushes structural colour ahead of topographical accuracy.
collection-of-beauty-henri-matisse-1920-: A reclining model in striped pantaloons inhabits the patterned Nice studio Matisse converted into a North African stage set. The early Riviera odalisques mark his return to sensuous, decorative figuration after the austerity of the war years.
collection-of-beauty-henri-meunier04: A poster, L'Heure du Silence, by the Belgian Art Nouveau designer Henri Meunier, lithographed in the flat planes and sinuous contour the Brussels school adopted from Japanese prints. Meunier worked extensively in advertising, treating commercial commissions with the same graphic refinement as his fine-art prints.
collection-of-beauty-henry-fuseli-titani: The fairy queen, drugged by Oberon's love-juice, fawns over the ass-headed weaver from A Midsummer Night's Dream. Fuseli, a key contributor to Boydell's Shakespeare Gallery, exaggerates limbs and eyes into a mannerist dream-language drawn from Michelangelo and his own night terrors.
collection-of-beauty-het-zojo-heiligdom-: A woman beneath a paper umbrella crosses a snowy courtyard before the vermilion gate of the Zōjō-ji temple in Shiba. From Hasui's Twenty Views of Tokyo, the print exemplifies the shin-hanga collaboration between artist, carver and printer in capturing weather as atmosphere.
collection-of-beauty-hieronymus-bosch-th: A pen drawing illustrating the Flemish proverb that walls and woods betray secrets, with eyes scattered through the meadow and ears sprouting on tree trunks. The sheet, in the Berlin Kupferstichkabinett, is among the few autograph drawings linking Bosch's moralising imagination to his painted demonology.
