A personification of Summer as harvest and abundance, with peasants gathering grain beneath a glowing sky. Part of a seasonal cycle, the image links rural labor to Flemish landscape tradition and to Rubens's late absorption in country life at Het Steen.
collection-of-beauty-peter-paul-rubens-t: Saint Peter holds the keys of the Kingdom, his furrowed brow and grizzled beard rendered with apostolic gravitas. An early work in Rubens's Apostolado tradition, the figure typifies the half-length devotional saint favored by Counter-Reformation patrons.

The infant Hercules, set to Juno's breast by Jupiter, draws milk so forcefully that the spilled stream becomes the Milky Way. Painted for Philip IV's Torre de la Parada, the canvas turns an Ovidian etiology into a swirl of cosmic drapery and divine flesh.

Moses raises the bronze serpent on a pole as Israelites writhe with snakebites below, looking up to be healed (Numbers 21). Rubens packs the foreground with anatomical contortions, a Counter-Reformation allegory of salvation through faith in the Crucified.

The lifeless Christ is lowered from the cross into the arms of the mourning Virgin and disciples, his pale flesh contrasted with the deep crimson of Joseph of Arimathea's robe. An early Roman-period work, it absorbs lessons from Caravaggio and the Carracci.

Muscular soldiers heave the cross upright on a steep diagonal, Christ's luminous body twisting against a stormy sky. Conceived for Antwerp's St. Walburga, the great triptych translates Italian heroic anatomy into a Flemish altarpiece of unprecedented physical force.

Christ's body is lowered onto the slab of anointing as the Marys and disciples crowd in grief. Rubens tightens Caravaggio's Vatican prototype into an oval of mourning figures, the dead flesh phosphorescent against the surrounding shadow.

Phaeton plummets from the chariot of the Sun as Jupiter's thunderbolt scatters the bolting horses and the Hours recoil through the zodiac. An early mythological tour de force, the picture shows Rubens already pushing Mannerist motion toward Baroque theater.

Worshippers garland a statue of Venus while putti, nymphs and satyrs dance in pagan revelry. Inspired by Titian's Worship of Venus and a passage in Philostratus, the late composition assembles Rubens's lifelong vocabulary of mythic celebration.

An aged hermit, drawn from Ariosto's Orlando Furioso, gazes lasciviously upon the sleeping Angelica in a moonlit forest. Rubens contrasts ascetic gauntness with luminous female flesh, transforming the chivalric romance into a Baroque meditation on temptation.

The Trojan prince Paris weighs the golden apple between Juno, Minerva and Venus while Mercury looks on. An early version of a subject Rubens revisited often, the composition arranges three nude goddesses to display Italianate disegno absorbed during his stay in Rome.

Straining executioners heave the cross bearing Christ skyward in a violent diagonal of muscular labor. A late reprise of his Antwerp altarpiece, this version concentrates the drama into a tighter, more urgent gathering of bodies and tackle.

The Phoenician princess clings to the white bull-Jupiter as he wades into the sea bound for Crete, while companions wave helplessly from shore. Painted as a copy after Titian for Philip IV, the canvas pays homage to Venetian color while asserting Rubens's own touch.

The Madonna and Child appear within an exuberant wreath of blossoms, painted in collaboration with the flower specialist Jan Brueghel the Elder. The conceit fuses Marian devotion with Antwerp's expertise in still life, the garland a votive offering of created beauty.

The Jesuit missionary Francis Xavier raises the dead and heals the afflicted before a toppling pagan idol, the converted crowding toward him in awe. Painted for Antwerp's Jesuit church, the altarpiece celebrates the global reach of Counter-Reformation evangelism.

Venus admires herself in a mirror held by a winged Cupid, a maidservant arranging her golden hair. The intimate composition, drawn from Titian's prototypes, balances cool reflective surface against the warm pink expanse of the goddess's back.

Two satyrs, one with a clutch of grapes and a piercing forward stare, the other grinning behind him, appear in close half-length view. The double tronie distills Rubens's vision of bacchic appetite into an arresting pair of bestial yet humanly knowing faces.
collection-of-beauty-peter-paul-rubens-v: Venus clings to the departing Adonis as he shoulders his spear and his hounds strain toward the fatal hunt. Rubens reworks Titian's Prado composition, heightening the embrace into a Baroque farewell of foreboding and tender resistance.

Rubens's own country estate near Mechelen lies under a low, golden dawn, with a hunter, hay wagon and far-flung fields stretching to the horizon. The expansive panorama, painted for personal pleasure, helped redirect Northern landscape toward atmospheric naturalism.
collection-of-beauty-peter-paul-rubens-w: An oil study of an armoured soldier, executed with the loose brushwork and ruddy palette Rubens reserved for working sketches. The figure's head and gleaming breastplate are modelled with rapid, confident strokes, capturing the swagger of a Baroque military type.

A half-length nude turns toward a mirror held by a maid, her pearly flesh rendered in the warm, fluid manner of Rubens's late period. The composition belongs to a Venetian-derived tradition of the toilette of Venus, transformed here by Flemish opulence.
collection-of-beauty-peter-paul-rubens-0: Bathsheba sits at her bath while a black page delivers David's letter, the Old Testament narrative reframed as an excuse for sumptuous flesh, drapery, and still life. The painting now hangs in the Gemäldegalerie Alte Meister, Dresden.

Salome presents the head of John the Baptist on a salver as Herod recoils at the banquet table. Rubens stages the gruesome moment with theatrical lighting and a dense crush of guests, balancing horror against the gleam of silver and silk.

An intimate, near-life-size portrait of Rubens's second wife Helena Fourment, draped only in a fur cloak. The picture's frank sensuality and tender naturalism mark it as a private image, kept in the artist's own household.

A panoramic view of the country estate Rubens purchased in 1635, with its turreted manor set among golden Brabant fields. The artist treats native landscape with vigorous brushwork and a low, glowing horizon, anticipating later Romantic vistas.
collection-of-beauty-peter-paul-rubens-1: Paris awards the golden apple to Venus while Juno and Minerva recoil in defeat, their nudes arranged in a graceful triad against an Arcadian landscape. Rubens revisited this mythological subject several times; the late Prado version is among his most assured.

A formal portrait of the young Mantuan duke Vincenzo II Gonzaga, painted during Rubens's years at the Gonzaga court. Lace, armour, and the sitter's pale gaze are rendered with the courtly polish the artist absorbed from Italian models.

From the Decius Mus cycle, a tapestry-cartoon-scale battle scene in which the Roman consul plunges to his ritual death amid rearing horses and clashing standards. The composition is a swirling diagonal of armoured bodies, an early showcase of Rubensian dynamism.

A state portrait of Isabella of Bourbon, queen consort of Spain, in stiff black court dress and pearls. Rubens softens the rigid Habsburg formula with subtle modelling of the face and a discreet warmth in the flesh tones.

The Carmelite mystic kneels in ecstasy as Christ appears in a burst of golden cloud. Rubens treats the Counter-Reformation visionary subject with operatic light and surging drapery, in keeping with the Jesuit altarpieces of his Antwerp years.
collection-of-beauty-peter-paul-rubens-2: A bust-length likeness of an unidentified gentleman in dark dress with a starched white collar. The directness of the gaze and the economical handling of the flesh suggest a private commission painted in the artist's mature Antwerp studio.
collection-of-beauty-peter-paul-rubens-t: Christ hangs between the two thieves, the crosses set against a darkening sky in a tightly massed composition. Rubens compresses the Calvary scene into a vertical screen of straining bodies, a Counter-Reformation meditation on suffering and redemption.
collection-of-beauty-peter-paul-rubens-s: A photograph of Rubens's Susanna and the Elders in the Galleria Borghese, Rome, recording the early Italian-period canvas. The biblical heroine twists away from the lecherous elders, her pose drawn from antique sculpture Rubens studied at first hand.
collection-of-beauty-philipp-otto-runge-: A close study of a hound's head in chalk and lead, the breed's soft fur and alert eye observed with quiet precision. The drawing belongs to Runge's wider effort to ground his Romantic symbolism in patient natural observation.

A black and white chalk drawing of the young Sophia Sieveking laid out in death, her features composed against a pale pillow. Runge made the sheet shortly before his own early death, lending its mortuary stillness an autobiographical weight.

A preparatory drawing in chalk and pencil for the unfinished Great Morning, mapping out the central goddess-figure rising amid lilies and infant spirits. The sheet shows Runge's diagrammatic approach to his cosmological Times of Day cycle.

A portrait of the painter and writer Friedrich August von Klinkowström, Runge's close friend and a fellow figure in early German Romantic circles. The sitter is shown with a Nazarene-like clarity of contour and a steady, contemplative gaze.

Three young siblings of the Hülsenbeck family stand and crouch in a Hamburg garden, the toddler clutching a sunflower leaf almost as large as himself. Runge invests the children with hieratic gravity, treating childhood as a metaphysical state.

Christ stretches out his hand to a sinking Peter on the Sea of Galilee, the figures reduced to clear, near-archaic outlines. Runge's interpretation of the Gospel episode reflects his ambition to fuse landscape, religion, and Romantic symbolism.

A detail from the unfinished Great Morning, showing the radiant central figure Runge devised as the embodiment of dawn within his Times of Day cycle. The hard-edged drawing and saturated colour reveal the artist's mystical, almost emblematic Romanticism.
collection-of-beauty-pierre-auguste-reno: The eight-year-old daughter of a Parisian banking family sits in profile, her copper hair flowing loose against a soft blue-green ground. Renoir's feathery brush captures both the child's reverie and the upholstered comfort of haute-bourgeois life.

A frieze of monumental nudes disports along a wooded bank, their crisp contours and pearly flesh marking Renoir's so-called Ingresque turn away from pure Impressionism. The picture occupied him for several years and now hangs at the Philadelphia Museum of Art.

A young woman in a straw hat leans by a sun-dappled lake, the foliage dissolved into iridescent strokes of green and pink. The picture exemplifies Renoir's sociable, plein-air Impressionism of the early 1880s.

Painted on the rocky beaches of Guernsey, where Renoir summered in 1883, the canvas shows small bathers playing among warm ochre boulders. The Channel light prompts a brighter, drier palette and the firmer drawing of his transitional period.

Renoir portrays his fellow Impressionist Frédéric Bazille at work before an easel, brush poised over a still life of a heron. The picture is a touching record of the early Batignolles circle, painted shortly before Bazille's death in the Franco-Prussian War.

Renoir's friends gather on the terrace of the Maison Fournaise at Chatou, lingering over wine and fruit after a row on the Seine. The composition weaves portraiture, still life, and shimmering river light into one celebrated image of modern leisure.

An early still life of garden flowers crowded into a white porcelain vase, painted under the influence of Courbet and Monet. The dense, dark ground and weighty paint surface show Renoir before his Impressionist palette had fully lightened.

The Comédie-Française actress Jeanne Samary leans forward with parted lips and luminous eyes, her pink dress dissolving into the rosy ground. Renoir's brush builds the portrait almost entirely from warm, transparent layers of colour.
collection-of-beauty-pierre-cecile-puvis: A young traveller sleeps in a flowering meadow as three allegorical female figures, Love, Glory and Wealth, drift toward him through the dawn air. Puvis paints The Dream in the muted, fresco-like tonalities that made him a touchstone for the Symbolists.

An allegorical female nude personifying Hope sits on a barren battlefield, holding an olive branch as ruins and grave markers recede behind her. Painted in the aftermath of the Franco-Prussian War, the muted palette and frieze-like simplicity exemplify Puvis de Chavannes's pale, fresco-inspired symbolism.
collection-of-beauty-pierre-auguste-reno: Renoir's view of Venice's central square dissolves the basilica's Byzantine facade into shimmering touches of pink, gold, and blue. Painted during his Italian journey, the canvas treats architecture as an Impressionist screen of light, with figures and pigeons scattered across the sunlit pavement.
collection-of-beauty-pierre-puvis-de-cha: A gaunt fisher stands in his moored skiff while a woman gathers flowers and an infant sleeps on the bank, the scene reduced to chalky, almost frescoed planes of pale color. The austere composition and melancholic mood made the picture a touchstone for Symbolist painters and the young Picasso.
collection-of-beauty-pieter-bruegel-d-a-: A teeming village square stages an allegorical joust between a barrel-riding Carnival, armed with a spit of meat, and a gaunt Lent on a church cart. Bruegel packs the panel with proverbs, Shrovetide revels, and Lenten almsgiving in a panoramic moral inventory of Netherlandish life.
collection-of-beauty-pieter-bruegel-der-: The Holy Family threads a precipitous mountain path while a vast world landscape opens beneath them, with rivers, cliffs, and a shadowed pagan idol toppling on a column. Bruegel's panoramic Weltlandschaft tradition dwarfs the sacred travelers within Alpine and Flemish topographies.
collection-of-beauty-pieter-bruegel-the-: A ploughman, shepherd, and angler go about their work along a sunlit harbor while Icarus's legs vanish almost unnoticed into the sea at lower right. The composition reads as a quiet meditation on Ovid's myth and human indifference, later memorialized in Auden's poem.

Soldiers ransack a snowbound Flemish village in a transposition of Herod's biblical slaughter into the contemporary Low Countries under Spanish occupation. In the Royal Collection version many of the slain children were overpainted as bundles, geese, and game by later hands.

A line of six blind men stumbles diagonally across the panel toward a ditch, each face individually studied for the disfigurements of disease. Bruegel illustrates Christ's parable from Matthew 15 with clinical precision and an unforgiving rhythm of falling bodies.

The smaller of Bruegel's two surviving Babel panels shows the spiraling brick ziggurat looming over a coastal plain, swallowed by storm clouds at its summit. Painstaking architectural detail draws on Roman ruins Bruegel had studied in Italy.

King Nimrod inspects stonemasons in the foreground as the colossal tower rises above a Flemish port, its arcades modeled on the Colosseum. The painting marries Genesis with a sober warning about overreaching ambition, rendered with engineering exactitude.
collection-of-beauty-pitagoras-prohibe-c: Pythagoras gestures toward a heaped still life of fruit, vegetables, and beans, urging his listeners to abstain from animal flesh. The figures are by Rubens while the lush produce was painted by Frans Snyders, the partnership's customary division of labor.
collection-of-beauty-polder-landscape-wi: Cattle wade through reedy lowland water beneath a wide Flemish sky, the herd dispersed across pasture and ditch. Rubens turned to such polder views in the late 1630s after retiring to his country estate at Het Steen.
collection-of-beauty-pont-neuf-1872-pier: Pedestrians and carriages cross Paris's oldest bridge under a clear summer sky, the Seine glittering between embankments. Renoir's brother Edmond reportedly delayed passersby in conversation so the painter could capture them in rapid, sun-drenched touches.
collection-of-beauty-pont-argenteuil-mon: Sailing boats line the basin at Argenteuil beneath the highway bridge, their masts reflected in the broken blue water of the Seine. The picture belongs to the suburban riverscapes that defined the early Impressionist summers of 1872 to 1874.
collection-of-beauty-pope-peter-pprubens: Saint Peter is shown in papal vestments holding the keys of heaven, his weathered features turned upward in devotion. Painted soon after Rubens's return from Italy, the half-length follows a Caravaggesque tradition of monumental, close-cropped apostle portraits.
collection-of-beauty-pope-julius-ii: The aged della Rovere pope is seated in three-quarter view, beard untrimmed in mourning for the loss of Bologna, hands resting on the arms of his chair. Raphael's psychologically piercing portrait introduced a new informal type that would dominate papal imagery for over a century.
collection-of-beauty-portrait-of-the-art: An early portrait by the teenage Vigée Le Brun shows her younger brother Étienne in elegant attire, his open expression rendered with the precocious assurance that would soon win her royal commissions. The handling already displays the porcelain finish and Rococo grace of her mature manner.
collection-of-beauty-portrait-d-edmond-a: Seurat's friend the painter Aman-Jean is seen in profile against a darkened ground, a cigarette in hand, modeled entirely in delicate hatchings of conté crayon on textured paper. The drawing was the first work Seurat exhibited at the Salon and helped establish the velvety tonal manner of his black-and-white sheets.
collection-of-beauty-portrait-de-l-artis: Raphael places himself behind a foregrounded companion who turns to point outward, the two men linked by a cascade of hands and gazes. The double portrait is among the late, most psychologically intricate works produced in his Roman studio.
collection-of-beauty-portrait-of-george-: The royal favorite is shown in armor with a baton of command, the Order of the Garter prominent at his knee. Long thought to be a copy, dendrochronology and technical analysis of the panel preparation now support attribution to Rubens's own studio of the 1620s.
collection-of-beauty-portrait-of-peder-s: Tuxen depicts his fellow Skagen colonist Krøyer at work, palette in hand, eyes fixed on an unseen sitter. The portrait commemorates a friendship between the two leading Danish naturalist painters, set down a few years before Krøyer's mental collapse.
collection-of-beauty-portrait-of-princes: The young princess is presented as Iris, draped in classicizing white muslin against a stormy sky, a length of rainbow gauze trailing from her shoulder. Vigée Le Brun painted her in Vienna during the émigré years that followed the artist's flight from revolutionary France.
collection-of-beauty-portrait-of-a-monk-: A bearded religious in dark habit emerges from a shadowed ground, his lined face caught in raking light. Rubens repeatedly studied such ascetic types as preparatory heads for his larger altarpieces of saints and church fathers.
collection-of-beauty-portret-van-etienne: The Parisian restaurateur is rendered with brisk, hatched brushwork in warm browns and greens, the modeling already loosened by Van Gogh's exposure to Impressionist technique. The portrait dates from his Paris year, when he was experimenting with the divisionist touch of Seurat and Signac.
collection-of-beauty-pozzi-samuel-par-sa: The celebrated Parisian gynecologist Samuel Pozzi stands full length in a flame-red dressing gown, fingers tugging at his collar and sash. Sargent's bravura essay in monochrome scarlet was painted in the sitter's own apartment and announced the young American's arrival in Parisian society.
collection-of-beauty-prayer-in-the-mosqu: Worshippers prostrate themselves in long rows beneath the soaring arches of Cairo's Mosque of Amr, sunlight slanting across the columns and prayer mats. Gérôme's enamel-smooth Orientalism rests on direct study of Egyptian architecture from his repeated travels in the country.
collection-of-beauty-print-the-flight-in: An engraving showing the Virgin and Child on the donkey under a date palm, with angels shaking down its fruit while Joseph gathers the dates. Schongauer's fine, controlled burin work and tender narrative detail set the model for later northern printmaking, including the young Dürer.
collection-of-beauty-procesion-de-pascua: A vast religious procession winds across a sunburnt steppe, sweeping clergy, peasants, gendarmes, and crippled pilgrims into a single dust-clouded cross-section of imperial Russian society. Repin's panoramic canvas is a touchstone of Peredvizhniki social realism.
collection-of-beauty-promenade-sur-la-fa: Strolling figures top a grassy clifftop above the Channel near Le Havre, with sailing vessels scattered across a breezy sea. Painted at his family's seaside resort, the picture marks Monet's emergence from Boudin's tutelage toward his own brisk plein-air idiom.
collection-of-beauty-purfleet-and-the-es: Sailing barges and Thames hoys ride the broad estuary off the chalk cliffs of Purfleet, the low Essex shore stretching beyond. The watercolor belongs to Turner's early topographical phase, when accurate maritime detail was still the foundation of his atmospheric effects.
collection-of-beauty-pythagoras-advocati: Rubens depicts the Greek philosopher gesturing toward a heap of fruit and grain as he urges his listeners to abstain from meat. The composition draws on Ovid's Metamorphoses, with abundant still-life passages painted in collaboration with Frans Snyders.
collection-of-beauty-qian-xuan-early-aut: A handscroll in ink and color on paper showing dragonflies, frogs, and aquatic plants beside a pond. Qian Xuan was a scholar-painter of the late Song and early Yuan, and the work pairs close natural observation with the lyric reserve of literati painting.
collection-of-beauty-quai-du-louvre-1867: An early Parisian cityscape, painted from a balcony of the Louvre overlooking the Seine. Bustling figures, carriages, and the dome of the Pantheon are rendered with the high vantage and bright daylight that anticipate Monet's mature Impressionist manner.
collection-of-beauty-raffaello-sanzio-ri: A portrait of the young Florentine banker Bindo Altoviti, who turns over his shoulder to meet the viewer's gaze. The cool palette, soft modeling, and elegant turn of the head exemplify Raphael's late Roman portrait style.
collection-of-beauty-raimondi-lucretia-s: An engraving after a design by Raphael showing the Roman matron Lucretia about to plunge a dagger into her breast. Raimondi's burin work helped establish reproductive printmaking as a vehicle for disseminating Italian Renaissance invention across Europe.
collection-of-beauty-raphael-fire-in-the: A fresco in the Vatican's Stanza dell'Incendio del Borgo, depicting the miraculous extinguishing of a fire in Rome's Borgo district by Pope Leo IV. Heroic nudes and a fleeing Aeneas-like figure carrying his father give the scene a deliberately classical cast.
collection-of-beauty-raphael-the-miracul: A cartoon for the Sistine Chapel tapestry series commissioned by Pope Leo X, showing Christ blessing Peter and Andrew as their nets break under the weight of fish. The kneeling apostle and the cranes on the shore are among the most studied figures in Renaissance art.
collection-of-beauty-raphael-young-man-c: A red-chalk drawing of Aeneas carrying Anchises from burning Troy, made as a study for the figure group in the Fire in the Borgo fresco. The athletic torsion and confident hatching show Raphael's response to antique sculpture and to Michelangelo.
collection-of-beauty-red-fuji-southern-w: Better known as Red Fuji, this woodblock print captures the volcano flushed warm by dawn light during the late summer southerlies. Banded clouds and a near-abstract triangular silhouette make it a touchstone of the Thirty-six Views series.
collection-of-beauty-remaining-snow-at-i: A shin-hanga woodblock print of the wooded park at Inokashira in Tokyo, with patches of late snow lingering beside dark water. Hasui treats the season with the muted palette and atmospheric stillness that defined his landscape practice.
collection-of-beauty-rembrandt-portrait-: A portrait of the artist's wife shortly after their marriage, dressed in fanciful pastoral costume with a feathered hat. Rembrandt's warm light glances across pearls, embroidery, and her unguarded expression.
collection-of-beauty-rembrandt-belsazar: The Babylonian king recoils as a glowing Hebrew inscription, MENE MENE TEKEL UPHARSIN, appears on the wall during his blasphemous feast. Rembrandt stages the moment with theatrical lighting, overturned vessels, and a sumptuous gold-embroidered cloak.
collection-of-beauty-rembrandt-susanna-a: The chaste Susanna shrinks as the two elders intrude upon her bath, drawn from the Book of Daniel. Rembrandt focuses on her vulnerability and the elders' menace, modeling flesh and drapery with a soft, glowing chiaroscuro.
collection-of-beauty-rembrandt-the-abduc: Europa is borne away across the water on the back of Jupiter disguised as a white bull, while her companions react in alarm on the shore. The dark, wooded riverbank and tiny carriage on the bridge give the Ovidian subject the weight of a Dutch landscape.
Rembrandt reimagines the classical myth as a startled, urinating infant snatched skyward by Jupiter's eagle. The unsettling realism breaks sharply with the idealized Italian tradition of the subject.
collection-of-beauty-rembrandt-the-anato: A group portrait of the Amsterdam Surgeons' Guild gathered around the dissected forearm of an executed criminal. Tulp's demonstration of the flexor tendons and the differentiated reactions of his colleagues turn a guild commission into a study of attention.
collection-of-beauty-rembrandt-the-windm: An etching of a stoutly built post mill rising above the bastion of an old town wall. Rembrandt's careful drypoint touches give the brickwork, thatch, and surrounding figures a grainy luminosity prized by collectors of his prints.
collection-of-beauty-rembrandt-zelfportr: A self-portrait from the artist's later years, in which he sits frontally with a mahlstick and palette implied by his pose. Broad, loaded brushwork records a lined face that meets the viewer with sober self-knowledge.
collection-of-beauty-rembrandt-christ-in: The disciples struggle with sail and rigging on a heaving boat as Christ is roused to calm the sea. Rembrandt's only known seascape, it was stolen from the Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum in 1990 and remains missing.
collection-of-beauty-rembrandt-christus-: An etching, in its later state, of Pilate presenting the bound Christ to a crowd that demands his crucifixion. Rembrandt scraped away the foreground onlookers and replaced them with shadowed arches, intensifying the confrontation between judge and viewer.
collection-of-beauty-rembrandt-girl-in-a: A young woman leans out of a painted frame, her hands resting on the illusory ledge in a sly play on the picture's own boundary. The trompe l'oeil device reflects Rembrandt's interest in animating the threshold between image and viewer.
collection-of-beauty-rembrandt-harmensz-: The apostle holds the knife of his martyrdom and turns aside in pensive reflection. Painted late in Rembrandt's career, the figure is built from rough, palpable strokes that give the saint the weathered weight of a real man.

Long known as The Jewish Bride, the painting shows a couple in tender embrace, often identified with Old Testament pairs such as Isaac and Rebecca. Built up with thickly trowelled pigment, the gold sleeve and ruby skirt are among Rembrandt's most celebrated passages of paint.
The evangelist pauses over his manuscript as an angel leans in to whisper at his ear. Rembrandt models both faces with a quiet warmth, treating divine inspiration as an intimate exchange.
collection-of-beauty-rembrandt-the-artis: A small early panel showing a painter, dwarfed by an enormous panel on his easel, contemplating his work from across a bare studio. The image foregrounds thought rather than craft, framing painting as a fundamentally mental activity.
collection-of-beauty-rembrandt-winterlan: A rare winter landscape in oil, with skaters, a horse-drawn sledge, and low farm buildings beneath a wide pale sky. The thinly brushed surface and silvery palette stand apart from the artist's more familiar dark interiors.
collection-of-beauty-rembrandt-van-rijn-: An early tronie, or character study, of a bearded old man in a fur cap, painted in Leiden. Rembrandt explores the fall of light across creased skin and the shaggy textures of the cap as exercises in expressive description.

A late self-portrait in which the painter sits with hands folded, dressed in a heavy gold-trimmed robe and dark cap. The frank, unflattering record of his own ageing has come to define how subsequent centuries imagine the artist.

A windmill stands silhouetted against a turbulent, luminous sky on a high earthen rampart above a quiet river. Long read as a brooding emblem of Dutch identity, the painting fuses topographical record with charged atmosphere.

An early Leiden self-portrait in which the young Rembrandt's face is half-lost in shadow beneath a tousled crown of hair. He scratches into the wet paint with the butt of the brush to suggest stray locks, an experimental touch typical of these years.
collection-of-beauty-renoir-pierre-augus: A bustling Sunday afternoon at a Montmartre dance garden, where dappled sunlight filters through the trees onto crowds of working-class Parisians. Renoir's loose, broken brushwork captures shifting light and lively conversation, treating modern leisure as a worthy subject for monumental painting.
collection-of-beauty-renoir-jeune-fille-: An intimate domestic scene of a young woman combing her hair, a recurring subject in Renoir's later years. The soft, feathery handling of flesh and fabric reflects his shift away from strict Impressionist plein-air practice toward a warmer, more classicizing manner influenced by Renaissance art.
collection-of-beauty-renoirgarden: A woman shaded by a parasol stands amid blossoming flowerbeds, her figure dissolving into the surrounding foliage. Renoir uses fragmented, comma-like brushstrokes and unmodulated touches of pure colour to fuse figure and garden into a single shimmering field of sunlight.
collection-of-beauty-repin-studying-exam: An early academic study from Repin's student years, depicting young men absorbed in their books before an examination. The careful tonal modelling and observed gestures already show the eye for everyday human drama that would shape his later realist canvases.
collection-of-beauty-repin-turf-bench: A relaxed open-air group portrait of Repin's wife, daughters, and in-laws gathered on a turf bench in the family garden. Painted during his summers in Krasnoye Selo, it shows him adapting Russian realism to a freer, sun-warmed plein-air idiom.
collection-of-beauty-reply-of-the-zaporo: A preparatory oil sketch for Repin's celebrated history painting, showing Cossacks erupting in raucous laughter as they dictate an obscene reply to the Ottoman sultan. The looser handling here preserves the immediacy and unbridled humour that the finished canvas would carry forward.
collection-of-beauty-retrato-de-juan-par: A portrait of Velázquez's enslaved assistant, an artist of Moorish descent whom the painter would soon free. Painted in Rome as a demonstration piece, it stunned contemporaries with its dignified presence, controlled silvery palette, and the assured economy of its brushwork.
collection-of-beauty-retrato-del-papa-in: A famously unflinching state portrait of the pope, whose shrewd, suspicious gaze emerges from a cascade of crimson and white. Velázquez orchestrates a near-monochrome of vermilions and rose tones, using fluid brushwork to render lace, silk and satin with startling immediacy.
collection-of-beauty-reverend-robert-wal: A Church of Scotland minister glides across a frozen Edinburgh loch in elegant profile, arms folded against the chill. The silhouetted black figure on pale ice and misty hills became an emblem of the Scottish Enlightenment and a touchstone of Scottish national painting.
collection-of-beauty-reception-du-grand-: Louis XIV welcomes the Prince de Condé on the grand staircase at Versailles, the victorious general bowing as courtiers look on. Gérôme reconstructs the seventeenth-century scene with archaeological precision, marshalling marble, brocade and ceremonial hierarchy in the polished academic style he made famous.
collection-of-beauty-robert-delaunay-191: A monumental synthesis combining the three Graces, the Seine and the Eiffel Tower into a single panorama of modern Paris. Delaunay fragments the city into faceted Cubist planes while introducing the prismatic colour rhythms that would define his Orphist style.
collection-of-beauty-robert-delaunay-der: An early treatment of the iron tower that would obsess Delaunay for decades, viewed from below and toppling against a turbulent sky. The fractured planes and sharp diagonals translate Cubist analysis into a celebration of modern engineering.
collection-of-beauty-robert-delaunay-eif: A later, lyrical revisiting of the Eiffel Tower set against the green expanse of the Champ de Mars. By the 1920s Delaunay had softened his Cubist fragmentation into rhythmic curves and bright contrasting colour, integrating the tower into a holiday vision of Paris.

Painted at the height of Delaunay's Cubist phase, the tower lurches and splinters as if seen simultaneously from multiple vantage points. Surrounding buildings collapse inward, dramatising the giddy modern experience of urban scale.
collection-of-beauty-robert-delaunay-l-h: A pre-Cubist portrait of fellow painter Jean Metzinger, holding a single tulip against a vivid mosaic of pure colour. The divisionist touch and saturated complementaries reveal Delaunay's debt to Neo-Impressionism on the eve of his turn to abstraction.
collection-of-beauty-rocks-at-mouthier-b: A close-up view of weathered limestone cliffs near the artist's native Franche-Comté, the river snaking through a narrow valley below. Courbet builds the rock face with palette-knife slabs of paint, asserting landscape as raw geological matter rather than picturesque scenery.
collection-of-beauty-rogier-van-der-weyd: A stark devotional Crucifixion in which the swooning Virgin and grieving Saint John flank the cross against a flat red cloth and grey wall. Rogier strips the scene of landscape and narrative incident, concentrating the drama into sculptural silhouettes and restrained, almost monochrome colour.

A Netherlandish bust portrait of a young woman whose elaborate winged linen headdress frames a composed, downcast face. Rogier's precise contours, translucent skin and patient observation of folded cloth set the model for fifteenth-century Burgundian portraiture.
collection-of-beauty-romaine-lacaux-by-p: A poised portrait of a Parisian ceramic-maker's young daughter, seated in a stiff white dress with a flower in her hand. Among Renoir's earliest signed works, it still carries the smooth finish of academic portraiture but already shows his sensitivity to reflected light on translucent fabric.
collection-of-beauty-roman-charity-piete: An interpretation of the antique tale of Pero, who secretly nurses her imprisoned father Cimon to keep him alive. Rubens treats the moralising subject with characteristic warmth of flesh, dramatic chiaroscuro and a tender, tightly compressed embrace.
collection-of-beauty-rome-from-mount-ave: A panoramic view of the Eternal City bathed in golden Mediterranean haze, with the Tiber winding past domes and ruins toward distant hills. Turner exhibited this canvas after his Italian travels, dissolving topographical detail into atmospheric light worthy of Claude Lorrain.
collection-of-beauty-roses-vincent-van-g: A dense bouquet of pale pink roses tumbling from a green vase, painted at the asylum of Saint-Rémy weeks before the artist left for Auvers. Van Gogh's swirling impasto and rhythmic brushstrokes turn a quiet still life into a study of vitality and renewal.
collection-of-beauty-rubens-peter-paul-m: A violent biblical tableau of soldiers wrenching infants from their mothers in a tangle of straining muscle and contorted limbs. Rubens compresses the figures into a writhing pyramid, drawing on his Italian study of Michelangelo, Caravaggio and antique sculpture.
collection-of-beauty-rubens-peter-paul-t: The mythological trio stand intertwined in a wooded glade, their pearly flesh set against deep green foliage. Rubens models the women on his second wife Helena Fourment, celebrating an unidealised, sensuous standard of female beauty.
collection-of-beauty-rubens-de-verenigin: An allegorical pairing of Cybele and Neptune, personifying earth and water joining hands in a fertile alliance. Painted during Rubens's diplomatic years, the picture has been read as a plea for free access to the sea for his blockaded native Antwerp.
collection-of-beauty-rubens-feast-of-sim: Christ reclines at the Pharisee's table while the kneeling Mary Magdalene bathes his feet, scandalising the assembled guests. Rubens orchestrates the Gospel scene as Baroque theatre, with surging diagonals, gleaming silver and vivid emotional reaction.
collection-of-beauty-rubens-landscape-wi: A panoramic Flemish countryside after a storm, peasants and cattle moving among glistening fields beneath a translucent rainbow. Painted on his country estate at Het Steen, it shows Rubens turning to landscape in his late years with broad atmospheric breadth.
collection-of-beauty-rubens-der-gefessel: Prometheus writhes in chains as Zeus's eagle tears at his liver, his body cascading head-down across the canvas. Rubens collaborated with the animal specialist Frans Snyders on the predator, fusing classical myth with anatomical violence in a thoroughly Baroque key.
collection-of-beauty-rubens-mars-et-rhea: The war god looms over the Vestal Rhea Silvia, the union that will produce Romulus and Remus and so found Rome. Rubens stages the encounter with cascading drapery, fluttering putti and the warm flesh-tones that mark his mythological inventions.
collection-of-beauty-rubens-portrait-of-: A bust-length portrait of a fair-haired sitter, her hair coiled into intricate braids above a richly trimmed bodice. The luminous skin, lively gaze and confident handling are typical of the workshop portraits Rubens produced for Antwerp's patrician class.
collection-of-beauty-rubens-rkdimages-27: An oil sketch of the Crucifixion, with Christ flanked by the two thieves on Calvary. Rubens uses dynamic diagonals and contorted, muscular bodies to charge the scene with Counter-Reformation pathos, the loose brushwork typical of his preparatory modelli.

A tronie-style head study of a youth in three-quarter profile, executed from life as workshop reference. The lively flesh tones and sketchy modeling show how Rubens accumulated a vocabulary of expressions and types for use in larger narrative compositions.
collection-of-beauty-rubens-medusa: The severed head of the Gorgon lies on a stony ledge, eyes still rolling and snakes writhing into a teeming brood of vipers, scorpions and lizards. Rubens collaborated with the animal specialist Frans Snyders on the reptilian still life, fusing Baroque horror with naturalist precision.
collection-of-beauty-rubens-painting-ada: An early treatment of the Fall of Man, with Eve offering the apple beneath the Tree of Knowledge as the serpent coils above. Painted in Antwerp before Rubens's Italian sojourn, the panel still shows the linear, Northern manner he would soon temper with Venetian color.
collection-of-beauty-rubens-pieter-paul-: Part of the twenty-four-canvas Marie de' Medici cycle commissioned for the Luxembourg Palace, this scene stages the queen's 1610 coronation at Saint-Denis. Rubens orchestrates a vast crowd of dignitaries in a sweeping diagonal, mythologizing the event the day before Henri IV's assassination.
collection-of-beauty-rubens-venus-at-a-m: The goddess sits at her toilette, gazing at her reflection while an attendant arranges her hair and a putto holds the mirror. Rubens reworks the Venetian theme of Titian and Veronese into an opulent meditation on beauty and self-regard, the pearly flesh playing against dark drapery.
collection-of-beauty-rubens-and-snyders-: The Achaean general Philopoemen, mistaken for a servant, is recognized by his hosts while chopping wood, an exemplum of stoic humility from Plutarch. The lavish kitchen still life of game and produce is by Frans Snyders, a frequent collaborator, while Rubens supplied the figures.
collection-of-beauty-rubens-apostel-andr: From the Twelve Apostles series painted for the Duke of Lerma, Andrew is identified by the X-shaped saltire cross of his martyrdom. Rubens characterizes the fisherman-evangelist as a weather-beaten elder, modelled with the bold chiaroscuro he absorbed in Italy.
collection-of-beauty-rubens-apostel-paul: Paul appears as a forceful, balding philosopher gripping the sword of his beheading, in keeping with the Pauline iconography of the Counter-Reformation. The half-length panel belongs to the Apostolado painted in Spain for the Duke of Lerma's collection.
collection-of-beauty-rubens-apostel-simo: Simon the Zealot stands holding the long saw of his legendary martyrdom, set against a plain neutral ground that throws the figure into relief. The panel belongs to Rubens's early Twelve Apostles cycle made for the Duke of Lerma during his time in Spain.
collection-of-beauty-rubens-peace-war: Painted in London for Charles I as a diplomatic gift, the allegory shows Minerva pushing back the war-god Mars while Pax nurses Plutus and children gather around the fruits of peace. The picture distilled Rubens's hopes for an Anglo-Spanish accord into a sumptuous tableau of bodies and abundance.
collection-of-beauty-rubens-saturn: The aged god Saturn devours one of his infant sons, biting into the flesh as the child writhes in his grip beneath the cold light of his eponymous planet. Painted for Philip IV's Torre de la Parada, Rubens fuses Ovidian myth with unflinching late-Baroque violence.
collection-of-beauty-sailing-boats-after: From the Inland Sea series of woodblock prints, this view shows fishing junks under afternoon light. Yoshida printed the same key block in successive variants from morning to night, a shin-hanga experiment in atmosphere achieved by altering only the inking and color blocks.
collection-of-beauty-sailing-boats-eveni: Junks ride a glassy sea under a low evening sun in this version of Yoshida's six-state Sailing Boats suite. The warm orange ground and silhouetted sails show how shin-hanga printers could transform a single composition through color alone.
collection-of-beauty-sailing-boats-mist-: Sailing junks drift in a pale, fog-bound sea, their masts dissolving into the haze. One of six time-of-day variants printed from a single key block, this state demonstrates Yoshida's command of bokashi gradations and his shin-hanga sensitivity to weather and air.
collection-of-beauty-sailing-boats-morni: Boats glide on still water under the cool tones of dawn, the mountains of the Seto Inland Sea faint on the horizon. Yoshida printed the work in six time-of-day variations, a virtuoso shin-hanga study in the chromatic rendering of light.
collection-of-beauty-sailing-boats-night: The darkest of Yoshida's six Sailing Boats variants, the scene is steeped in deep indigo with the silhouettes of junks just visible against a moonlit sea. The print shows shin-hanga craftsmen working color and gradation to evoke night without losing the woodblock's clarity.
collection-of-beauty-saint-francis-of-as: The saint swoons in the arms of an angel after receiving the stigmata, his companions sleeping in the moonlit landscape behind. One of Caravaggio's earliest religious nocturnes, the picture introduces the raking light and tender naturalism that would shape his mature style.
collection-of-beauty-saint-jerome-writin: The aged Church Father bends over an open volume, his bald skull echoing the memento mori at his elbow. Painted in Rome for Cardinal Scipione Borghese, Caravaggio strips the scholar's cell to a few essentials so that flesh, parchment and shadow can do all the speaking.
collection-of-beauty-saint-sebastian-ten: After the Roman martyr's ordeal at the column, two angels withdraw the arrows and bind his wounds. Rubens treats Sebastian's nude body in the heroic manner of antique sculpture and Italian masters, softening the violence into a Baroque tableau of muscular grace.
collection-of-beauty-san-romano-battle-p: From the three-panel cycle painted for the Bartolini Salimbeni and later Lorenzo de' Medici, this London panel shows Niccolo da Tolentino leading the Florentines against Siena. Uccello's foreshortened lances, fallen soldier and patterned ground are a famous early experiment in linear perspective.
collection-of-beauty-sandro-botticelli-a: A late workshop Madonna in the Florentine devotional tradition, the Virgin tenderly cradling the Christ Child against a quiet ground. The flowing contours and elongated hands are characteristic of Botticelli's late manner, when his style turned more austere under Savonarolan influence.
collection-of-beauty-sandro-botticelli-i: A profile bust of a young woman with elaborately plaited hair, long identified, however speculatively, with the Florentine beauty Simonetta Vespucci. Botticelli converts a Quattrocento portrait formula into a half-mythological nymph, the linear refinement balanced by jewels and serpentine ornament.
collection-of-beauty-sandro-botticelli-l: The newborn goddess arrives at Cyprus on a scallop shell, blown ashore by Zephyr while a Hora prepares to robe her. Painted on canvas for a Medici villa and indebted to the antique Venus Pudica, the image fuses Neoplatonic philosophy with Botticelli's hallmark linear grace.

The dead Christ is supported across the Virgin's lap as Mary Magdalene clasps his feet and saints crowd the dark mouth of the tomb. Botticelli's late Lamentations, painted in the wake of Savonarola's preaching, abandon courtly sweetness for compressed, anguished figures.
collection-of-beauty-sandro-botticelli-r: The Hebrew heroine strides home with her maidservant after slaying Holofernes, the general's severed head balanced in a basket on the older woman's head. The small Uffizi panel pairs Old Testament tyrannicide with Botticelli's lyrical, wind-swept drapery.
collection-of-beauty-sandro-botticelli-0: A late Lamentation showing the dead Christ across the Virgin's knees, ringed by mourners in a steeply compressed group. Botticelli's tightly knotted figures and stark grief reflect the religious severity that gripped Florence in the Savonarola years.

Saint Augustine sits in his study, deep in thought before book, inkwell and astronomical instruments. Botticelli's fresco for the Florentine church of Ognissanti casts the Church Father as a Renaissance scholar, painted as a pendant to Ghirlandaio's Saint Jerome.
collection-of-beauty-sandro-botticelli-l: Botticelli reconstructs a lost painting by the Greek master Apelles as described by Lucian: King Midas with ass's ears is approached by Slander, dragged forward by a sinister train of Envy, Deceit and Conspiracy, while naked Truth points to the heavens. The panel is a humanist defense of innocence against false accusation.
collection-of-beauty-sanson-matando-al-l: Samson grapples a roaring lion to the ground and tears its jaws apart, the biblical strongman rendered with anatomical force and twisting torso. Rubens reworked the subject several times, transposing the heroic male nude of antique sculpture into a violent Old Testament exemplum.
collection-of-beauty-sargent-group-with-: Four figures recline on a grassy slope beneath parasols, their forms half-dissolved in dappled shade. Sargent treats the bourgeois leisure scene with rapid, broken brushwork, prizing the play of filtered light over portrait specificity.
collection-of-beauty-sargent-life-study-: A standing nude rendered with academic precision during Sargent's travels in North Africa, the body modeled in warm earth tones against a neutral ground. The study reflects his sustained interest in the figure outside commissioned portraiture.
collection-of-beauty-sargent-madame-ramo: The Chilean diplomat's wife is shown seated at a piano in an interior of pale silvery tones, her loose white dress catching the light. The composition belongs to Sargent's early Parisian period and was admired at the 1881 Salon.
collection-of-beauty-sargent-monetpainti: Sargent depicts his friend Monet at work outdoors at Giverny, easel propped beside a thicket as a young woman reads nearby. The picture is a personal homage from one painter to another, executed with the very plein-air looseness it documents.
collection-of-beauty-scene-des-massacres: A monumental Romantic response to the Ottoman massacre of Greeks on the island of Chios, exhibited at the 1824 Salon. Delacroix piles broken bodies and dazed survivors across a parched foreground, the distant smoking landscape echoing the philhellenic outrage of the moment.
collection-of-beauty-schongauer-anthony: An engraving in which the hermit saint is hoisted into the air and tormented by a swarm of bristling, scaled, and beaked demons. Schongauer's burin work, with its dense parallel hatching and inventive monstrous anatomy, exerted a famous influence on the young Dürer.
collection-of-beauty-scops-owl-cherry-bl: A small owl perches on a flowering cherry branch silhouetted against a full moon, in the kachō-e bird-and-flower tradition. Koson's woodblock combines soft tonal printing with a quiet, almost photographic stillness aimed at early-twentieth-century export markets.
collection-of-beauty-sebastiano-ricci-it: The huntress goddess pauses at rest, half-draped, with her hound pressed against her side and quiver beside her. Ricci handles the mythological subject with the airy palette and rococo grace that bridge late Venetian Baroque and the eighteenth century.
collection-of-beauty-sebastiano-ricci-ve: A reclining Venus, attended by putti, is approached by a leering satyr in a confection of soft pinks, blues, and silver greys. Ricci's eroticized mythology helped translate the Venetian tradition of Veronese into a lighter rococo idiom.
collection-of-beauty-selbstportrat-by-al: Dürer presents himself in courtly Italianate dress with striped cap, gloves, and a window onto Alpine peaks at his shoulder. The painting is an early proclamation of the Northern artist as gentleman-humanist, painted shortly after his first Italian journey.
collection-of-beauty-self-portrait-as-th: Gentileschi merges self-portrait and emblem, depicting herself as the personification of Painting from Ripa's Iconologia: brush raised, gold chain bearing a mask, sleeves pushed back over a working arm. The conceit could only be staged by a woman, since the allegory was female.
collection-of-beauty-self-portrait-by-sa: The Berlin painter regards the viewer directly in a sober, naturalistic likeness keyed to muted browns and ochres. Lepsius trained in Berlin and Paris and worked within the cultivated portraitist circles of late nineteenth-century Wilhelmine society.
collection-of-beauty-self-portrait-in-a-: The artist holds palette and brushes beneath a wide plumed straw hat, smiling outward in a pose adapted from Rubens's Chapeau de Paille. Vigée Le Brun cast herself as both fashionable subject and confident professional, a self-promotional image she repeated.

The artist holds palette and brushes beneath a wide plumed straw hat, smiling outward in a pose adapted from Rubens's Chapeau de Paille. Vigée Le Brun cast herself as both fashionable subject and confident professional, a self-promotional image she repeated.
collection-of-beauty-self-portrait-with-: The painter is shown embracing her young daughter Julie in a Neoclassical interior, both wearing simple white chemise dresses. The intimate, sentimental staging was calculated to project natural maternal feeling for a Salon audience on the eve of revolution.
collection-of-beauty-sesshushuutoutou: A pair of seasonal landscapes in ink on paper by the Muromachi-period Zen monk-painter, with sheer rock faces, dwellings, and a solitary traveler picking his way through the cold. Sesshū's broken, axe-cut brushwork adapts Chinese Song models into a distinctly Japanese austerity.
collection-of-beauty-seven-sacraments-ro: A triptych setting the seven sacraments inside a vast Gothic church, with the Crucifixion at the center of the nave and the rites unfolding in the aisles. Van der Weyden orchestrates devotional precision, contemporary Netherlandish architecture, and an extraordinary system of inscribed banderoles.
collection-of-beauty-sharaku-1794-ebisu: An ukiyo-e print issued during Sharaku's brief, intense ten-month career, depicting a kabuki actor in the role of the smiling fisherman-deity Ebisu. The mica-grounded portrait style condenses a stage character into bold contour and exaggerated expression.
collection-of-beauty-shipwreck-turner: A naval transport founders amid towering green-grey waves, lifeboats overloaded with survivors swept up the swells. Turner makes the wreck of HMS Minotaur an early demonstration of his lifelong subject of nature's indifferent force at sea.
collection-of-beauty-shironeko-by-takaha: A white cat is shown in profile against a plain ground, executed in the shin-hanga revival of woodblock printing. Shōtei's subtle gradations of grey in the fur and quiet, almost domestic mood typify the movement's blend of tradition and Western naturalism.
collection-of-beauty-siernetel-in-bloemp: A potted coleus painted in Paris during Van Gogh's transition from the dark Dutch palette toward brighter, more broken color. The everyday flowerpot becomes an exercise in observing complementary reds and greens at close range.
collection-of-beauty-sinite-parvulos-a-g: Christ, seated, gathers small children to him while apostles and mothers crowd around, taking up the gospel injunction "Suffer the little children to come unto me." Gentileschi treats the religious subject with naturalistic Caravaggesque lighting and grounded, individualized figures.
collection-of-beauty-sir-anthony-van-dyc: The Stuart king is presented in three-quarter length with elegant hauteur, a pose Van Dyck refined repeatedly to fix the image of Caroline kingship. The painter's silvered palette, fluid drapery, and aristocratic poise shaped English portraiture for a century.
collection-of-beauty-sir-david-wilkie-17: Wilkie commemorates the heroine Agustina de Aragón firing a cannon during the 1808 siege of Zaragoza, painted after his Spanish travels. The smoky, candlelit handling shows him reaching past genre toward the broader Romantic history mode of Géricault and Delacroix.

A crowded Scottish wedding feast unfolds in a low-beamed interior, fiddler at the front and dancers, drinkers, and gossips crammed across the canvas. Wilkie's careful Dutch-inspired genre painting helped popularize sentimental rural Scottishness for a Regency audience.
collection-of-beauty-sir-henry-raeburn-c: The Highland chieftain stands armed in full Glengarry tartan against a heroic landscape, every weapon and badge of clan pride on display. Raeburn's broad, direct brushwork supplies the swagger that Macdonell himself cultivated as a self-styled embodiment of clan tradition.
collection-of-beauty-sir-peter-paul-rube: Peasants, livestock, and farm tools fill a thatched Flemish barn lit by a cold doorway onto a snowy yard. Rubens's late-career rural scenes treat the working countryside around his estate at Het Steen with the same vigorous handling he gave to mythological subjects.

Milkmaids tend cattle amid baskets of fruit and vegetables in a fertile Brabant landscape, a cornucopia of rural abundance. Rubens fuses pastoral genre and landscape with sweeping diagonal composition and an almost mythological sense of fertility.
collection-of-beauty-slave-ship: A slaver flings the dead and dying from its hold as a typhoon rises over a blood-orange sea, painted in response to the Zong massacre and abolitionist debate. Turner dissolves moral horror into dazzling atmosphere, the figures and chains barely surfacing through churning paint.
collection-of-beauty-slovane-v-pravlasti: The opening canvas of Mucha's Slav Epic shows a couple cowering in a moonlit field while spectral horsemen ride overhead, embodying the early sufferings of the Slavic peoples. Mucha's tempera-and-oil technique on huge canvas gives the symbolist scene a pale, dreamlike pallor distinct from his Art Nouveau posters.
collection-of-beauty-smalllakewithboatmu: An early oil sketch on cardboard from Munch's teenage years, depicting a still pond with a moored rowboat. The plein-air naturalism, painted before his Symbolist breakthrough, shows him absorbing Norwegian landscape conventions in compact, atmospheric form.
collection-of-beauty-sokokura-by-hiroshi: A woodblock view of Sokokura, one of the seven hot springs of Hakone, with bathers and travelers gathered amid the steep wooded gorge. The print belongs to Hiroshige's series Hakone shichiyu zue, devoted to the spa villages prized by Edo-period travelers along the Tōkaidō.
collection-of-beauty-song-of-the-lark-wi: A young farmer pauses with his scythe at twilight, head tilted toward the unseen call of a lark. Painted shortly after the Civil War, the work distills Homer's interest in rural American labor and the silent intervals that punctuate it, rendered in a low-keyed palette of dusk.
collection-of-beauty-sophia-alekseyevna-: Repin depicts the deposed regent Sofia Alekseyevna confined at Novodevichy Convent in 1698, her glare turned outward as a hanged Streltsy is glimpsed through the barred window. The picture compresses defeat, fury, and political claustrophobia into a single suffocating cell.
collection-of-beauty-spring-flowers-by-c: Peonies, hydrangeas, and lilacs spill from basket and pot against a darkened ground, painted with an almost botanical attentiveness. An early still life, it shows the young Monet weighing studio precision against the looser, light-driven handling that would soon define his work.
collection-of-beauty-starry-night-over-t: Gaslamps along the Arles quay double in the dark water of the Rhône while the Great Bear hangs overhead. A small couple strolls in the foreground, anchoring Van Gogh's nocturne to the human scale of the river town.
collection-of-beauty-stieler-jane-digby: Painted for Ludwig I's Schönheitengalerie, the portrait shows the English aristocrat at twenty-four, before the marriages and travels that would carry her from Munich to a Bedouin tent near Damascus. Stieler's smooth, neoclassical finish lends the sitter the air of court decorum she would soon scandalously discard.
collection-of-beauty-stieler-alexandra-p: A Schönheitengalerie portrait of Princess Alexandra of Bavaria, daughter of Ludwig I, rendered in Stieler's characteristic porcelain finish. The sitter is shown with the soft idealization the Munich court demanded of its gallery of beauties.
collection-of-beauty-stieler-lola-montez: The Irish-born dancer who styled herself a Spanish noblewoman, painted at the height of her hold over Ludwig I of Bavaria. Her place in the Schönheitengalerie was itself a small political event, the affair contributing to the king's abdication the following year.
collection-of-beauty-stieler-portrait-of: Countess Caroline von Holnstein, a Munich society figure, painted for Ludwig I's Schönheitengalerie. Stieler frames her in dark drapery and pearls, the smooth modeling and steady gaze typical of the court's gallery of beauties.

A late entry in the Schönheitengalerie, depicting the Bavarian baroness with the soft chiaroscuro and idealized features Stieler used throughout the cycle. The portrait belongs to the long Munich project that gathered thirty-six women from across the social spectrum at Ludwig I's command.
collection-of-beauty-stra-e-mit-kirche-i: A village street in the Black Foothills opens toward the parish church, the planes of road, façade, and sky reduced to firm coloured patches. Painted during Macke's Kandern period, the scene shows him absorbing Cézanne and the early Blaue Reiter palette before his shift to fully saturated colour.
collection-of-beauty-stuck-franz-von-sal: Franz von Stuck stages Salome in dark, lurid Symbolism: the dancer raised in ecstasy beneath jewelled veils while a servant hoists the Baptist's head into shadow. The picture distils the Munich Secession's fascination with the femme fatale.
collection-of-beauty-stuckelberg-sappho-: The Swiss painter imagines the Greek poet on a Lesbos cliff, lyre in hand, gazing seaward in the conventional pose of impending suicide. Stückelberg works in the late academic idiom, smoothing antique legend into a moralized salon image.
collection-of-beauty-sumida-river-the-wo: From One Hundred Famous Views of Edo, the print frames the Sumida River through the trees of the Suijin shrine grove at Masaki. Hiroshige uses the steep foreground branches and high horizon characteristic of the late series to fold the viewer into the riverside scene.
collection-of-beauty-sumo-wrestling-toad: Two toads grapple in a sumō ring while companions wait their turn, a gentle parody of the Edo wrestling print. Koson, working in the shin-hanga revival, brings naturalist observation to a comic subject with long roots in Japanese caricature.
collection-of-beauty-sunset-across-the-r: Plate twelve of the Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji shows boatmen and stevedores along the Sumida embankment, the long span of Ryōgoku Bridge cutting the composition while Fuji rises faintly in the dusk. Hokusai keeps the celebrated peak modest in scale, ceding the foreground to working Edo.
collection-of-beauty-sunset-at-montmajou: Painted on the rocky outcrop near Arles, the work surveys the plain of La Crau through a foreground of scrub oak and limestone, the abbey ruins behind. Long doubted as authentic, it was reattributed in 2013 on the basis of letters, pigments, and the painter's own catalogue.
collection-of-beauty-surprised-rousseau: A tiger crouches through whipping grasses as rain slashes a tropical thicket lit by lightning. Rousseau's first jungle picture, it stitched together motifs from botanical gardens and prints into the imagined wilderness that would occupy him for the rest of his life.
collection-of-beauty-susanna-and-the-eld: A late treatment of the Book of Daniel episode, with the bathing Susanna recoiling from the two judges who threaten to denounce her. Gentileschi returned to the subject across her career, here in a more restrained, tenebrist key than her early Pommersfelden version.

Painted when the artist was seventeen, the canvas turns the usual erotic pretext on its head: Susanna twists from the leering elders in unmistakable distress. The closed marble parapet and tightly wound figures give the scene a claustrophobia rare in the subject's long history.
collection-of-beauty-susanna-i-badet-187: The Danish painter takes up the biblical bather in a polished academic idiom learned in Bonnat's Paris atelier. Susanna is set against a shadowed garden wall, the elders pressed close to a single edge of the canvas to sharpen the moment of intrusion.
collection-of-beauty-suzanna-rembrandt-v: Rembrandt's small panel catches Susanna at the moment she steps from her robe, glancing back with sudden alarm as the elders emerge from the dark garden. The pearly nude and deep umber surroundings show the painter testing a Lastman-derived biblical mode in the year of Danaë.
collection-of-beauty-suzuki-harunobu-c-1: From Harunobu's Eight Views of Ōmi, the print transposes the Chinese landscape topos onto Lake Biwa, here the returning sails at Yabase. Slender figures in a domestic interior stand in for the classical scenery, a witty mitate typical of his refined nishiki-e.
collection-of-beauty-suzuki-harunobu-wom: A young woman holds a paper lantern up to a flowering plum branch in the snow, the colour-printed surface tuned to soft greys and blacks. Harunobu was the first ukiyo-e artist to exploit the full nishiki-e palette, and the quiet nocturne is a showcase for it.
collection-of-beauty-tabi-miyage-dai-iss: An autumn road through the old Koshiji region, recorded in the first of Hasui's Souvenirs of Travel series. The shin-hanga print pairs sketchbook directness with Watanabe Shōzaburō's carefully graded colour blocks.

Iwai Beach on the Bōshū coast, fishing boats drawn up under a high cloud bank. Hasui's first travel album returns again and again to such quiet shorelines, the bokashi gradients standing in for shifting maritime weather.

Workmen load quarried stone onto a flat-bottomed boat in Awa Province, the labour rendered with the documentary clarity Hasui brought to early Taishō travel. The print belongs to Souvenirs of Travel I, the series that established his collaboration with publisher Watanabe.

The Asano River at Kanazawa, its banks lined by the dark eaves of the old castle town. Hasui treats the scene in muted dusk colour, balancing the traditional Edo-period view with a modern, photographically framed viewpoint.

A view of the Nagare pleasure quarter in Kanazawa, lanterns warming the wooden façades along a narrow lane. Hasui finds in the licensed district the same hushed melancholy he gave his rural and seaside views.

The Mishima River winding through Mutsu Province in northern Honshū, drawn from sketches Hasui made on his first long tour of the region. Issued by Watanabe in 1919, it stands among the earliest plates of the Souvenirs of Travel series.

A still woodblock view of Tsuta Marsh in the northern province of Mutsu, where reflected trees double the autumn forest on glassy water. The print belongs to Hasui's first Souvenirs of Travel series, in which the artist toured rural Japan recording the country's quieter corners.

A mountain temple outside Sendai is glimpsed through a screen of trees, the architecture half-absorbed into the hillside. Hasui's compact composition and softly graded skies belong to the shin-hanga revival, which reworked classical ukiyo-e idioms with modern atmospheric effects.

Autumn descends on the hot-spring village of Arayu in Shiobara, the slopes blazing with red maple above wooden bathhouses and a narrow river. The print captures the kind of seasonal regional travel that drove the early shin-hanga market.

Yuhi Falls drops between mossy cliffs at Shiobara, rendered with the layered greens and quietly observed water Hasui made his signature. Carved and printed in collaboration with publisher Watanabe Shozaburo, it is part of the first Souvenirs of Travel cycle.

The cliff face of Senjomaku rises from the still surface of Lake Towada, its scale exaggerated by Hasui's high horizon. Carved from successive woodblocks, the print exploits the bokashi gradient technique to soften water into mist.

Pine-fringed Kude Beach in Wakasa Province curves along the Sea of Japan, the foreground trees framing a low strip of water and sky. Hasui's clean geometry and reduced palette draw on Hiroshige's coastal landscapes while pushing toward modern simplification.

Lake Kugushi lies under a still, overcast sky, its shore traced by reeds and a scatter of fishing boats. The composition is typical of Hasui's first travel series, which favored the unspectacular waterways and country roads of provincial Japan.
collection-of-beauty-tabi-miyage-dai-nis: Rain falls in long diagonal strokes across the wooden stage of Kiyomizu-dera in Kyoto, its temple roofs darkened against a washed grey sky. Hasui treats the famed pilgrimage site not as a monument but as an atmospheric encounter with weather.

A quiet stretch of beach in Echigo Province frames moored boats and a low coastline under heavy cloud. Printed for Watanabe Shozaburo, the sheet exemplifies the shin-hanga preference for unremarkable rural views observed at a contemplative distance.

Kosho Temple at Himi nestles among trees on the Etchu coast, its tiled roof anchoring a composition of slope, sea and sky. Hasui's keyblock outlines are softened by carefully overlapping color blocks printed in registered succession.

Bare winter branches stand against the pale Hozu River at Arashiyama outside Kyoto, a district usually depicted in cherry blossom or maple. Hasui takes the inverse season, finding austere geometry where his predecessors found color.

Moonlight silvers the surface of Lake Kamo on Sado Island, the far shore reduced to a dark silhouette. The night scene relies on the bokashi technique, gradient printing across a single block to model the broad sky.

A rough beach hut at Himi sits low against the Etchu shoreline, its dark timbers contrasted with pale sand and water. Hasui's interest is in working coastal architecture rather than scenic landmarks, a recurring trait of the second Souvenirs cycle.

A street view in the Shimohonda neighborhood of Kanazawa shows a low row of merchant houses under a wide, flat sky. The print's restrained palette and elongated horizontal proportion are characteristic of Hasui's urban observation prints.

The Yanagawa district in Kai Province appears as a cluster of farmhouses and rice paddies set against distant mountains. Hasui sketched directly from life on his journeys, then reworked the studies for the carvers and printers of Watanabe's workshop.

Wooden irrigation troughs lift water across a Sado hillside, a practical landscape of rural infrastructure. The print's quiet subject reflects Hasui's commitment to ordinary working scenes over the picturesque set pieces of older ukiyo-e.

Stone lanterns flank the wooded approach to Kasuga Shrine in Nara, a route lined with thousands of votive lights to the Fujiwara tutelary deities. Hasui sets a single human figure within deep vertical trees to dramatize the scale of the precinct.

Nigatsu-do, the hall on the eastern slope of Todai-ji where the Omizutori water-drawing rite is held each spring, is shown perched above its veranda balustrade. Hasui's diagonal viewpoint compresses the wooden gallery into a strong horizontal band.

Morning along Osaka's Dotonbori canal finds the famed entertainment district at its quietest, the theater district's lanterns unlit and its banks emptied of crowds. The composition trades nightlife spectacle for a city caught off-stage.

The mining town of Aikawa on Sado climbs a hillside in tightly packed wooden houses, each roof outlined by Hasui's keyblock. Once the seat of the Tokugawa silver and gold mines, the settlement appears here as a quiet provincial village.

The village of Kamo on Sado Island is laid out along the lake shore, low farmhouses set among rice fields. Hasui composes a near-empty middle ground that lets the water and ridge of the island do most of the visual work.

Mano Bay opens broadly on the western coast of Sado, its beach and headlands rendered in Hasui's typical low-saturation greens and blues. The print is part of an extended Sado suite within the second Souvenirs series.

A steep road climbs the Nishimikawa slope on Sado Island, its gradient cutting between dense pines. Hasui chooses an everyday traveler's vantage rather than a famous-place view, in keeping with the modest tone of the series.

The harbor of Ogi at the southern tip of Sado Island holds a cluster of fishing boats below sheltering hills. Hasui's composition layers sea, vessel and shore into clearly delimited bands of overlapping flat color.

Takamatsu Castle in Sanuki Province, one of the few sea-fed castles in Japan, is glimpsed across its tide-fed moat. Hasui's view registers the rebuilt keep and surrounding stone embankments under a softly graded shin-hanga sky.

The fishing town of Miyazu in Tango Province, near the spit of Amanohashidate, opens onto a calm bay edged by mountains. Hasui's restrained palette of slate blues and greens is tuned to the overcast light of the Sea of Japan coast.

Night rain falls heavily on the port of Teradomari, lantern light scattering across wet ground. Hasui was a master of nocturnes, and the sheet uses successive overprintings to build a saturated, almost lacquered darkness.

Dawn snow lies thick over the harbor at Ogi on Sado, with moored boats reduced to silhouettes against pale water. The print pairs a graded pink horizon with cold blue shadows, a daybreak chord Hasui returned to throughout his career.

Snow blankets the pine spit of Amanohashidate, the narrow sandbar in Tango Bay long counted among the Three Views of Japan. Hasui strips its familiar profile back to a quiet white horizontal between sea and sky.

A clear winter day at Miyajima sees fresh snow lining the rooftops of Itsukushima and the shore of the Inland Sea, the famous floating torii absent from this oblique view. Hasui exploits the contrast of warm vermilion architecture against cold white slopes.

A woodblock print from the Souvenirs of Travel II series depicts the Asahi Bridge spanning the river at Ojiya. Hasui's shin-hanga style fuses Western perspective with traditional Japanese block-printing, building atmosphere through layered bokashi gradations.

A partial view of the Phoenix Hall at Byodo-in in Uji, rendered as a shin-hanga woodblock print. Hasui isolates a fragment of the eleventh-century temple's tiled roofline, using flat colour fields and quiet tonal washes characteristic of the Souvenirs of Travel II series.
collection-of-beauty-tabi-miyage-dai-san: A coastal view of Futomi in Awa Province from the Souvenirs of Travel III series. Hasui captures the meeting of village rooftops and sea through carefully calibrated woodblock gradations, a hallmark of the shin-hanga revival of landscape printing.

A panoramic alpine vista showing Asahi Peak as seen from Mt. Hakuba in the Northern Japanese Alps. Hasui's woodblock layers translate distant snow ridges and atmospheric haze into broad fields of muted colour, typical of his shin-hanga landscapes.

A traveller's view along the Nakayama-Shichiri road threading through the mountains of Hida Province. The print belongs to Hasui's Souvenirs of Travel III, in which woodblock bokashi gradations evoke the changing light of remote inland routes.

A nocturnal scene at the Arifuku hot springs in Iwami Province, with lantern-lit inns reflected on damp streets. Hasui exploits the deep, saturated indigos that became a signature of his nocturnes within the shin-hanga movement.

A coastal view of Hinomisaki on the Izumo peninsula, where pine-clad cliffs meet the Sea of Japan. Hasui's woodblock translates wind, wave and weather into a few decisive colour blocks, a characteristic of his Souvenirs of Travel III landscapes.

A hazy moon hangs over the canals of Matsue in Izumo Province, the city's wooden buildings dissolving into a violet dusk. Hasui's bokashi shading delivers the title's oborozuki — the misted moon of classical Japanese poetry.

Fishing boats and tile-roofed houses cluster along the harbour at Mihogaseki on the Shimane peninsula. The shin-hanga print balances closely observed coastal architecture against the broad calm of the bay.

An autumn rainbow arcs above rice fields at Hatta in Kaga Province. Hasui uses overlapping bokashi gradations to render the prismatic band — a meteorological subject that had become a quiet specialty of his shin-hanga landscapes.

The five timber arches of the Kintai Bridge in Suo Province span the Nishiki River, a famed seventeenth-century engineering feat. Hasui presents the structure in shin-hanga woodblock, foregrounding its rhythmic silhouette against riverbank pines.

A view of the Gozanoishi Shrine on the wooded shore of Lake Tazawa, Japan's deepest lake. The print uses Hasui's signature still-water reflections and bokashi-shaded skies to anchor the small red torii within an expansive landscape.
collection-of-beauty-takiyasha-the-witch: A nishiki-e triptych staging the sorceress Takiyasha as she summons a colossal skeleton against the warrior Mitsukuni in the ruined Soma palace. Kuniyoshi compresses three full sheets into one continuous, theatrically lit scene drawn from the gothic literature of late Edo.
collection-of-beauty-tamakatzura-tamator: The ama diver Tamatori clutches the Dragon King's pearl as sea creatures swarm in pursuit. Kuniyoshi treats the legendary recovery dive as a swirling, kinetic composition typical of his late ukiyo-e narrative prints.
collection-of-beauty-tamatori-being-purs: A second telling of the Tamatori legend, with the diver fleeing the underwater palace as a dragon and its attendants give chase. Kuniyoshi exploits the woodblock medium's capacity for sweeping diagonals and undulating tendrils of marine life.
collection-of-beauty-tempelskatten-peter: Despite the catalogue title, the panel records a bustling Flemish ferry crossing to Antwerp, packed with passengers, cargo and livestock. Jordaens animates the scene with the boisterous, low-life realism that anchored his early Antwerp output.
collection-of-beauty-tentaciones-de-cris: A fresco from the south wall of the Sistine Chapel staging all three temptations of Christ around a centrally placed temple based on Rome's Hospital of Santo Spirito. Botticelli folds the gospel narrative into a contemporary Roman setting alive with allegorical figures and Medici allusions.
collection-of-beauty-the-moreno-garden-a: Painted during Monet's 1884 stay on the Italian Riviera, the canvas captures the orange-and-olive grove of the Moreno estate at Bordighera. Tangled trunks and a brilliant Mediterranean palette mark the artist's response to a southern light that he likened to fairyland.
collection-of-beauty-the-antiques-seller: A genre interior in oil on panel showing an antiques dealer surrounded by carpets, ceramics and weaponry from the Ottoman world. Weisse, a Czech-born painter active in Paris, specialised in such Orientalist bazaar scenes detailed with cabinet-picture precision.
collection-of-beauty-the-artist-s-garden: A corner of Monet's Argenteuil garden ablaze with dahlias, with the painter's house glimpsed beyond a wooden fence. The canvas — long associated with a similar work by Renoir painted alongside Monet — applies broken Impressionist touches to translate the heat of late summer.
collection-of-beauty-the-arts-painting: Part of Mucha's four-panel allegorical suite The Arts, this lithograph personifies Painting as a young woman in profile contemplating a flower at twilight. The slender format, ornamented halo and arabesque border are classic vocabulary of Mucha's Paris-period Art Nouveau.
collection-of-beauty-the-attack-by-veres: A surging Russian infantry charge with fixed bayonets, drawn from Vereshchagin's late series on the 1812 Napoleonic invasion. The painter, a veteran himself, refused heroic abstraction, packing the canvas with the smoke, mud and panic of close-quarters assault.
collection-of-beauty-the-barber-institut: Turner's marine pairs working fishermen and beached fish with a low sun burning through coastal mist. Held by the Barber Institute, the picture exemplifies his early atmospheric naturalism, in which form is dissolved by light and humidity rather than drawn line.
