collection-of-beauty-the-bathing-posts-b: A small panel of bathing markers driven into the Breton sand under a soft, overcast sky. Whistler reduces the seaside to a few horizontal bands of muted colour, in keeping with the tonal restraint and Aestheticist economy of his late oils.
collection-of-beauty-the-beach-at-trouvi: Painted on the Normandy coast during Monet's honeymoon summer, the canvas shows two seated women — likely Camille Monet and a companion — under parasols on the windswept sand. Grains of beach are embedded in the paint, a documentary trace of the open-air method.
collection-of-beauty-the-beheading-of-sa: Painted for the Oratory of San Giovanni in Valletta, where it remains the only canvas Caravaggio signed — in the spilled blood of the saint. The huge, half-lit composition stages the executioner mid-action with Caravaggio's signature tenebrist sobriety.
collection-of-beauty-the-blinding-of-sam: A violent rendering of Judges 16, with Philistine soldiers pinning Samson while a blade is driven into his eye and Delilah flees with the shorn hair. Rembrandt extracts maximal drama from a sudden shaft of daylight cutting into the cave-like darkness.
collection-of-beauty-the-christian-marty: A Roman arena scene in which kneeling Christians pray as lions are released and crucified figures burn against the stadium walls. Gerome combines archaeological detail of the Circus Maximus with the polished academic finish typical of his Salon machines.
collection-of-beauty-the-conspiracy-of-t: The surviving central fragment of Rembrandt's largest history painting, commissioned for Amsterdam's new Town Hall. Civilis raises a sword in a one-eyed oath-taking with his Batavian rebels — a coarsely lit, almost barbaric image that the city soon rejected.
collection-of-beauty-the-creation-of-ada: A fresco at the centre of the Sistine Chapel ceiling, in which God reaches across the void to a languid Adam. Michelangelo compresses the moment of ensoulment into the famously unmet gap between the two extended fingers.
collection-of-beauty-the-emperor-commodu: An engraving from Goltzius's Roman Heroes series, depicting the megalomaniac emperor in the lion-skin and club of Hercules. The muscular low-angle figure and virtuoso swelling burin lines exemplify the technical bravura of Northern Mannerist printmaking.
collection-of-beauty-the-fall-of-man-161: Adam and Eve are shown beneath the Tree of Knowledge with the serpent coiled overhead, their elongated bodies posed in a Mannerist contrapposto. A late work by the Haarlem master, treating the Genesis subject with the smooth idealism of his post-Italian style.
collection-of-beauty-the-famous-scenes-o: A vertical ōban from the Famous Places in the Sixty-odd Provinces series, looking down on the swirling Naruto whirlpools that churn between Awa and Awaji. Hiroshige flattens the sea into concentric arcs of bokashi blue, framed by a steep cliff at left.
collection-of-beauty-the-feast-in-the-ho: A vast Venetian banquet staged in a Palladian loggia of three Serlian arches, populated by guests, servants, dwarves, dogs and a parrot. Originally painted as a Last Supper, the work was retitled after the Inquisition objected to its profane figures.
collection-of-beauty-the-fighting-temera: The 98-gun veteran of Trafalgar is towed by a black steam tug along the Thames toward the breaker's yard, set against a smouldering sunset. Turner contrasts the pale, ghostly hulk with the squat industrial tug, mourning the passing of the age of sail.
collection-of-beauty-the-fuji-from-kanay: An additional view from the Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji, looking across the boulder-strewn Ōi River from the Kanaya post-station toward a snow-capped Fuji. Travellers and porters ford the shallows in the foreground, anchoring the landscape in everyday Tōkaidō life.
collection-of-beauty-the-garden-of-earth: A panoramic triptych running from Eden through a teeming earthly paradise to a nightmarish hell, populated by hybrid creatures, oversized fruits and fantastical machinery. Bosch's moral allegory is rendered in jewel-bright oils on oak.
collection-of-beauty-the-gorge-at-vareng: Painted on the Normandy coast near Dieppe, this canvas looks down through a wooded ravine toward a sliver of the Channel. Monet's broken touches of green, ochre and lilac translate the cliff vegetation into vibrating colour.
collection-of-beauty-the-holy-family-wit: An intimate devotional panel showing the Madonna and Child with St Elizabeth, the infant Baptist and a hovering dove of the Holy Spirit. The warm flesh tones and tender exchange of glances reflect Rubens's recent return from Italy.
collection-of-beauty-the-hookah-lighter: An Orientalist genre scene of a turbaned attendant kindling the coals of a water pipe in a tiled interior. Gérôme's polished academic finish renders brass, silk and ceramic with photographic precision.
collection-of-beauty-the-house-on-the-ri: Painted during Monet's Dutch sojourn after the Franco-Prussian War, the canvas shows a row of green-shuttered houses reflected in the Zaan. Long horizontal brushstrokes mirror the calm of the Holland waterways that fascinated him.
collection-of-beauty-the-ill-matched-pai: A satirical Antwerp moral subject in which a young woman fingers the purse of an enamoured old man. Matsys updates a theme inherited from his father Quinten with sharper caricature and a cool grey-green palette.
collection-of-beauty-the-incredulity-of-: Christ guides the apostle's finger into the wound in his side as two other disciples crane forward to watch. Caravaggio collapses the moment into a tight, half-length composition lit from the upper left, the brows knit in shared concentration.
collection-of-beauty-the-junction-of-the: An estuary scene with merchantmen and a small boat tossed in choppy water where the two rivers meet. Turner builds the squally light from layered glazes, showing his early debt to Dutch marine painters.
collection-of-beauty-the-lion-and-the-mo: An Aesop subject likely painted in collaboration with Frans Snyders, who handled the animals while Rubens supplied the human figure. The freed lion crouches beside the sleeping man as the tiny mouse gnaws through the rope.
collection-of-beauty-the-luncheon-by-cla: An ambitious early figure piece showing Monet's companion Camille and their son Jean at a sunlit table, with a visitor lingering at the garden's edge. Submitted to and rejected by the 1870 Salon, it announces the everyday subjects that would define Impressionism.
collection-of-beauty-the-lute-player: A single seated figure tunes a long-necked lute in a dim Cairo interior, the strings catching a shaft of light. Gérôme uses the musician as a pretext for a study in muted tonality and meticulous costume detail.
collection-of-beauty-the-mantree-bosch: A drawing of the hollow tree-bodied figure that recurs in the right wing of the Garden of Earthly Delights, his cracked eggshell torso supported on legs that end in tiny boats. Long attributed to Bosch's own hand, the sheet is among the few surviving drawings linked to him.
collection-of-beauty-the-milk-maid-by-wi: A young woman in a bonnet and apron carries a brimming pail through a sunlit pasture. Homer's loose, summery handling belongs to the Houghton Farm watercolours and oils that distilled American rural life in the post-Civil War decade.
collection-of-beauty-the-moscow-cathedra: A view across the thawing Moskva toward the bulbous domes of the Kremlin cathedrals. Vereshchagin, better known as a war painter, here records the Russian capital with the same documentary precision he brought to his battlefield reportage.
collection-of-beauty-the-muezzin-1865-je: A solitary cleric calls the faithful to prayer from a high minaret balcony, the city of Cairo spreading out far below. The vertiginous viewpoint and crisp morning light are typical of Gérôme's Egyptian travel subjects.
collection-of-beauty-the-old-adam-and-ev: A pen-and-ink illustration prepared for an edition of Carlyle's Sartor Resartus, picturing the first couple as worn, clothed elders rather than as youthful nudes. Sullivan's nervous cross-hatched line carries the late-Victorian taste for Symbolist book ornament.
collection-of-beauty-the-petit-bras-of-t: A quiet backwater of the Seine fringed by bare poplars and reflected on a glassy surface. Painted in the first weeks of Monet's settlement at Argenteuil, the canvas balances delicate atmospheric grey-blues against incipient Impressionist touch.
collection-of-beauty-the-pond-at-benten-: A shin-hanga woodblock from Hasui's Tokyo Twenty Views, showing the red-railed bridge to the Benten shrine reflected in still water at twilight. Layered bokashi gradations and crisp keyblock lines exemplify the Watanabe workshop's revival of ukiyo-e technique.
collection-of-beauty-the-ragpicker-1869-: A life-size Spanish-inspired portrait of a Paris chiffonnier propped on his stick, set against a neutral grey ground. Manet borrows the format of Velázquez's philosopher portraits to dignify a marginal modern Parisian.
collection-of-beauty-the-rising-squall-h: An early oil view from the cliffs above the Avon Gorge as a sudden storm sweeps in over the Hot Wells spa. The seventeen-year-old Turner already orchestrates wind, rain and shifting light into a unified atmospheric event.
collection-of-beauty-the-sea-at-le-havre: A grey Channel seascape painted from the harbour Monet had known since childhood, with sailing vessels riding a low swell beneath broken cloud. The high horizon and economical palette anticipate the marines of the following decade.
collection-of-beauty-the-seine-at-bougiv: Painted from the riverbank at the village that drew Monet, Renoir and Pissarro alike, the scene catches a row of moored boats and the mirror of the Seine. Quick, comma-shaped strokes record passing reflections rather than fixed contours.
collection-of-beauty-the-sleepers-by-gus: Two nude women lie entwined in dishevelled bedding, jewellery and a broken pearl strand scattered on the sheet. Commissioned by the Turkish diplomat Khalil-Bey, the canvas treats lesbian love with a frank realism that scandalised the Second Empire.
collection-of-beauty-the-taking-of-chris: Judas presses forward to deliver his kiss as an armoured soldier seizes Christ's neck and a fleeing apostle howls behind. A self-portrait of the painter raising a lantern at the right edge introduces the artist into the violent nocturne.
collection-of-beauty-the-tea-plantation-: A woodblock print from the Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji series, framing the sacred peak through workers tending neat rows of tea bushes in Suruga Province. Hokusai integrates rural labor with landscape, the foreground figures pressing leaves into bundles while Fuji rises in cool, flat planes behind.
collection-of-beauty-the-triumph-of-chri: A vast allegorical canvas in which a cross-bearing Christ, ringed by sword-wielding angels, sweeps above the toppling deities of antiquity. Dore stages the contest as a vertiginous airborne procession, exploiting the canvas's monumental scale to push the pagan host into chaotic free-fall below.
collection-of-beauty-the-triumph-of-bacc: An exuberant Rococo procession of the wine god, attended by satyrs, maenads and tumbling putti amid garlands and overturned vessels. Natoire animates the mythological subject with feathery brushwork and pearled flesh tones characteristic of the French eighteenth century court taste.
collection-of-beauty-the-triumph-of-fred: An allegorical celebration of the Dutch stadtholder Frederick Henry, painted for the Oranjezaal at Huis ten Bosch. Jordaens orchestrates a swirling Baroque cavalcade of personifications, captives, and trophies in the Rubensian Antwerp manner, glorifying the prince's military and political triumphs.
collection-of-beauty-the-vision-of-the-v: An engraving from Dore's illustrated Bible, depicting Ezekiel's vision in which scattered bones reanimate as a vast army. The artist exploits dense crosshatching and dramatic chiaroscuro to give the desolate plain a haunted, otherworldly atmosphere.
collection-of-beauty-the-wailing-wall-by: An Orientalist tableau of worshippers gathered in prayer at the Western Wall in Jerusalem. Bauernfeind, trained as an architect, renders the weathered ashlar masonry and embroidered prayer shawls with exacting documentary precision, capturing the devotional hush of the site.
collection-of-beauty-the-water-lily-pond: A view across the Japanese footbridge spanning the lily pond at Monet's Giverny garden, the green arch dissolving into shimmering reflections. The painting belongs to the celebrated bridge series in which Monet examined a single subject under shifting conditions of light.
collection-of-beauty-the-white-horse-by-: The first of Constable's six-foot Stour valley canvases, showing a workhorse ferried across the river on a flat-bottomed barge. Constable elevates everyday East Anglian labor to grand-manner scale, with broken impasto highlights and a damp, atmospheric sky.
collection-of-beauty-the-zuiderkerk-amst: A view up the Groenburgwal canal toward the spire of the Zuiderkerk, painted during Monet's brief Dutch sojourn. The canvas already carries the rapid, broken brushwork of nascent Impressionism, dissolving brick facades and water into vibrating coloured strokes.
collection-of-beauty-the-back-of-the-fuj: A woodblock view from the series Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji, taken from the rear face of the mountain along the Minobu River. Hokusai contrasts the sinuous river bend and rocky banks with the broad, simplified silhouette of the peak, animated by tiny travelling figures.
collection-of-beauty-the-coast-of-seven-: A print from Hokusai's Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji, in which a clearing of pruned, circular pines frames the distant volcano. The flattened decorative pattern of the foliage demonstrates the artist's command of Edo-period graphic abstraction.
collection-of-beauty-the-end-of-the-pose: A studio interior in which a model steps down from the dais at the close of a session, while the artist's marble in progress stands beside her. Gerome, both painter and sculptor, treats the academic ritual with cool, polished precision and an eye for telling props.
collection-of-beauty-the-grand-canal-of-: Painted on a Venetian visit alongside Monet, the canvas captures the Grand Canal in shimmering broken touches of blue, white and ochre. Striped mooring posts cut vertical accents through the rippling water, marking Manet's most overtly Impressionist experiment.
collection-of-beauty-the-lake-of-hakone-: From Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji, this print sets the still mirror of Lake Hakone in the foreground, with Fuji rising over wooded ridges beyond. Hokusai contrasts smooth bands of water with crisply outlined cedars and the modulated bokashi gradations of the sky.
collection-of-beauty-theodore-gericault-: A Romantic landscape in the grand tradition, dwarfing fishermen beneath looming cliffs and gathering storm clouds. Gericault works the scene in dark earth tones, drawing on Poussin and Salvator Rosa to fuse classical composition with fervent natural drama.

One of Gericault's series of portraits depicting inmates of a Paris asylum, each suffering from a specific monomania. The unflinching, dignified treatment of the sitter and the somber palette mark a pioneering encounter between Romantic painting and clinical psychiatry.

An intimate study of two lovers locked in an embrace, modelled with the vigorous, sculptural brushwork that characterises Gericault's late work. The intense physicality and shadowed flesh tones reflect his sustained interest in bodily expression.
collection-of-beauty-thomas-howard-ruben: A state portrait of Thomas Howard, 14th Earl of Arundel, the great English collector of antiquities, painted during Rubens's diplomatic stay in London. The sitter is shown armoured and commanding, in the rich blacks and gleaming highlights of mature Flemish Baroque portraiture.
collection-of-beauty-thomas-eakins-ameri: A monumental scene of surgeon Samuel Gross presiding over an operation before his medical students at Jefferson College. Eakins records the bloodied hand, the chloroformed patient and the rapt amphitheatre with uncompromising realism, anchoring American painting in the lecture-hall light of modern science.
collection-of-beauty-thomas-gainsborough: A pastoral scene of cattle pausing at a wooded stream, painted with the feathery, scumbled foliage typical of Gainsborough's mature landscapes. The work belongs to his Suffolk-derived rural idylls, indebted to Dutch seventeenth-century models yet handled with English freshness.

A late landscape in which a shepherd and flock cross a windswept shore beneath gathering clouds. Gainsborough loosens the topography into soft, tonal silhouettes, prefiguring the atmospheric coastal scenes that would influence British Romantic painters.

A wooded river landscape in the Dutch-influenced manner of Gainsborough's middle years, with travellers descending toward a rustic bridge. Feathered foliage and a silvery palette establish the gentle, picturesque mood that defined his Bath-period landscapes.

A double portrait of a young Suffolk landowner and his bride seated at the edge of their estate, the rolling cornfields and oak deliberately presented as part of their identity. The crisp early style fuses French rococo elegance with topographical precision unusual in English portraiture.

A full-length portrait of the singer and society beauty Elizabeth Linley, wife of the playwright Sheridan, posed against a windswept landscape. Gainsborough merges sitter and setting through fluttering drapery and feathery foliage in his late, freely brushed manner.

A rural landscape in which figures with laden horses return along a sunken track at the close of day. Gainsborough constructs the scene from imagined, idealised motifs, building a poetic vision of English country life in the Bath-period pastoral mode.
collection-of-beauty-three-friends-of-wi: An ink handscroll bringing together pine, bamboo and flowering plum, the classical scholarly emblems of resilience that endure through winter. Zhao Mengjian, a Song-dynasty literatus, brushes the trio in spare, calligraphic strokes that fuse poetry, painting and personal virtue.
collection-of-beauty-tintoretto-jacopo-c: A Venetian rendering of the Gospel episode in which Christ appears to the disciples on a storm-tossed sea. The dramatic torsion of the figures, raking light and turbulent green-grey waves are characteristic of the Tintoretto workshop, though the attribution has been debated in favour of Lambert Sustris.
collection-of-beauty-tintoretto-prayer-i: A nocturnal Agony in the Garden, with Christ kneeling in radiant spotlight while the disciples slumber and torch-bearing soldiers approach through the olive grove. The picture exemplifies Tintoretto's preference for elongated figures, plunging diagonals and theatrical chiaroscuro.
collection-of-beauty-tintoretto-st-mary-: A solitary image of the penitent Magdalen withdrawn in a rocky wilderness with her book and ointment jar. Tintoretto isolates the saint against a brooding twilight landscape, animating the meditation with flickering, sketch-like brushwork.
collection-of-beauty-tintoretto-the-bapt: A Mannerist treatment of the Baptism in which crowds press toward the Jordan beneath a luminous descent of the Holy Spirit. Tintoretto orchestrates space through deep diagonals and silvery, restless light, characteristic of his late Venetian altarpieces.
collection-of-beauty-tintoretto-and-lukr: A late Mannerist treatment of the Roman legend in which Tarquin's assault on Lucretia precipitates the founding of the Republic. Tintoretto stages the violence with twisting bodies, broken pearls and torn drapery, the diagonal composition lit by sharp Venetian chiaroscuro.
collection-of-beauty-tintoretto-depositi: A nocturnal Lamentation in which the slumped body of Christ is supported by mourning figures emerging from deep shadow. Tintoretto compresses the scene around a single shaft of light, using elongated forms and rapid brushwork characteristic of his late Venetian manner.
collection-of-beauty-titian-self-portrai: A late self-portrait of the Venetian master, shown in three-quarter profile with brush in hand and the gold chain of imperial favor across his black robe. The looser handling and reserved gaze typify Titian's introspective final decade.
collection-of-beauty-titian-the-flaying-: Apollo punishes the satyr Marsyas for his musical hubris, suspending him upside down to be skinned alive. Painted in the artist's final years, the canvas is built from open, almost dissolving brushwork that anticipates later European painting.
collection-of-beauty-tizian-portrait-of-: A court portrait of the Marquis (later Duke) of Mantua, shown in slashed silk and resting one hand on a small white dog as an emblem of fidelity. Titian sets the half-length figure against a neutral ground, balancing courtly polish with naturalistic detail.
collection-of-beauty-tizian-015-2: One of Titian's mythological poesie made for Philip II of Spain, depicting Diana's discovery of the pregnant nymph Callisto among her bathing companions. Diagonal drapery, a marbled fountain and richly varied flesh tones articulate the moment of accusation.
collection-of-beauty-tizian-085: A late poesia for Philip II showing the Phoenician princess carried across the sea on the back of Jupiter disguised as a bull. Loose, atmospheric handling and a glowing pink-gold sky transform the abduction into a turbulent seascape.
collection-of-beauty-tizian-091: Adam and Eve beneath the Tree of Knowledge, the serpent rendered as a child clutching the fatal fruit. Titian gives the Genesis subject a sculptural, classicizing weight, drawing on Northern prototypes filtered through Venetian colorism.
collection-of-beauty-tokaido28-mitsuke: Mitsuke, the twenty-eighth station of the Tokaido road, depicting travelers crossing the Tenryu River by ferry. Hiroshige's woodblock design uses graded blue bokashi and a low horizon to evoke morning mist over the water.
collection-of-beauty-torii-kotondo-kamis: A bijin-ga showing a young woman drawing a comb through her loosened black hair, her undergarment slipping from one shoulder. Kotondo revives the intimate vanity scenes of Edo-period printmaking within the early Showa shin-hanga movement.
collection-of-beauty-torii-kotondo-woman: A bijin-ga in which a woman in a patterned kimono adjusts her hair before a round mirror, the reflection partly visible at the print's edge. Kotondo balances flat decorative pattern against a quietly observed private moment.
collection-of-beauty-tokyo-junidai-daiko: Daikon-gashi, the radish wharf along the Nihonbashi River, from the series Twelve Scenes of Tokyo. Hasui combines crisp architectural drawing with soft tonal washes typical of the shin-hanga revival of landscape woodblock printing.
collection-of-beauty-tokyo-junidai-fukag: The Kaminohashi Bridge in Fukagawa, from the series Twelve Scenes of Tokyo. Hasui captures a quiet canal-side moment, using graded inks and reflective water in the shin-hanga tradition that updated ukiyo-e cityscape conventions.
collection-of-beauty-tokyo-junidai-kiba-: Evening at the Kiba lumber yards, where stacked timber and floating logs catch the last light along the canal. From Twelve Scenes of Tokyo, the print exemplifies Hasui's shin-hanga blend of working-city subject and lyrical atmosphere.
collection-of-beauty-tokyo-junidai-samid: Early summer rain falls on the precincts of Hie (Sanno) Shrine, figures sheltering under umbrellas along the wet stone steps. Hasui's woodblock contrasts vertical rain lines against muted greens and greys in the shin-hanga manner.
collection-of-beauty-tokyo-junidai-shina: A view of fishing boats anchored in the bay off Shinagawa, sails reflected on a tranquil sea. Part of Twelve Scenes of Tokyo, the design relies on flat planes of graded blue and economical silhouettes characteristic of shin-hanga.
collection-of-beauty-tokyo-junidai-toyam: A winter moon rises over the bare expanse of the Toyama plain, with tiny figures crossing a darkened field. From Twelve Scenes of Tokyo, the print reduces the landscape to broad tonal bands of deep blue and silvered sky.
collection-of-beauty-tokyo-niju-kei-kand: The grounds of the Kanda Myojin shrine, with worshippers passing beneath the vermilion gate. From Twenty Views of Tokyo, the design pairs precise carpentry detail with the soft snow effects that became a Hasui signature.
collection-of-beauty-tokyo-niju-kei-shin: The steel girders of the Shin Ohashi bridge over the Sumida River, rendered through slanting rain and a darkening sky. Hasui treats modern Tokyo infrastructure with the lyrical atmospherics of traditional landscape woodblock printing.
collection-of-beauty-transfiguration-rub: Christ rises in radiant white between Moses and Elijah while the apostles below shield their eyes, a subject drawn from Matthew 17. Rubens organizes the composition around a vertical axis of light, marrying Italian disegno with Flemish painterly warmth.
collection-of-beauty-triumphant-cupid-am: Cupid sits in triumph atop instruments of art, science and war, a Baroque allegory of love's universal sway. Willeboirts Bosschaert painted the figures and de Vos the still-life trophies, reflecting the seventeenth-century Antwerp practice of collaborative specialization.
collection-of-beauty-triumphant-entry-of: Part of the Constantine cycle designed for tapestries woven for Louis XIII, showing the emperor's victorious procession through Rome after the Battle of the Milvian Bridge. Rubens stages the parade as a ceremonial relief, packed with banners, classical architecture and rearing horses.
collection-of-beauty-tsukioka-yoshitoshi: A pale full moon rises over the windswept grasses of Musashino, where a fox glances back through the autumn pampas. The plate from One Hundred Aspects of the Moon distills a literary motif into spare silhouette and graded sky.
collection-of-beauty-tsunami-by-hokusai-: The opening sheet of Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji shows a towering offshore wave (okinami) breaking over fishing boats while Fuji stands small on the horizon. Hokusai's use of imported Prussian blue and his geometric treatment of foam made the print a touchstone of Edo design.
collection-of-beauty-turner-j-m-w-the-gr: A view down the Grand Canal toward the domes of Santa Maria della Salute, gondolas dispersed across luminous water. Turner dissolves the architecture of Venice into pale light and reflected color, characteristic of his late Romantic vedute.
collection-of-beauty-turner-joseph-mallo: Fishing boats work the swell off Hastings under a turbulent sky, the figures and rigging set against pale sea-light. Turner treats the everyday coastal labor with the atmospheric breadth of his mature marine paintings.
collection-of-beauty-turner-deluge: An early biblical disaster scene depicting the universal flood, with massed bodies struggling against rising water beneath churning storm clouds. Turner builds the composition from a baroque diagonal of light, a precedent for his later sublime catastrophes.
collection-of-beauty-turner-antwerp-van-: An imagined homage in which the Dutch master Jan van Goyen sketches from a small boat in Antwerp harbor, surrounded by shipping under broken cloud. Turner pays tribute to the seventeenth-century landscape tradition while asserting his own atmospheric handling of sky and water.
collection-of-beauty-turner-fishermen-up: Fishermen struggle to bring a sailing vessel ashore as breaking surf and squall lines press against a steep coast. An early Royal Academy exhibit, the canvas sets Turner's lifelong concern with mariners' jeopardy in a darker, more Dutch-inflected idiom.
collection-of-beauty-turner-newark-abbey: The ruined Augustinian priory of Newark stands above the still waters of the Wey, framed by trees and scattered cattle. Turner adopts the picturesque vocabulary of the topographical watercolorists, softened by an early painterly attention to reflected light.
collection-of-beauty-turner-peace-burial: Commemorates the sea burial of the painter David Wilkie off Gibraltar, the steamer's silhouette and inky black sails set against a flaring sun. Exhibited as a pendant to Turner's fiery 'War,' the picture pairs cool nocturnal calm with the warmth of mourning.
collection-of-beauty-turner-port-rusdael: An invented harbour named in homage to the Dutch marine master Jacob van Ruisdael, with squat fishing craft straining against a grey-green swell. Turner adopts the seventeenth-century tonal vocabulary while loosening the brushwork toward atmospheric haze.
collection-of-beauty-turner-rain-steam-a: A Great Western Railway locomotive races across Maidenhead Bridge through curtains of rain, the iron viaduct dissolving into vapour and golden light. The picture is a defining statement of the modern industrial sublime, painted with palette knife and finger as much as brush.
collection-of-beauty-turner-ships-bearin: A squally Channel scene of merchantmen turning into the wind, indebted to the Dutch tradition of Willem van de Velde. Commissioned by the third Earl of Egremont, the canvas still hangs at Petworth House where Turner often painted.
collection-of-beauty-turner-the-banks-of: A French river view rendered in luminous gouache and watercolour on blue paper, part of Turner's projected 'Wanderings by the Loire' series. The chalky highlights catch a milky riverine light typical of his continental tour studies.
collection-of-beauty-turner-the-bright-s: The Rhine fortress of Ehrenbreitstein rises in golden haze above the confluence with the Moselle, paired with the riverside tomb of the French general Marceau. Turner illustrates a passage from Byron's Childe Harold, fusing topography with literary elegy.
collection-of-beauty-turner-the-eruption: A Caribbean volcano detonates against the night, fountains of incandescent ash silhouetting palms and figures along the shore. Worked from eyewitness reports of the 1812 eruption, the picture exemplifies the early nineteenth-century taste for geological catastrophe.
collection-of-beauty-turner-the-evening-: A solitary boy with a dog wades along an empty Margate beach as Venus pricks the violet dusk. Pared back to bands of damp sand, sea and sky, the canvas distils Turner's late preoccupation with thresholds of light.
collection-of-beauty-turner-the-trout-st: An angler casts beside a wooded English river in a pastoral key drawn from Claude Lorrain. Painted in Turner's early Thames-and-Wales manner, the composition balances feathery foliage against a glassy reach of water.
collection-of-beauty-turner-the-wreck-bu: A scarlet buoy bobs above a foundered hull as a low rainbow arcs over a ruffled sea. One of Turner's last completed oils, the picture reworks an earlier seascape into a meditation on hazard and salvage.
collection-of-beauty-turner-thomson-s-ae: A pastoral elegy for the poet James Thomson, with classical figures grouped on a hillside above the Thames at Richmond. Turner pairs the picture with verses of his own, blending Claudean idyll and literary tribute.
collection-of-beauty-turner-venice-the-d: Looking across the Bacino, the Dogana's customs point and Palladio's San Giorgio Maggiore float in a wash of lagoon light. Turner reduces Venice's masonry to shimmering tints, the gondolas mere flecks against pearl water and sky.
collection-of-beauty-turner-walton-bridg: Cattle wade in the Thames below the timber arches of Walton Bridges, drawn from Turner's sketching campaigns along the river. The picture marks his transition from topographical watercolour into open-air oil painting.
collection-of-beauty-turner-cologne-the-: Cologne's quayside gilds in evening light as a packet boat draws in beneath the unfinished cathedral. The vast canvas, now in the Frick Collection, pushes Rhineland topography toward Venetian-style golden atmosphere.
collection-of-beauty-two-sisters-on-the-: Two girls in flowered hats sit on a Chatou terrace overlooking the Seine, a basket of wool spilling between them. The picture is among Renoir's most ambitious figure compositions, set against a softly broken backdrop of foliage and river.
collection-of-beauty-umegawa-in-sagami-p: From the Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji, the volcano floats beyond a quiet manor pond ringed by cranes and pines. Hokusai marshals his celebrated bero-ai Prussian-blue gradients to balance domestic foreground against distant peak.
collection-of-beauty-unmatinapresledelug: A vortex of golden light spirals around the figure of Moses inscribing Genesis, with a serpent and submerged figures at its rim. Turner conceived the picture as a pendant illustrating Goethe's theory of positive, light-giving colour.
collection-of-beauty-urteil-des-paris: The shepherd Paris hands the golden apple to Venus while river gods recline at the foreground edge. Known chiefly through Marcantonio Raimondi's engraving after Raphael's lost design, the composition shaped European depictions of the nude for centuries.
collection-of-beauty-utagawa-hiroshige-1: A vertical triptych charts the famed Naruto whirlpools off Awa province, the churning indigo eddies set beneath a banded sky. Hiroshige stretches the ooban format to give the tide its full vertiginous scale.
collection-of-beauty-utagawa-hiroshige-i: Tall purple irises crowd the foreground of the celebrated Horikiri garden east of Edo, dwarfing the strolling visitors beyond. The radical close-up viewpoint, from One Hundred Famous Views of Edo, would be admired and copied by Van Gogh.
collection-of-beauty-utagawa-kunisada-18: From the series Modern Parodies of the Four Classes, this sheet personifies the artisan estate as a fashionable beauty. Kunisada combines bijin-ga portraiture with the Confucian shi-no-ko-sho social hierarchy.
collection-of-beauty-utagawa-kunisada-i-: Dawn breaks over the linked Wedded Rocks at Futami-ga-ura on the Ise coast, their sacred shimenawa rope strung between them. Kunisada renders the famous pilgrimage site in soft graded blues typical of early-1830s ukiyo-e landscape.
collection-of-beauty-valley-of-aosta-sno: Storm, avalanche and lightning converge over the Italian Val d'Aosta, tiny figures swept along the ravine floor. Drawing on Alpine sketches made with Hugh Munro of Novar, Turner orchestrates Romantic catastrophe through swirling diagonals of paint.
collection-of-beauty-vangogh-starry-nigh: A cypress flames upward through a sky of churning spirals above a sleeping Provençal village. Painted from memory at the Saint-Paul-de-Mausole asylum in Saint-Rémy, the canvas welds observation, symbol and rhythmic impasto.
collection-of-beauty-van-dyck-sir-anthon: Venetia, Lady Digby is shown the morning after her sudden death, propped against a pillow with a single rose shedding its petals. Commissioned by her grieving husband Sir Kenelm, the picture is a rare seventeenth-century memorial portrait painted from the corpse.
collection-of-beauty-van-dyck-pieter-bru: An ageing Pieter Brueghel the Younger turns toward the viewer in a quiet half-length portrait. The plate belongs to Van Dyck's Iconography, his series of etched and engraved likenesses of the leading artists, scholars and statesmen of his day.
collection-of-beauty-van-gogh-die-brucke: Washerwomen kneel along the canal bank as a horse-cart crosses the wooden drawbridge at Arles. Van Gogh returned to the Pont de Langlois several times in 1888, drawn to its clean geometry and Provençal light reminiscent of Japanese prints.
collection-of-beauty-van-gogh-abendstund: A peasant family gathers indoors at lamplight in a copy after Jean-François Millet. Working from a black-and-white print at Saint-Rémy, Van Gogh reimagines Millet's design in his own dense weave of complementary colour.
collection-of-beauty-van-gogh-am-stadtra: Allotments and a windmill straggle across the heights of Montmartre at the edge of Paris. Painted during Van Gogh's two-year Paris stay, the picture absorbs Impressionist colour and the broken touch of Pissarro and Signac.
collection-of-beauty-van-gogh-die-schafe: A young shepherdess leans on her staff among the flock, a free copy after Millet made during Van Gogh's confinement at Saint-Rémy. He treats Millet's composition almost as a musical score, transposing its tones into vibrant blues, ochres and greens.
collection-of-beauty-van-gogh-grunes-wei: A Provençal field rendered in surging diagonal brushstrokes, the unripe wheat treated as a tapestry of green and blue rhythms. Painted at Saint-Rémy, the canvas demonstrates the artist's habit of converting agricultural labor's quiet subjects into studies of pictorial energy.

Made in the final months at Auvers-sur-Oise, this study of a young wheat crop uses short, parallel strokes to register the wind moving over the field. The horizon is pushed high, leaving the green expanse to dominate as a meditation on growth and impermanence.
collection-of-beauty-van-gogh-le-moulin-: Painted during the Paris years, this view of the Montmartre windmill blends the muted palette of his earlier Dutch work with the lighter tonalities he was absorbing from the Impressionists. The motif, a popular dance hall draw for Parisian painters, is treated soberly rather than festively.
collection-of-beauty-van-gogh-les-alysca: The Roman necropolis at Arles, lined with poplars turning autumn yellow, is rendered with thick impasto and a steeply receding perspective. The work belongs to a small series painted alongside Gauguin during their fraught Arles collaboration.
collection-of-beauty-van-gogh-seineufer-: An industrial bend of the Seine at the edge of Paris, treated with the broken, stippled brushwork the artist absorbed from Signac and Seurat during his Paris stay. Smokestacks and bridge ironwork are softened into a screen of pale pinks and blues.
collection-of-beauty-van-gogh-wei-es-bau: A modest farmhouse glimpsed through writhing olive trunks, painted in the months at the Saint-Paul-de-Mausole asylum. The silver-green foliage is laid down in curling, comma-like strokes that turn the orchard into a continuous nervous surface.
collection-of-beauty-van-gogh-zwei-bauer: Two stooped laborers turning the earth, a subject the artist returned to throughout his career as homage to Millet. The Saint-Rémy treatment swaps the dark Dutch tonalities of his earlier peasant scenes for a brighter, more rhythmic handling of soil and sky.
collection-of-beauty-van-gogh-zypressen-: Two small figures stand beneath the flame-like silhouette of cypresses, trees the artist described as obsessing him for their dark sculptural force. The composition belongs to the Saint-Rémy cypress group, in which the species becomes a vehicle for swirling, vertical brushwork.
collection-of-beauty-van-gogh-f1040-jh10: An early drawing from the Borinage period, when the artist was working as a lay preacher among Belgian coal miners and teaching himself to draw from manuals and Millet prints. The sheet's dense, laborious hatching reflects his self-taught struggle with the figure.
collection-of-beauty-vasily-vasilevich-v: A solitary informant figure rendered with the documentary literalism for which the Russian battle painter was known. Vereshchagin's military scenes typically reject heroic convention in favor of the bleak human detail of campaign life.
collection-of-beauty-veld-met-klaprozen-: A summer field around Arles scattered with poppies, painted in short crosshatched strokes that articulate grasses and blossoms as discrete dabs of saturated red and green. The tightly cropped foreground reflects the artist's interest in Japanese print composition.
collection-of-beauty-venus-and-mars: Mars sleeps disarmed while Venus watches, mischievous satyrs playing with his lance and helmet. The horizontal panel was likely produced as a marriage spalliera or backboard, its Neoplatonic theme contrasting feminine vigilance with the exhaustion of war.
collection-of-beauty-vereshchagin-napole: Part of an extensive cycle the painter devoted to the 1812 French invasion of Russia, the canvas shows the emperor on the battlefield reduced to an ordinary, weary commander. The series was researched on site and built around demystifying the Napoleonic legend.
collection-of-beauty-vermeer-view-of-del: A panorama of the artist's home city seen across the Schie, rendered with the peculiar optical fidelity for which he is celebrated. Pinpoint dabs of impasto on the boats and roofs, possibly studied via a camera obscura, mimic the glint of refracted morning light.
collection-of-beauty-veronese-persee-ren: Andromeda, chained to the rock, twists toward the descending hero as Perseus drives his lance into the sea monster below. The picture's silvery palette and elongated figures, long debated among Veronese and his workshop circle, exemplify late-sixteenth-century Venetian narrative painting.
collection-of-beauty-veronese-allegory-o: Divine Wisdom, crowned and turned upward, stands beside a brawny Hercules whose attention is fixed on the worldly riches at his feet. The pairing, painted for the Habsburg court, sets celestial contemplation against earthly power in characteristic Venetian color.
collection-of-beauty-vertumnus-arstidern: The Roman god of seasons assembled entirely from fruit, vegetables, grains and flowers, each species chosen to flatter Emperor Rudolf II as the bringer of perpetual abundance. The composite portrait is the most refined example of the painter's signature conceit, made for the Prague court.
collection-of-beauty-vesalius-fabrica-p1: A woodcut from Andreas Vesalius's De humani corporis fabrica of 1543, the foundational atlas of Renaissance anatomy. Cut in Venice after drawings associated with Titian's workshop, the plates pose flayed figures in landscape settings to wed scientific demonstration to humanist art.

A muscle-man plate from Vesalius's De humani corporis fabrica, depicting progressive layers of dissection with the cadaver theatrically standing in a Paduan-style landscape. The series broke with medieval anatomical convention by presenting direct observation as the basis of medical knowledge.
collection-of-beauty-vigee-lebrun-julie-: The painter's daughter Julie posed as the Roman flower goddess, garlanded and seen against a soft pastoral ground. The mythological costume portrait belongs to the artist's exile years following the Revolution, when she worked across the European courts.
collection-of-beauty-vigeelebrun-viewoft: A Savoyard panorama of Lake Challes with Mont Blanc rising in the distance, unusual within the painter's output for being a pure landscape rather than a portrait. The view records her travels through the Alps during her long emigration from revolutionary France.
collection-of-beauty-vigee-lebrun-elisab: The Controller-General of Finances under Louis XVI shown at his desk, gesturing to a state document. The portrait, lavishly painted in silks and gilt, became notorious as evidence at court of the painter's alleged closeness to her Versailles sitters.
collection-of-beauty-vigee-lebrun-marie-: The queen in a simple white muslin gaulle holding a single rose, a deliberately informal court image the painter produced for the Salon. Public outrage at the chemise dress forced the picture's withdrawal and replacement with a more conventionally regal version.
collection-of-beauty-vincent-van-gogh-wh: A late Auvers wheat field interrupted by clusters of blue cornflowers, the surface built from short, directional strokes pulling the eye across the crop. It belongs to the burst of tall double-square canvases the artist produced in the weeks before his death.
collection-of-beauty-vincent-van-gogh-00: A self-portrait of the painter trudging to a motif under the Provençal sun, easel and canvases on his back. The picture, formerly in the Kaiser-Friedrich-Museum in Magdeburg, was lost during the Second World War and survives only in reproduction.
collection-of-beauty-vincent-willem-van-: A wheat field at Saint-Rémy seen through a downpour, the rain itself drawn as long diagonal scratches that cut across the painted surface. The device adapts a graphic shorthand from Japanese woodblock prints, which the artist collected and copied.

The iron lifting bridge at Trinquetaille on the Rhône at Arles, painted with a tilted, plunging perspective down its broad stone steps. Cool blues and grays mark a deliberate departure from the high-keyed yellow palette of the artist's earlier Arles canvases.

Convicts circling a stone yard under guard, copied at Saint-Rémy after a print by Gustave Doré of Newgate Prison. The artist's choice of source from his asylum bed gives the trudging round of figures a transparent autobiographical charge.

A dozen sunflowers in a yellow earthenware vase set against a yellow ground, made in Arles to decorate the bedroom prepared for Gauguin's visit. The picture pushes saturated yellow into both subject and field, treated as a near-monochrome experiment in pigment.
collection-of-beauty-vincent-van-gogh-ba: Bathers and rowboats along the willow-fringed Oise during the artist's final summer at Auvers. The reflective water and slack figures are built from broad, ribbon-like strokes, a calmer register among the more turbulent canvases of these last weeks.
collection-of-beauty-vincent-van-gogh-de: A panoramic view of the harvest on the plain of La Crau outside Arles, with stacked sheaves, a blue cart, and distant farm buildings under a Provençal sky. Van Gogh structures the composition with receding parallel bands of yellow stubble, layering complementary blues and oranges to register the heat of high summer.
collection-of-beauty-vincent-van-gogh-ho: A cluster of village rooftops in Auvers-sur-Oise, painted during the artist's final months in the small town north of Paris. Thatched and tiled roofs press together in interlocking diagonals, the brushwork rhythmic and ribbon-like across walls, foliage, and a streaked summer sky.
collection-of-beauty-vincent-van-gogh-ir: A bouquet of cut irises set in a simple vase, painted at the asylum of Saint-Paul-de-Mausole in the spring before the artist's death. Violet petals are built from short, kinetic strokes against a yellow ground, the complementary contrast deliberately heightening the flowers' chromatic intensity.

A still life of irises in a vase against a saturated yellow ground, executed at Saint-Rémy. Stems curve upward in calligraphic lines while the petals are massed in cool violets and blues, an overt complementary pairing that the artist used repeatedly during his late flower studies.
collection-of-beauty-vincent-van-gogh-la: A view of the wheat fields and olive groves surrounding the asylum at Saint-Rémy-de-Provence, with the Alpilles ridges rising in the distance. Curling, parallel brushstrokes animate the soil, foliage, and sky alike, dissolving the topography into a continuous vibrating surface.

A wide Auvers landscape with a horse-drawn carriage on a country road and a steam train cutting across the middle distance. The composition layers green fields, yellow wheat, and a band of poplars, registering the encroachment of modern travel on the rural plain.

A riverside scene along the Seine on the outskirts of Paris, painted during the artist's Asnières period with Bernard and Signac. The handling shows the impact of Neo-Impressionist contact, with broken touches of pure colour modeling the embankment, water, and screen of trees.
collection-of-beauty-vincent-van-gogh-le: A study of the Moulin de Blute-Fin, one of the surviving windmills on the butte of Montmartre that the artist returned to repeatedly after arriving in Paris. The palette remains dark and earthy in the manner of his Dutch period, even as the subject anticipates the lighter Parisian views to come.

A view of the Moulin de la Galette, the Montmartre windmill turned popular dance garden depicted by Renoir and Toulouse-Lautrec. Van Gogh treats the wooden mill and surrounding fence soberly, its silhouette isolated against an overcast Paris sky.

A solitary peasant cutting grain in a sun-blanched field, a subject the artist linked in letters to Millet and to thoughts of mortality. The wheat is built from sweeping yellow strokes that engulf the figure, the reaper's body almost dissolving into the matter he harvests.

A view of the Vessenots hamlet at Auvers-sur-Oise, with thatched cottages set among fields of long grass and slender trees. Painted in the artist's final weeks, the surface is densely worked in horizontal greens and yellows, the buildings reduced to compact accents within the landscape.
collection-of-beauty-vincent-van-gogh-mo: A view across the allotment gardens and windmills of upper Montmartre, then still a semi-rural quarter on the northern edge of Paris. Wooden fences, vegetable plots, and the silhouettes of the mills are arranged in receding bands beneath a wide, pale sky.
collection-of-beauty-vincent-van-gogh-pe: A stand of peach trees in full bloom on the plain of La Crau near Arles, painted in the burst of early Provençal spring. Pink blossom is set against fresh green grass and a clear blue sky, part of a series the artist conceived as a tribute to his recently deceased cousin Anton Mauve.
collection-of-beauty-vincent-van-gogh-ro: A country road at twilight with two figures, a horse-drawn cart, a flame-like cypress, and a crescent moon and evening star above. The composition compresses earth and sky into spiraling, rhythmic strokes; it was among the last canvases finished at Saint-Rémy before the move north.
collection-of-beauty-vincent-van-gogh-se: A frontal self-portrait painted at the Saint-Rémy asylum, the face modeled in cool greens against a turquoise ground worked in tightly curling strokes. The artist holds himself rigid and clear-eyed, the swirling background registering the disquiet of the institutional setting.

A Paris-period self-portrait in which the artist tests Neo-Impressionist methods on his own face, breaking flesh, beard, and felt hat into short, separate touches of pure colour. The effect is mottled and atmospheric, registering the lessons absorbed from Signac and Seurat.

An early self-portrait with the artist's clay pipe, painted shortly after his arrival in Paris while his palette was still close to that of his Dutch years. Sober earth tones model the bearded face against a neutral ground, before the chromatic experiments of the following year.
collection-of-beauty-vincent-van-gogh-st: Starry Night over the Rhône, painted from the riverbank at Arles, with the gas lamps of the quay reflected as ragged streaks of yellow on the dark water. A foreground couple anchors the scene as the Great Bear wheels overhead, the constellations rendered as concentric haloed dots.
collection-of-beauty-vincent-van-gogh-th: A view of the artist's own bedroom in the Yellow House at Arles, repeated in a slightly later replica painted at Saint-Rémy. Tilted floor, paired chairs, and framed prints are arranged in flat planes of colour intended, in his words, to suggest absolute rest.

The medieval parish church of Auvers seen from the apse, its stonework leaning under a deep cobalt sky split by branching lanes. The masonry and lead roofs are worked in thick, undulating strokes that twist the Gothic structure into a single restless form.
collection-of-beauty-vincent-van-gogh-tu: A small enclosed garden on the slopes of Montmartre, with strolling couples among trees and wooden fencing. Painted during the Paris years, the canvas registers the artist's growing engagement with broken touches of complementary colour learned from his Neo-Impressionist contemporaries.
collection-of-beauty-vincent-van-gogh-ve: A flowering field outside Arles, with stands of red poppies and white wildflowers spread across a foreground of summer grass. Distant farm buildings break the horizon, the whole laid down in rapid, directional strokes that record the southern light Van Gogh sought in Provence.
collection-of-beauty-vincent-van-gogh-wh: Wheat, olive trees, a flame-shaped cypress, and the limestone ridges of the Alpilles arranged in horizontal bands under a sky of swelling cumulus. One of three closely related canvases the artist made on this motif at Saint-Rémy in the summer of 1889.

A wheat field at Saint-Rémy with twisting cypresses on the right and the Alpilles range behind, the sky churning with rolled, scroll-like clouds. The composition belongs to a small group of variants the artist regarded as among his strongest summer landscapes.

A wide double-square wheat field under a lowering blue-grey sky, painted in the Auvers fields during the artist's final weeks. The horizon is set high, leaving a vast expanse of ripe grain whose horizontal strokes resist the weight of the gathering storm.

A walled wheat field at Saint-Rémy with a single reaper at work in the high sun, the grain rendered as a near-uniform sheet of yellow. The artist described the figure as an image of humanity being mown down like the wheat, an idea that recurs across the late Provençal landscapes.

A double-square panorama of a wheat field crossed by three diverging paths, with a flock of black crows lifting against a turbulent blue sky. Painted in the Auvers fields shortly before the artist's death, the work compresses storm, harvest, and flight into a single agitated surface.
collection-of-beauty-vincent-van-gogh-ze: A seascape made during the artist's brief stay at the Mediterranean fishing village of Saintes-Maries-de-la-Mer, with small fishing boats riding choppy water under a wind-driven sky. Short, stabbing strokes register the swell, while patches of pure colour mark sails and reflected light.
collection-of-beauty-vincent-van-gogh-co: A close, near-aerial view of grass, weeds, and butterflies in a corner of garden, painted as a fragment of nature seen at very short range. Stems and blades are flicked on in fine, directional strokes that nearly abstract the patch into a tapestry of green and yellow.
collection-of-beauty-vue-du-val-d-arco-d: A topographical watercolour of the Arco valley in the southern Tyrol, made during Dürer's return journey from his first Italian trip. The fortified hilltop town, terraced vineyards, and outcrops of rock are observed with surveyor's precision, an early instance of independent landscape study in Northern art.
collection-of-beauty-wlanl-adagio808-clo: A Norman coastal scene capturing the chalk cliffs of Pourville under shifting summer light. Monet sets a high horizon and broken brushwork against the sea, dissolving rock and water into a single atmospheric weave typical of his Normandy campaign.
collection-of-beauty-wang-meng-dwelling-: A towering hanging scroll in ink on paper, its densely textured peaks built from layered hemp-fibre strokes and moss dots. Wang Meng's restless, near-trembling line gives the Qingbian range a vibrating, almost airless density emblematic of late-Yuan literati landscape.
collection-of-beauty-wassilij-wassiljewi: Two dervishes pose in vivid ceremonial garments studded with shells, beads, and embroidery. The careful ethnographic detail reflects Vereshchagin's Central Asian travels and his reportorial approach to Russian Orientalist subjects.
collection-of-beauty-water-lilies-and-ja: The arched footbridge of Monet's Giverny garden hangs above a pond crowded with floating blossoms, framed by curtains of willow and iris. Painted in tightly woven greens and rose-tinted whites, the canvas turns a private water garden into an enclosed world of reflection.
collection-of-beauty-water-lilies-agapan: From Monet's late Giverny series, the painting abandons horizon and shoreline for a pure expanse of water surface. Lily pads and agapanthus stalks float among broken reflections of sky and trees, the brushwork loosened toward near-abstraction.
collection-of-beauty-water-lilies-by-mon: An overhead view of the Giverny pond, dotted with discs of pink and white lilies. Cool blues and greens shift toward lavender where the surrounding foliage is mirrored in still water, with no sky visible above the bank.
collection-of-beauty-waterloo-bridge-lon: Part of Monet's London Thames series, the bridge dissolves into industrial mist warmed by low sun. Smokestacks beyond the river emit plumes that fuse fog, water, and sky into a unified veil of orange and violet.
collection-of-beauty-watteau-antoine-que: A reclining nymph leans on her urn beside a flowing stream in a wooded glade. The drawing's soft contours and pastoral setting prefigure the Arcadian fete galante imagery for which Watteau would soon become known.
collection-of-beauty-wedding-of-nicholas: An imperial ceremonial scene staged inside a Russian Orthodox chapel, with Tsar Nicholas II and Alexandra Fyodorovna at the centre of a glittering crowd of courtiers, clergy and grand dukes. Tuxen, official painter to several European royal houses, marshals the assembly into a measured, document-like composition.
collection-of-beauty-weyden-deposition: The body of Christ is lowered from the cross within a shallow gilded niche, surrounded by ten mourners arranged in a tight, frieze-like rhythm. Van der Weyden's sharp drapery folds and tear-streaked faces compress the Passion into a sculpted relief of grief, a touchstone of Early Netherlandish painting.
collection-of-beauty-whaling-off-the-coa: Tiny whaling boats encircle a leviathan in choppy waters off Kyushu, their oarsmen dwarfed by the dark mass beneath. From Hokusai's Oceans of Wisdom series, the woodblock print pairs documentary subject with sweeping rhythmic line.
collection-of-beauty-wheat-field-with-re: A solitary reaper bends amid blazing yellow stalks beneath a swollen sun. Painted from Van Gogh's window at the Saint-Paul asylum in Saint-Remy, the scene was for him an emblem of human labour and mortality bound to nature's cycles.
collection-of-beauty-whistler-nocturne-i: Showers of golden sparks fall through a smoky night sky above the Cremorne Gardens fireworks. The thinly washed, near-monochrome surface so provoked John Ruskin that his attack triggered the famous 1878 libel trial.
collection-of-beauty-whistler-nocturne-g: Westminster Bridge and the Thames vanish into a haze of grey-blue twilight, pierced by scattered gold lamplight. The painting belongs to Whistler's Nocturne series, in which musical titles signalled the priority of tonal harmony over narrative.
collection-of-beauty-whistler-james-symp: Joanna Hiffernan stands full-length in a flowing white dress on a white drape, holding a single lily. Whistler's emphasis on subtle gradations of cream, ivory and pale flesh tones over storytelling helped lay the ground for Aestheticism's art-for-art's-sake credo.
collection-of-beauty-whistler-selbstport: A self-portrait built from narrow tonal intervals of grey, the painter emerging only as a darkened silhouette and pale face from a near-uniform field. The careful tuning of values exemplifies Whistler's musical analogies for picture-making.
collection-of-beauty-whistlers-mother-hi: Anna McNeill Whistler sits in profile in a London interior, bonneted and dressed in black against a pale grey wall hung with one of her son's etchings. The strict horizontal-vertical geometry and muted palette underscore Whistler's framing of a familial portrait as a tonal arrangement.
collection-of-beauty-wilhelm-von-kaulbac: A swirl of butterflies fills the sheet, drawn with finely observed wing markings and a decorative arabesque rhythm. Kaulbach, best known as a history painter, here turns to natural history as ornamental composition.
collection-of-beauty-wilhelm-von-kobell-: Drovers, riders and townspeople negotiate over cattle on a rise overlooking a Bavarian lake, with a distant city silhouetted under bright clear air. Kobell's crisp draftsmanship and high-keyed Alpine light typify his Biedermeier landscapes.
collection-of-beauty-wilhelmine-sulzer: A young woman gazes outward in a richly trimmed dress and lace collar. Painted for Ludwig I's Schonheitengalerie in Munich, the portrait shows Stieler's polished court manner: porcelain skin, unblemished surface, and discreet idealisation.
collection-of-beauty-william-holman-hunt: An ivy-choked manor stands beyond a dark woodland pool that catches a livid sunset. Painted early in Hunt's career, the eerie stillness and minutely studied foliage signal the emerging Pre-Raphaelite commitment to truth-to-nature charged with literary mood.

The artist meets the viewer's eye in a fur-collared coat, painted with the meticulous, hard-edged detail he championed. Hunt was a founding member of the Pre-Raphaelite Brotherhood, and the picture's sober concentration befits his role as its theoretical anchor.

Hunt's young wife Edith is portrayed in profile against a deep ground, adorned with elaborate jewellery and patterned fabrics gathered during the painter's eastern travels. The exacting detail of ornament shows his characteristic Pre-Raphaelite finish in an intimate domestic register.

The boy Jesus is discovered among Jerusalem's elders, who recoil or peer at him in a chamber thick with embroidered textiles, scrolls and oil lamps. Hunt prepared the picture during years in the Holy Land, pursuing archaeologically researched detail as a vehicle for biblical truth.
collection-of-beauty-william-m-chase-n-a: The painter William Merritt Chase is shown three-quarter length in studio dress, brush poised. Sargent's fluid, summarising touch reduces background and costume to broad sweeps, throwing the sitter's alert, assessing face into relief.
collection-of-beauty-william-merrit-chas: Grapes, peaches and other fruit are heaped on a draped tabletop in a darkened interior. The Munich-school chiaroscuro and confident, loaded brushwork mark the young Chase's training in 1870s Bavaria.
collection-of-beauty-william-merritt-cha: A wooded path glows with the first yellow leaves of fall, painted in long, vibrant strokes. Chase's plein-air technique here owes as much to French Impressionism as to the Long Island summer school he led at Shinnecock.

A young woman lies on cushions in a kimono, surrounded by fans and lacquered objects. The picture taps the Aesthetic-era taste for Japonisme, deploying its props in a frankly studio-arranged tableau of textures.

Chase's family takes breakfast in a leafy Brooklyn yard, the white tablecloth and hammock catching dappled sun. Loose brushwork and a bright outdoor palette align the scene with American Impressionism's domestic, garden-side mode.

Chase's family takes breakfast in a leafy Brooklyn yard, the white tablecloth and hammock catching dappled sun. Loose brushwork and a bright outdoor palette align the scene with American Impressionism's domestic, garden-side mode.

A hummingbird hovers above a cluster of cut flowers and fruit on a dark ground. The dense Munich-trained shadows and tightly observed wings reflect Chase's early academic still-life manner before his later turn to airy Impressionist plein-airs.

A late portrait by the American Impressionist of a young woman absorbed in performance, rendered with the fluid brushwork and rich tonal harmonies Chase admired in Velazquez. The dark, atmospheric ground throws the figure's pale skin and parted lips into intimate relief.

A pensive girl in mourning black sinks into a red upholstered chair, her solitary figure modeled against a sober interior. Chase's debt to Whistler and the Munich school is visible in the subdued palette and confident, economical handling of the dress.
collection-of-beauty-william-turner-brid: Turner's Venetian capriccio assembles the Doge's Palace, the Bridge of Sighs and the Punta della Dogana along a sun-bleached lagoon. Architecture dissolves into luminous haze, foregrounding the Romantic interest in atmospheric light over topographic precision.
collection-of-beauty-william-holman-hunt: A Pre-Raphaelite vision of Christ as a young carpenter stretching after labor, his outflung arms casting a cruciform shadow across the workshop wall. Hunt painted the scene in Jerusalem to ground every tool, shaving and textile in archaeological accuracy.
collection-of-beauty-wind-god-and-thunde: Sotatsu's pair of two-fold screens places Fujin clutching his wind sack and Raijin riding a ring of drums against expanses of unworked gold leaf. The bold negative space and tarashikomi ink pooling became a touchstone for the Rinpa school and a National Treasure of Japan.
collection-of-beauty-windmill-and-boats-: Painted during Monet's residence in the Dutch town of Zaandam, the canvas registers a working waterway under low northern light, with a windmill anchoring the moored barges. Quick, broken touches across the water anticipate the Impressionist breakthroughs of the next few years.
collection-of-beauty-winners-by-vasily-v: Drawn from Vereshchagin's Balkan series, the painting confronts the aftermath of battle as scavengers strip the bodies of the fallen. The Russian realist refused to glamorize war, building documentary detail into a deliberately disquieting field of corpses and crows.
collection-of-beauty-winslow-homer-ameri: An Adirondack hunter shoulders a deer hide while his hounds press against him on a windswept ridge. Homer's late realism strips the scene to silhouette and weather, registering the rougher economy of post-frontier wilderness.

A coastguardsman pulls a fainting woman across the surf on a breeches buoy, the rope line cutting taut against churning spray. Homer studied actual life-saving drills on the New Jersey coast, and the obscured rescuer's face concentrates attention on the woman's limp red scarf.
collection-of-beauty-winslow-homer-after: A black sailor lies cast up beside his shattered sloop on a Bahamian beach, the sea behind still heavy from the storm. Homer's late watercolor technique uses transparent washes and reserved white paper to convey both tropical glare and human vulnerability.
collection-of-beauty-winslow-homer-artis: Three painters perch on a New Hampshire ridge, easels braced against the wind as they survey the valley below. The wry small canvas comments on the Hudson River School fashion for plein-air landscape, with Homer himself among the figures.
collection-of-beauty-winslow-homer-breez: A catboat heels in stiff wind off the Massachusetts coast, three boys and a fisherman crowded along the windward rail. Homer reworked the composition over two years, settling on the diagonal mast that turns a leisure scene into an emblem of post-Civil-War optimism.
collection-of-beauty-winslow-homer-clear: A small watercolor study of a sailboat on open water, painted with Homer's characteristic economy of wash. Sky and sea are summarized in a few transparent layers, leaving the white paper to carry the brightness of the canvas sail.
collection-of-beauty-winslow-homer-crab-: Two figures lean from a beached dory to lift a crab pot, painted during Homer's productive Gloucester summer. The watercolor balances loose washes of harbor light with sharper notations of rope, basket and net.
collection-of-beauty-winslow-homer-dad-s: A boy perched on an overturned dory watches the horizon while his mother and infant sister wait beside him on the beach. The hopeful title masks Homer's familiar coastal anxiety, as the absent father's sail is nowhere yet visible.
collection-of-beauty-winslow-homer-eagle: Three young women emerge from the surf onto the Cape Ann shore, their wet bathing dresses clinging in a way that scandalized contemporary critics. Homer treats the scene as a study of weight, wind and waterlogged fabric rather than narrative incident.
collection-of-beauty-winslow-homer-girl-: A young woman in a straw hat stands among flowering mountain laurel, her face shaded against the New England summer light. The composition belongs to Homer's mid-career sequence of solitary rural figures absorbed in landscape.

A woman reclines in a slung hammock with a book, her white dress catching dappled light through the canopy. Homer's flattened design and decorative pattern of leaves reflect his exposure to contemporary Japanese prints.
collection-of-beauty-winslow-homer-long-: Two fashionable women in bustled dresses pause on a cliff at the Jersey Shore resort, parasols snapping in the Atlantic wind. Homer's bright, almost Impressionist palette captures the new postwar leisure culture with magazine-illustrator clarity.
collection-of-beauty-winslow-homer-mink-: A trout, frog and lily pads share a single watercolor surface in this Adirondack pond study. Homer dissolves traditional figure-ground hierarchy, letting reflections and refraction flatten the underwater and overwater worlds into one decorative plane.
collection-of-beauty-winslow-homer-moonl: A study of figures on a darkened shore under a low moon, painted in oil with the heavy tonal contrasts Homer favored after the Civil War. Silvered light on water dominates the otherwise nearly silhouetted forms.
collection-of-beauty-winslow-homer-on-th: Children and bathers cluster on a sunlit Atlantic strand, the composition organized in horizontal bands of sand, surf and sky. Homer's interest in the new American leisure shore renders the figures with quick, illustrational economy.

A young woman pauses at a wooden stile separating two pasture fields, her gaze turned away from the viewer. The painting belongs to Homer's Houghton Farm idylls, where rustic labor is filtered through the conventions of the genteel illustration.
collection-of-beauty-winslow-homer-shark: Two black fishermen in a small boat haul against a hooked shark amid Caribbean swells. The watercolor anticipates Homer's later Gulf Stream subjects, using a thrashing diagonal to crowd the picture plane with imminent danger.
collection-of-beauty-winslow-homer-sunli: Surf detonates against a Maine ledge under hard noon light, with no human figure to soften the shock. Homer's Prouts Neck marines reduced the genre to elemental confrontations of rock, water and atmosphere.
collection-of-beauty-winslow-homer-the-a: A solitary woodsman paddles his canoe across a still mountain lake, the watercolor reserving the white of the paper for highlights on water and shirt. The picture distills Homer's late respect for the working backwoodsman into a quietly monumental profile.
collection-of-beauty-winslow-homer-the-b: Two young cattle drovers in straw hats lean on a rail fence, watching their herd from a sun-soaked pasture. Often catalogued as Cow Boys, the canvas typifies Homer's Gloucester-era interest in agrarian boyhood as an emblem of postwar American innocence.
collection-of-beauty-winslow-homer-the-f: A halibut fisherman in a dory pauses at the crest of a swell to gauge the schooner against a wall of advancing fog. The diagonal of the boat and the steep horizon convert a routine moment into a measured study of solitude at sea.

A young woman in a wide-brimmed hat searches a meadow for the proverbial lucky clover, her downturned head shaded by the brim. Homer turns a slight pastoral conceit into a study of summer light filtered through straw.
collection-of-beauty-winslow-homer-the-g: A small group of figures crosses the slope of a sunlit New England hillside, the composition reduced to broad bands of grass, sky and shadow. The work belongs to Homer's late-1870s rural idylls, balancing illustration and quiet landscape observation.

A Black sailor lies on the deck of a dismasted sloop adrift on rough seas, encircled by sharks while a waterspout darkens the horizon. Homer's late marine paintings turn man-against-nature subjects into terse moral allegories, the figure's resigned posture sharpened by the painter's economical handling of pigment and salt-bleached light.
collection-of-beauty-winslow-homer-the-h: Two fishermen work a dory laden with the silver gleam of a hauled net, the larger schooners reduced to ghosted shapes in the Atlantic mist. Painted after Homer settled at Prouts Neck, the picture distills the labor of New England's offshore fleet into a low horizon, broad tonal blocks, and a pearly fog that flattens space.
collection-of-beauty-winslow-homer-the-r: A solitary harvester swings his scythe through tall grain under a high summer sun, the field's ochre stalks brushed with rapid, almost graphic strokes. The watercolor belongs to Homer's Houghton Farm period, when he reworked rural American labor in the lean, light-keyed manner he had absorbed during his recent stay in Cullercoats.
collection-of-beauty-winslow-homer-two-b: Two barefoot boys perch on a grassy headland, gazing seaward at distant schooners drifting across a flat New England bay. The watercolor pares the scene to bands of sky, water, and sunlit turf, characteristic of Homer's Gloucester summers and his interest in childhood as a vantage on the wider working coast.
collection-of-beauty-winslow-homer-warm-: A young woman in a sunbonnet sits on the warm grass of a hillside pasture, a study of stillness and rural reverie. Painted during Homer's Houghton Farm summers, the watercolor shows the artist's command of transparent washes and reserved white paper, qualities that helped redefine the medium's ambitions in America.
collection-of-beauty-winslow-homer-watch: Figures stand at the edge of a rocky shore as heavy Atlantic surf detonates in spray against the ledges. The picture belongs to Homer's Prouts Neck years, when he reduced his palette to slate, foam, and granite and treated the meeting of land and sea as the subject in itself.
collection-of-beauty-woman-writing-a-let: A seated lady drafts a letter at a draped table while her standing maid waits, glancing toward a sunlit window. Vermeer constructs the interior with his characteristic geometry of floor tiles, parallel walls, and a hanging picture, weaving the small domestic event into a quietly orchestrated study of light and waiting.
collection-of-beauty-xsxlt-fankuan: A monumental hanging scroll in which towering peaks rise abruptly above a thread of waterfall, while travelers and pack mules trace a path across the valley floor. A landmark of Northern Song monumental landscape, the work uses dense raindrop texture strokes and a vertiginous vertical scale to express the Confucian ideal of nature dwarfing humanity.
collection-of-beauty-yamamoto-1904: A fisherman bends to his work in a small skiff, rendered in flat planes of color and direct gouged lines. The print is a foundational image of Japan's sosaku-hanga (creative print) movement, in which the artist designed, carved, and printed the block himself rather than relying on the traditional ukiyo-e workshop division of labor.
collection-of-beauty-yoshiclimber: Lit by a thin crescent moon, the young Toyotomi Hideyoshi leads a roped band of warriors up the sheer face of Inaba Mountain to surprise its castle. From the One Hundred Aspects of the Moon series, the design exploits steep diagonals and silvered rock to dramatize a famous episode of Sengoku-era stealth.
collection-of-beauty-yoshioldwoman: The aged poet Ono no Komachi crouches by a wooden gravemarker beneath an autumn moon, contemplating the suitors she once spurned. Yoshitoshi pares the print to muted earth tones and a single stark vertical, turning the courtly love legend into a meditation on vanity and impermanence.
collection-of-beauty-yoshida-hiroshi-rap: White water churns over boulders in a narrow gorge, with overhanging pines describing the steep banks. Yoshida, trained as a Western-style oil painter before turning to woodblock, achieves the river's translucent depth through dozens of hand-pulled impressions in a single block-printed image.
collection-of-beauty-yoshida-hiroshi-gra: The South Rim falls away into vast layered cliffs flushed with desert light, a Japanese sensibility brought to the American Southwest. The print belongs to Yoshida's United States series, made after his sketching tour abroad, and uses successive overprintings to suggest the canyon's atmospheric haze.
collection-of-beauty-yoshida-kameido: The arched drum bridge of Kameido Tenjin shrine reflects in the still pond below, surrounded by spring wisteria. Yoshida revisits a motif made famous by Hiroshige, but rebuilds it with the soft tonal gradations and calibrated overprinting of the shin-hanga revival.
collection-of-beauty-yoshida-komagatake: Ridges of the Southern Japanese Alps recede in cool blues from the summit of Komagatake, a vantage informed by the artist's lifelong mountaineering. Yoshida's woodblock layering produces the thinning air and dry granite light that he sought in his alpine series.
collection-of-beauty-yoshida-sailboat-mo: Sailboats lie at anchor in a sheltered bay, their masts and sails answered by wide reflections in glassy water. The composition belongs to Yoshida's celebrated set of six prints in which the same sailboat block was printed in different colorways to render morning, afternoon, evening, mist, and night.
collection-of-beauty-yoshitoshi-100-aspe: Wu Gang, the legendary Chinese woodcutter condemned to chop forever at the cassia tree on the moon, swings his axe against silvered foliage. Yoshitoshi adapts the Daoist tale into the One Hundred Aspects of the Moon series, paring the design to the figure's strained pose against an empty sky.

A Shinto priest performs a dawn rite as the late moon hangs pale over a torii gate. The print uses Yoshitoshi's restrained late palette and softly graded sky to evoke the dim hour between ritual and daybreak, characteristic of the One Hundred Aspects series.

Minamoto no Tsunemoto draws his bow on a sika deer that has wandered into the imperial palace grounds, an autumn incident recorded in the year 932. From the One Hundred Aspects of the Moon, the design contrasts the courtier's taut profile with a low harvest moon hanging over palace eaves.
collection-of-beauty-yoshitoshi-the-ghos: A spectral female figure with loosened black hair and pallid skin hovers against an empty ground, a staple of the Japanese yurei tradition. Yoshitoshi was a defining late ukiyo-e designer of ghost imagery, drawing on kabuki stagecraft to give such apparitions their quiet, gravity-loosened menace.
collection-of-beauty-yoshitoshi-tsuki: The warrior-monk Benkei stands at the prow of a storm-tossed vessel in Daimotsu Bay, calming the vengeful spirits of fallen Taira warriors with chanted prayers. From One Hundred Aspects of the Moon, Yoshitoshi pitches the scene against rolling waves and a clouded moon to dramatize the Heike legend.
collection-of-beauty-zaandam2: Dutch windmills line a low horizon while flat water carries their reflections under a wide sky. The etching belongs to Whistler's later European tours, where his economical needlework and selective wiping of the plate convert a mundane port into a tonal study close in spirit to his Nocturnes.
collection-of-beauty-zeitungsleser-im-ha: A Biedermeier gentleman absorbs himself in his newspaper amid the trellised greenery of a small private garden. Spitzweg specialized in such gently humorous bourgeois vignettes, painted in rich enamel-like color, that catalogued the introspective civic culture of mid-nineteenth-century Munich.
collection-of-beauty-zentralbibliothek-z: A young Zurich sitter is shown half-length in soft chalk and wash, her gaze direct, her hair lightly powdered in late Rococo fashion. Fuseli's Swiss portrait drawings of friends and relatives are markedly more sober than his theatrical paintings, prizing physiognomic precision over the visionary excess of his London canvases.
collection-of-beauty-zwei-junge-seehunde: Two young harbor seals lie on a stretch of Flemish shoreline, painted with the close observation of fur, hide, and salt-damp sand for which the de Vos workshop was prized. The Antwerp painter built his career on Baroque animal subjects, often supplying livestock and game for collaborators including Rubens.
